  direct and child error-result validation, plus client-side transactional
  injection before auto-discovered direct sends.
- USync query foundation with typed users/protocol selection, IQ construction,
  contact/device/status/disappearing-mode/LID/username result parsing, direct
  and child error-result validation, and a client execution helper over the
  existing binary-node query path.
- Higher-level USync convenience helpers for phone existence checks, LID mapping
  fetch-and-persist flows, and device-JID extraction with hosted/LID domain
  handling, current-device exclusion, zero-device filtering, and missing
  key-index filtering for non-zero devices.
- Additional USync facade coverage for status fetch, disappearing-mode duration
  fetch, and bot-profile lookup, with typed query builders, result mappers, and
  mock connection tests.
- Message-generation foundation with WhatsApp-compatible random and v2 message
  ID generation, validated message keys, text-message protobuf construction
  with quote/mention/forwarding context and typed text/audio styling fields,
  supplied-metadata link-preview fields including in-message JPEG thumbnails and
  high-quality thumbnail media metadata,
  contact and contact-array builders, location/live-location builders, reaction
  builders, poll/event creation builders with message-secret validation and
  event join-link support,
  direct client send facades for poll, event, reaction, edit, delete, pin, and
  disappearing-mode messages, and store-backed Signal-provider variants for
  those send paths that reuse the high-level relay path for device discovery,
  session assertion, reporting-token attachment where eligible, and provider
  encryption, Baileys-style generic `send_message`/Signal-provider send facades
  over any `MessageContent` with contact/location/media regression coverage,
  edit/delete/pin/disappearing-mode protocol builders, group invite builders,
  typed uploaded-media descriptor plus image/video/ptv/audio/document/sticker
  protobuf builders with strict media key and hash validation, modern
  future-proof wrapper unwrapping for stanza-type classification, product/catalog
  snapshot builders that reuse the uploaded image path, boxed content enum
  variants to keep the send API small in memory, top-level view-once and
  disappearing-setting future-proof message wrapping, album placeholders,
  request/share-phone-number builders, limit-sharing protocol builders,
  button/template/list reply builders, encoded protobuf output helpers, and
  feature-gated bounded JPEG thumbnail generation helpers that populate image,
  video/PTV, and document message thumbnail metadata from supplied image bytes.
  Video thumbnails can also be extracted through a configurable ffmpeg command
  boundary and then validated/resized through the bounded JPEG thumbnail path.
  PDF document thumbnails can be extracted through a configurable `pdftoppm`
  command boundary and then validated/resized through the same bounded JPEG
  thumbnail path.
  Feature-gated
  link-preview helpers now generate bounded inline/high-quality JPEG preview
  images and upload high-quality thumbnail media as `ThumbnailLink` descriptors.
  Video and document remote thumbnail descriptors can also be uploaded with the
  parent media key as `ThumbnailVideo`/`ThumbnailDocument` media and attached to
  message protos.
  The optional `noise,image` path can now extract video-frame and PDF-page
  thumbnails through the configured command boundaries and immediately upload
  them as parent-key remote thumbnail descriptors.
  Automatic link-preview metadata fetching now performs bounded HTTP HTML
  retrieval, extracts Open Graph/Twitter/basic title and description metadata,
  resolves preview image URLs, and returns existing `LinkPreviewContent` through
  core/client facades. The optional `link-preview,image` path now also fetches
  resolved preview image bytes with HTTP/content-type/size bounds, generates
  inline and high-quality JPEG previews, uploads high-quality thumbnail media as
  `ThumbnailLink`, and attaches both thumbnail forms to `LinkPreviewContent`
  through core/client facades.
- Direct-device message relay foundation with a provider-based encryption
  boundary, participant `to`/`enc` node construction, own-device
  device-sent-message protobuf wrapping, stanza type detection, client
  `send_text_to_devices`/`relay_message_to_devices` methods over the connection,
  auto-discovered direct-send `send_text`/`send_message`/`relay_message` helpers,
  discovered
  device recipient classification for PN/LID/hosted linked devices,
  missing-session assertion before relay, v2 participant hash generation for
  fanout stanzas, automatic stored device identity inclusion for pre-key
  ciphertext relay stanzas, explicit duplicate avoidance for caller-supplied
  identity nodes, high-level direct-send `c.us` remote normalization before
  device lookup/relay/tc-token handling, no-recipient-device failure coverage,
  session-assertion failure stop coverage, and focused relay stanza tests.
- Group sender-key outbound relay foundation with root `enc` stanza generation,
  provider-backed `skmsg` enforcement, group-JID validation, client
  `send_group_sender_key_text`/generic `send_group_sender_key_message`/
  `relay_group_sender_key_message` helpers, canonical `c.us` participant alias
  handling before distribution-recipient device lookup, retry cache integration
  under the noise feature, and focused core/client stanza tests.
- Store-backed sender-key provider foundation with codec-level group-session
  bypass, local-sender-key record encryption, inbound distribution processing
  into provider records, inbound `skmsg` verification/decryption/record
  advancement, and client facade construction from authenticated credentials.
- Outbound sender-key distribution setup foundation with local record
  create-or-load semantics, current-state distribution frame generation,
  top-level sender-key distribution message construction, client direct-fanout
  relay to participant devices, and focused core/client tests.
- High-level group sender-key send foundation with participant metadata lookup,
  device discovery, missing-session assertion before distribution fanout,
  two-stanza distribution-plus-`skmsg` orchestration, and text/generic
  `MessageContent` convenience helpers with focused mock-connection tests,
  including no-recipient-device failure coverage and session-assertion failure
  stop coverage.
- Status/broadcast fanout foundation with audience device discovery, own-device
  lookup for linked-device fanout, missing-session assertion before relay,
  `status@broadcast` participant-node relay construction, and text/proto/generic
  `MessageContent` client helpers with focused mock-connection tests, including
  duplicate own linked-device PN/LID alias collapse, no-recipient-device failure
  coverage, and session-assertion failure stop coverage.
- Group retry sender-key redistribution foundation with requester JID carried
  into prepared retry jobs, canonical requester-device discovery for all-device
  group retries, one sender-key distribution relay per group retry target
  before cached message replay, all-device no-recipient and session-assertion
  failure stop coverage, all-device relay-failure cache preservation, and
  focused participant/primary-requester retry tests.
- Retry key-bundle device-identity reissue foundation that carries parsed inline
  retry `device-identity` payloads into pre-key retry resend options, reuses the
  same payload for group sender-key redistribution relays, preserves the public
  prepared-resend API for caller-supplied replays, and covers direct/group retry
  resend stanzas plus participant-target session-assertion failure stops and
  relay-failure cache preservation with focused tests.
- Reporting-token foundation with eligibility checks for excluded message
  classes, message-secret and message-id validation including explicit 32-byte
  message-secret length rejection, direct-key and participant-bearing
  message-key JID direction coverage, allowlisted protobuf field filtering over
  encoded message bytes, HKDF/HMAC token derivation, typed
  `reporting/reporting_token` node construction, high-level direct-send, group
  sender-key, and status/broadcast attachment after final message-id
  preparation, retry-replay suppression, duplicate caller-supplied node
  avoidance, peer-category suppression coverage, future-proof wrapped-message
  recursion and inner message-secret lookup for wrapper-only encrypted
  payloads including modern status/lottie/poll-v4 wrapper fields, and focused
  core/client tests.
  Broader send-parity edge cases remain unfinished.
- Poll/event send foundation now includes poll/event creation messages,
  caller-supplied encrypted poll-update and event-response message builders,
  message-secret-derived encrypted poll-vote and event-RSVP payload builders
  with decrypt round-trip helpers, duplicate poll-option rejection, and negative
  extra-guest-count rejection, selected poll-vote option-hash duplicate
  rejection, opt-in receive-side decrypted update annotation from caller-supplied
  creation-secret maps, automatic native-store lookup of persisted poll/event
  creation-message secrets during live client receive processing including
  future-proof wrapped creation messages, delayed
  pending poll-update and event-response decryption when the creation message
  arrives later, future-proof wrapped poll/event update receive mapping for live
  and history add-on paths plus delayed pending-update merge, generic
  `MessageContent` variants, direct client facades, and focused core/client
  relay tests. Broader live decrypted receive orchestration and edge-case
  coverage remain unfinished.
- Receipt/read-message foundation with typed receipt kinds, receipt stanza
  construction including timestamped read/read-self receipts and multi-id
  `list/item` payloads, sender-receipt addressing, aggregation of non-own
  message keys by chat/participant, and client `send_receipt`, `send_receipts`,
  and `read_messages` helpers.
- Media retry receipt-node foundation with encrypted retry request stanza
  construction, media retry update parsing from `rmr` receipt nodes, error/status
  mapping, requester JID normalization, and client helpers for sending
  caller-supplied or media-key-encrypted retry payloads.
- Media metadata bridge helpers that convert encrypted media output into uploaded
  media descriptors, derive download URLs from direct paths, verify plaintext and
  encrypted SHA-256 hashes, and decrypt media bytes with metadata validation for
  focused in-memory test coverage.
- Bounded media transfer foundation with a pluggable transport trait,
  encryption-before-upload orchestration, uploaded-media metadata construction,
  hash-verified download/decrypt orchestration, configurable upload/download
  size limits, and client facade helpers for upload/download byte workflows.
- Media connection and HTTP transfer foundation with typed media-connection IQ
  construction/parsing, host/auth/TTL extraction, direct and child error-result
  validation, URL-safe upload-token generation from encrypted media hashes,
  kind-to-upload-path mapping, optional `http-media` transport backed by
  `reqwest`, local HTTP upload/download test coverage, and client facade support
  for fetching media connection info.
- Bounded media upload-cache foundation with typed cache keys based on media kind,
  plaintext SHA-256, and length; cache-entry validation before reuse; a
  capacity-limited TTL in-memory cache with LRU eviction; cached upload transfer
  and client facade helpers; and focused tests proving reuse, expiry, eviction,
  and bad-entry rejection.
- Bounded file-source media helper foundation with async chunked file reads and
  writes, pre-read file-size validation, upload/download-to-file helpers,
  cached file upload facade methods, file size-limit rejection before transport
  work, stream-encrypted file upload that avoids buffering the plaintext file,
  file-backed streamed HTTP upload bodies for encrypted media transfers,
  streamed HTTP download bodies into encrypted temporary files for
  download-to-file workflows,
  authenticated streaming download-to-file through a verified temporary-file
  path, multi-chunk file transfer validation, streamed HTTP over-limit
  rejection that preserves existing targets, and focused core/client tests.
  Byte-returning media APIs still buffer by contract.
- Receive-side stanza foundation with typed ACK/NACK construction, client
  `send_ack`/`send_nack` helpers, known NACK and server ACK error constants,
  inbound ACK/receipt/notification metadata parsers, addressing-context
  extraction, and message-stanza metadata decoding into typed keys, authors,
  senders, and message classes without panicking on malformed network input.
- Inbound message payload decode boundary with strict random-padding removal,
  plaintext node decoding, encrypted `msg`/`pkmsg`/`skmsg` payload extraction
  through an async decryptor trait, device-sent-message unwrapping,
  sender-key-distribution callback handling, and focused malformed-payload
  tests in both all-feature and no-default builds.
- Receive-event mapping helpers that convert decoded inbound messages into
  typed message upsert events with encoded protobuf payloads and stable metadata
  fields, convert inbound receipts into receipt update events while preserving
  raw list-item participant/timestamp metadata on the node-aware receive path,
  convert inbound message ACKs into message status updates while preserving
  recipient metadata and keeping malformed ACKs as handled per-node errors
  instead of aborting raw/offline receive batches, convert inbound media retry
  receipt nodes into typed media retry events that retain encrypted retry
  payloads and error status data, convert decoded reaction messages into typed reaction
  update events, convert decoded edit/revoke/pin protocol messages into typed
  message update/delete events, convert decoded encrypted poll vote update
  messages into typed message update events for the target poll, convert
  decoded encrypted event RSVP response messages into typed message update
  events for the target event, convert inbound call stanzas into typed call
  events, and push decoded messages through the bounded event buffer without
  bypassing pending item limits. Reaction, revoke, pin, poll-update,
  event-response, and history message-add-on mapping now share the
  future-proof unwrapped message view where Baileys hides those payloads behind
  wrapper fields.
- Receive-side native-store persistence that writes typed message
  upsert/update/delete events, chat upsert/update/delete events, contact
  upsert/update/delete events, group update events, receipt update events,
  reaction update events, label edit events, label association events,
  quick-reply update events, media-retry events, call events, and history
  chat/contact/message batches from direct receive calls and spawned
  incoming-processor events into the versioned native store while preserving the
  existing bounded event-buffer emission path and LID/PN mapping persistence.
  Pending message updates now also merge into later message upserts instead of
  being deleted, including delayed poll/event decrypted update annotation when
  the later creation-message upsert supplies the previously missing secret and
  the original update-message payload is still stored.
- Fair-yielding offline-node processing that walks offline child nodes through
  the same inbound message/receipt/ack/notification router, returns every child
  response for ACK/NACK emission, integrates with the spawned raw-node processor,
  and keeps the bounded event-buffer and native-store persistence paths intact.
  A direct offline-node combined facade now also runs placeholder resend, retry
  resend, and media retry handling for offline child batches, with a
  Signal-provider wrapper for the same client-owned decrypt/encrypt boundary.
- Live history sync event-pump facade that downloads and processes encrypted
  history sync notifications, persists resulting chat/contact/message/update
  batches and LID/PN mappings through the native receive-store path, and emits
  the same typed batch plus mapping events through the client event hub for
  subscribers. A transfer-free inline notification facade also scans decoded
  message events, deduplicates history notifications across direct upsert and
  batch event shapes, decodes inline bootstrap payloads, and feeds the same
  persistence/emission path.
- Media retry application foundation with deterministic mock notification
  encryption for protocol tests, encrypted media retry notification decryption,
  stanza/result validation, refreshed media descriptor construction from retry
  direct paths, message-secret retention, retry-download helpers over the
  existing media transfer boundary, and client facade coverage for descriptor
  refresh plus retry downloads.
- Bounded media retry coordinator foundation with message-keyed pending media
  descriptors, media-kind and fallback-host retention, strict descriptor
  validation, configurable capacity and TTL eviction, LRU pressure handling,
  pending-state removal after successful retry downloads, a client-owned default
  coordinator, facade helpers for registering pending media plus consuming typed
  retry events, versioned pending-descriptor store encoding/decoding,
  coordinator snapshot/restore, and client store-backed pending retry
  registration, restart restore, lazy persisted batch/event handoff during
  receive processing, video/document stored-replay coverage, SQLite-backed
  failed-to-success retry replacement and multi-page replay coverage,
  malformed stored retry-event and pending-descriptor cleanup, and
  successful-download cleanup helpers.
- Media retry batch handling foundation that consumes typed retry events from an
  `EventBatch`, drives the bounded pending-media coordinator and transfer path,
  downloads successful retry results, ignores unmatched retry events without
  allocating new state, collects per-message retry errors, and exposes the path
  through the client facade.
- Opt-in incoming-node media retry processing that routes a live received node
  through the inbound parser, sends required ACK/NACK responses, flushes typed
  event batches, drives the client-owned pending-media coordinator and transfer
  path for retry events, returns downloaded retry media or per-message retry
  errors to the caller, and still emits the typed batch to subscribers.
- Automatic spawned incoming-node media retry processing that watches raw socket
  node events, sends required ACK/NACK responses, persists and emits the normal
  receive batches, drives the client-owned pending-media coordinator and media
  transfer path for retry events in the background, removes pending state after
  successful retry downloads, and emits a typed in-memory
  `MediaRetryProcessed` event with downloads/errors for subscribers. A combined
  direct incoming-node facade and spawned raw-node processor now also run
  placeholder resend, retry resend, and media retry handling through one receive
  flow, with Signal-provider wrappers that reuse the client-owned provider codec
  for decrypt and resend encryption.
- Inbound node processor foundation that routes `message`, `receipt`, `ack`, and
  `notification` stanzas through the receive parsers/event mappers, returns
  typed processing results, builds ACK/NACK response nodes where required, and
  preserves bounded event-buffer enforcement for queued events.
- Client incoming-node facade that uses authenticated account JID/LID metadata
  to drive the inbound node processor, writes required ACK/NACK response stanzas
  over the active connection, and flushes typed receive events into the bounded
  client event stream.
- Automatic incoming processor task handle that subscribes to raw socket node
  events, routes them through the same inbound processor, sends required
  ACK/NACK responses, flushes typed events into the client event stream, stops
  on connection close, reports lag as a protocol error, and aborts on drop so no
  background task is silently detached.
- Raw-node event boundary for connection/router dispatch so socket-originated
  nodes can be consumed by the incoming processor without reprocessing
  post-processor node events such as notifications.
- Bounded retry/session-recovery manager foundation with recent-message cache,
  retry counters, MAC-error and missing-session recreation decisions, session
  recreation cooldowns, base-key collision tracking, deterministic delayed phone
  request scheduling without runtime timers, statistics, and explicit cache
  capacity/TTL configuration.
- Retry receipt handling foundation with strict inbound retry receipt parsing,
  bounded multi-id receipt extraction with non-empty list item IDs, public
  retry planning/preparation message-id count validation, retry reason/error
  parsing, registration-id parsing, key-bundle presence detection, resend target
  selection for primary versus specific device requesters, group sender-key
  clear decisions, and session recovery action planning for refresh, bundle
  injection, stale registration, base-key collision cases, and recent-message
  cache resend preparation that reports missing message IDs without
  reconstructing unavailable payloads. Retry receipt parser fuzz coverage now
  decodes arbitrary binary nodes, parses successful retry receipts, and drives
  bounded retry planning/preparation with a missing-session snapshot to harden
  this network-facing retry/session-recovery surface.
- Retry resend execution foundation with a client-owned bounded message retry
  manager, automatic recent-message caching after successful direct relay,
  public plan/prepare/statistics facades, prepared-job execution to requesting
  devices or rediscovered direct-send device sets through the existing relay
  pipeline, successful retry cache cleanup, automatic retry session-action
  handling for stale-session deletion and forced session refresh, opt-in
  incoming-node and spawned raw-node retry receipt handling that ACKs then
  replays cached messages, inline retry key-bundle payload parsing/injection,
  typed retry key-bundle device-identity payload preservation/exposure, group
  sender-key memory cleanup for group retry receipts, requester-device
  rediscovery for all-device group retries, sender-key distribution relay before
  group retry replay, retry key-bundle device-identity reissue for pre-key retry
  replays and group sender-key redistribution relays, and focused replay tests.
  Outbound pre-key relay identity-node attachment is covered by the direct-device
  relay foundation above; broader group sender-key cryptographic
  retry/redistribution edge cases and live retry key-bundle device-identity
  validation remain unfinished.
- Placeholder resend request/response foundation with a typed peer-data operation
  protocol-message builder, validated batched message keys, high-priority peer
  relay to the authenticated account JID, default meta-node attachment,
  caller-option preservation, peer-data response decoding from embedded
  `WebMessageInfo` bytes into recovered message-upsert events, request-id/source
  metadata retention, integration with decoded-message event batches, a bounded
  duplicate-request tracker with TTL/capacity, duplicate rejection before
  network work, explicit response-event pending-request resolution, eligible
  unavailable-stub detection from decoded `WebMessageInfo` with age and excluded
  unavailable-type filters, a client facade that drives peer-data requests from
  those detected stubs while suppressing duplicates, manual expired-request
  purge support, an owned background cleanup task that purges expired pending
  requests without detaching, receive-side raw unavailable-message stub mapping
  with ACK/no-event handling for excluded unavailable fanouts, opt-in live
  incoming-node processing that emits the placeholder stub then sends the
  peer-data request, automatic pending-request resolution from live
  placeholder-response batches, opt-in spawned raw-node processor dispatch for
  direct and offline unavailable-message stubs, and focused core/client mock
  coverage.
- Typed tctoken native-store foundation with size-bounded binary records,
  validated JID keys, received-token and sender-marker timestamp retention,
  load/save/delete helpers, malformed record rejection, marker-preserving expiry
  cleanup, one-to-one send-node construction and high-level send attachment,
  privacy-token issue IQ construction, trusted-contact token result parsing,
  merge-and-store helpers, explicit client issuance facade, SQLite-backed
  helper plus mock-query tests, and bounded automatic fire-and-forget post-send
  issuance for eligible one-to-one sends with in-flight coalescing and mock
  transport coverage. Post-send issuance coverage now also holds the first
  privacy-token issue in flight across two eligible sends to the same target and
  proves the second send does not emit a duplicate privacy IQ while still
  allowing the first result to persist the trusted-contact token. Spawned
  issuance failure coverage now also drives an error privacy IQ, proves the
  in-flight target is cleared without storing a token, and verifies a later
  schedule for the same target can issue and persist successfully. Identity-change
  token reissue foundation has landed with
  typed encrypt-notification outcomes, bounded debounce, existing-session and
  recent-sender-timestamp gates including missing/expired marker coverage,
  pre-refresh reissue scheduling, cross-source in-flight coalescing with already
  pending post-send issuance, live and spawned incoming-node hooks, and mock
  coverage. Bounded token pruning has
  landed with paginated store scans, malformed-record cleanup, regular-user
  gating, sender-marker preservation for recently issued tokens, and client
  facade coverage. Connection-open prune maintenance has landed with interval
  throttling, bounded batch sizing, an owned abort-on-drop task handle, and
  event-driven mock coverage. Profile-picture URL token attachment has landed
  with LID-aware storage lookup, regular-user gating, self-target skips,
  group-target skips, duplicate avoidance, and mock query coverage.
  Presence-subscribe token attachment has landed with LID-aware storage lookup,
  regular-user gating that skips group targets, self PN/LID target skips, and
  sent-node coverage. Direct send tctoken attachment now reuses the shared
  normalized storage-JID resolver, so legacy `@c.us` targets find canonical
  PN-stored tokens and LID mappings consistently with profile-picture and
  presence paths. Direct send tctoken attachment now also preserves explicit
  caller-supplied token sidecars and skips generated tokens for peer-category
  relay options. Post-send issuance planning now has explicit peer-category and
  protocol-message skip coverage plus native-store sender-marker freshness
  coverage. Direct send tctoken attachment and post-send issuance planning now
  also skip the client's own PN and LID identities, and the explicit issuance
  facade now returns without a
  privacy query for self PN/LID targets. Mapped self-target checks now consult
  the native PN/LID mapping store, so self-skipping attachment paths, explicit
  issuance, post-send issuance planning, identity-change reissue planning, and
  ACK 463 recovery skip the client's own identity even when credentials only
  expose one side of the PN/LID pair. Incoming privacy-token notifications now
  also skip mapped self senders before storing received tokens. Retry replays
  now suppress tctoken sidecar attachment,
  matching the non-new-send behavior. Broader tctoken orchestration edge cases
  remain unfinished.
- Chat/account operations foundation with typed privacy settings fetch/update
  IQs, default disappearing-mode updates, profile status and profile-picture
  URL/update/remove IQ helpers, shared account mutation result validation for
  privacy/default-disappearing/profile-status/profile-picture/blocklist updates,
  blocklist fetch/update parsing, LID/PN-aware block/unblock facade behavior
  through the native mapping store, feature-gated bounded profile-picture JPEG
  image/preview generation plus a client update helper, presence and chat-state
  node builders, presence subscription helpers, public client facade methods,
  and mock connection coverage for stanza shape plus parsed results.
- App-state/chat-mutation foundation with typed app-state collection requests,
  dirty-bit clean IQ construction, raw encoded patch upload IQ construction,
  app-state sync and patch-upload result validation, pre-encryption chat
  mutation builders for mute/archive/read-pin-star/delete/push-name sync
  actions, message-range conversion to generated protobuf types, exact
  sync-action protobuf encoding from JSON index bytes, app-state key expansion
  plus index/value/snapshot/patch MAC helpers, deterministic encrypted
  mutation-record construction, native LT-hash add/subtract arithmetic,
  outbound app-state hash/index-value-MAC state evolution for set/overwrite/
  remove mutations, typed `SyncdPatch` bundle construction with derived next
  version/snapshot/patch MAC fields, inbound patch decode with value/index/
  patch/snapshot MAC verification and state advancement, verified snapshot
  decode/application into exact-version app-state baselines, typed sync response
  extraction for collections, patch bytes, has-more flags, and snapshot blob
  references, bounded external app-state blob download/decrypt/decode helpers
  for snapshots and mutation blobs, random-IV chat mutation encryption, initial
  high-level pin/archive/mute/read/delete/star/profile-name/contact/
  quick-reply/label mutation upload facades plus opt-in upload-and-apply
  variants, pure receive-side decoded
  sync-action to `EventBatch` mapping for chat/contact/message/label/
  quick-reply store updates, compact native-store app-state patch-state
  persistence, decoded patch/snapshot apply helpers that save state and emit
  mapped event batches through the client facade, inline sync-response
  orchestration that fetches a sync response, decodes inline patches in order
  from native store state, persists advanced state, emits mapped event batches,
  reports pending snapshots/has-more flags, and follows bounded has-more inline
  patch pages until current, plus external snapshot recovery that downloads,
  MAC-verifies, applies, persists, and emits recovered snapshot batches before
  continuing any remaining pages, native app-state sync-key load/save helpers,
  store-key external snapshot recovery that loads snapshot key IDs from native
  sync-key storage, parks missing snapshot keys as blocked collections, applies
  decoded snapshots, and continues bounded pagination, store-key sync
  application that parks collections blocked on missing sync keys without
  treating the whole sync as fatal, and bounded store-key pagination that stops
  retrying blocked collections until their key arrives, direct patch/snapshot
  application plus explicit-key sync application that clear stale blocked
  markers after successful apply, app-state
  sync-key-share extraction from self-originated protocol messages, key-share
  persistence, automatic blocked-collection resync through manual and spawned
  incoming-node processing paths, and an opt-in key-share snapshot recovery path
  that downloads and applies external snapshots after saving the shared key;
  opt-in chat/profile mutation upload-and-apply facades that persist the
  accepted app-state cursor, derive typed local event batches from the submitted
  mutation, save those chat/contact/message/label/quick-reply side effects
  through the native store, and emit the same batches through the client event
  hub;
  public client facade methods; and mock connection coverage for stanza shape,
  inline patch application, snapshot recovery, missing-key
  retry-after-key-arrival, sync-key-share unblock reporting, and key-share
  snapshot recovery flows.
- History sync foundation with bounded zlib inflate/decode for inline and
  downloaded history payloads, media-key verified external history download
  using the existing pluggable media transfer boundary, typed conversion into
  `HistorySetEvent`/`EventBatch` values for chats, contacts, and messages,
  history `WebMessageInfo` reaction, poll update, and event response add-on
  extraction plus generic `message_add_ons` poll/event extraction into typed
  target-message update events, native-store persistence for those
  chat/contact/message history events and reaction/add-on updates, LID/PN
  mapping extraction from direct mapping
  records and conversation fields, item-count limits for mapped history events,
  non-blocking data mapping for pushnames, status messages, accounts, recent
  stickers, call logs, past participant group updates, global-settings default
  disappearing mode, and LID/PN mapping records, public client facade methods
  including decoded message upsert/batch notification scanning, and
  focused core/client mock transport coverage.
- Group operations foundation with typed `w:g2` IQ builders, metadata and
  participating-group parsers, create/leave/subject/description helpers with
  mutation result validation, participant add/remove/promote/demote result
  mapping including Baileys-style raw participant result content, invite
  code/revoke/accept/info helpers, ephemeral/settings/
  member-add/join-approval helpers with mutation result validation, join-request
  list and approve/reject helpers, client facade methods, and mock connection
  coverage for outbound stanza shape plus parsed results. Group and community
  join-request list parsers now also preserve Baileys-style PN/LID/username
  metadata aliases in the returned `GroupJoinRequest` values.
- Group notification event foundation that maps recognized inbound group
  notifications for subject, description, description deletion,
  announcement/restrict settings, ephemeral duration, member-add/join-approval
  mode, and participant add/remove/promote/demote changes into typed group
  update batches while retaining raw notification delivery and ACK behavior.
  Group notification parsing now also preserves Baileys-style actor, author,
  participant, and membership-request fallback aliases such as `senderPn`,
  `phoneNumber`, `pn`, `pn_jid`, `lid_jid`, `lidJid`, and
  `participantUsername` in the existing typed update fields. Pending
  membership-approval request list notifications now also carry validated LID,
  PN, and username metadata through `join_requests_*` fields and persisted
  group-membership request stubs, and all membership-request stubs preserve
  available usernames in their serialized participant objects. Group and
  community join-request parsing now also accepts camel-case `requestMethod`
  aliases in addition to `request_method`/`method`, and invite/accept
  notifications preserve Baileys-style invite-admin PN and username aliases
  such as `adminPn`, `adminUsername`, `author_pn`, and `author_username`.
  Invite-accept notifications that omit participant children now synthesize
  participant-accept fields from the parent notification participant/PN/username
  metadata so durable `GroupParticipantAccept` stubs are still emitted.
- Group dirty refresh workflow that fetches the participating-group snapshot
  first, then marks the group dirty bit clean only after the refresh query
  succeeds, returning both refreshed group metadata and the clean marker node
  through the client facade, with typed dirty-notification processing,
  notification-triggered refresh through the incoming processor, and focused
  group-update event emission from refreshed metadata. Dirty refresh group and
  community events now also include participant snapshot fields and
  admin/superadmin subsets from the refreshed participating snapshot. Spawned
  incoming processors now treat a `groups` dirty notification as covering both
  participating groups and participating communities, fetch both snapshots, and
  clean the shared group dirty bit only after the selected refresh queries
  succeed. Dirty notification parsing now also exposes all dirty children in a
  wrapper node, and group/community refresh orchestration scans every dirty
  child so an unrelated dirty marker cannot hide a later group-surface refresh.
  Group participating snapshot errors now propagate instead of being treated as
  empty successful snapshots, so dirty cleanup is not sent after a failed group
  refresh.
- Group metadata and join-request PN/LID alias parsing now validates optional
  participant JID aliases, and linked-parent metadata is validated as a group
  JID before it can feed community-resolution workflows.
- Group v4 invite query foundation with validated typed invite descriptors,
  accept and revoke IQ builders, result parsing, client facade methods, opt-in
  accepted-invite message update/upsert event emission, transactional native
  store persistence for those side effects, buffered emission variants, and mock
  connection coverage for stanza shape plus emitted and persisted side effects.
  The v4 accept facades now return the validated accepted group/community JID
  from the result stanza, matching Baileys' response shape, while revoke-v4 keeps
  the boolean success parser.
- Newsletter foundation with reusable bounded WMex JSON IQ envelope handling
  including direct and child stanza-error validation, typed metadata
  lookup/create/update/follow/mute/delete/admin-count builders, metadata and
  count parsers, direct message-update/live-update/reaction stanza builders,
  typed fetched-message result parsing into message events, typed reaction
  result validation, receive-side direct plaintext message, reaction, view,
  participant, and settings notification event mapping, MEX settings and
  admin-promotion notification mapping, linked-profile notification parser/event
  mapping, receive-side native-store persistence for linked-profile mappings,
  client facade methods, and focused mock connection coverage.
- Business profile, catalog, and product mutation foundation with typed profile
  fetch/update IQ builders, profile and cover-photo mutation result validation,
  business-hours serialization/parsing, catalog query builder, product/catalog
  parser, product create/update/delete builders/parsers, typed pre-uploaded
  product image references, product-image single/batch upload bridge methods,
  product create/update image-upload orchestration methods, metadata-preserving
  media upload results, cover-photo update/delete profile IQ builders,
  collection query/parser, order detail query/parser, client facade methods,
  focused core parser tests, and mock connection facade coverage.
  Business media edge cases, notifications, broader business workflows,
  broader mock validation, and ignored-by-default live e2e validation remain
  unfinished.
- Community IQ/facade foundation with typed metadata and participating-community
  queries, community create/subgroup-create/leave/subject/description helpers,
  link/unlink and linked-group parsing, Baileys-style linked-group parent
  resolution for subgroup inputs, participant and join-request actions,
  invite/v4-invite query helpers, ephemeral/settings/member-add/join-approval
  helpers, shared community mutation result validation for leave/subject/
  description/link/unlink/ephemeral/settings/member-add/join-approval updates,
  explicit participating-community dirty refresh that cleans the group dirty bit
  only after a successful refresh query, typed dirty-notification parsing,
  notification-triggered refresh through the incoming processor, public client
  facade methods, opt-in accepted-invite message update/upsert event emission,
  transactional native store persistence for those side effects, buffered
  emission variants, and focused core/client mock coverage. Normal community
  invite acceptance now parses the Baileys `<community jid="...">` result shape
  instead of falling through the group-only parser, and community metadata reads
  Baileys' child-node `addressing_mode` form. Community create and
  subgroup-create now handle Baileys' `<group id="...">`/`<group jid="...">`
  result stub by fetching full group metadata before returning. Broader
  protocol edge cases, broader mock validation, and ignored-by-default live e2e
  validation remain unfinished.
- Account restriction and new-chat capping foundation with typed WMex query
  builders/parsers, direct and child stanza-error validation, typed account
  update events, receive-side WMex notification parsing for reachout timelock
  and message-capping updates, client facade fetch methods, and focused mock
  connection plus inbound processing coverage.
- Core query manager with unique tags, duplicate protection, response waiters,
  timeout cleanup, pending-waiter shutdown, and tests.
- Core connection runtime scaffold with mockable frame sink/stream traits,
  bounded outbound queue, typed inbound frame events, tagged query-response
  routing, lifecycle close handling, and task-cleanup regression tests.
- Binary-node dispatch helpers that decode inbound protocol frames, extract
  `attrs.id` response tags, resolve pending query waiters, and emit unmatched
  decoded nodes as typed events.
- Connection runtime binary-node integration with encoded node send/query
  helpers, inbound binary-node decoding, query resolution by node id, and
  fallback raw-frame emission for non-node payloads.
- Feature-gated WebSocket transport adapter using `tokio-tungstenite`, default
  Rustls TLS support, optional native TLS support, binary-frame send/receive
  tests against a local server, text-frame rejection, and connection-runtime
  integration tests.
- Feature-gated Noise frame sink/stream runtime wrappers that encode outbound
  frames, decode fragmented inbound Noise frames, avoid holding handshake locks
  across network awaits, and plug into the same connection runtime traits.
- Typed client payload builders for login and registration, including user
  agent/web info fields, device pairing key bundle encoding, app-version hash,
  device properties, history sync capabilities, JID validation, and focused
  protobuf field tests.
- Basic event hub foundation with bounded broadcast capacity and typed
  connection, frame, node, QR, and credential-update events.
- Bounded event-buffer foundation with typed batch events for history snapshots,
  message upsert/update/delete, chat/contact upsert/update/delete, label edits,
  label associations, quick-reply updates, group updates, receipts, and
  reactions; consolidation merges updates by typed keys, applies deletes, keeps
  immediate lifecycle events ordered before batches, and rejects pushes that
  would exceed the configured pending-item limit.
- End-to-end mock connection validation that sends the client Noise hello, reads
  a mock server hello, encrypts login or registration payloads into the client
  finish, sends that finish before transport-state encryption begins, transitions
  into the shared Noise transport, spawns the bounded connection runtime, and
  emits typed connection updates for success and failure paths.
- Client facade scaffold with builder/config/prelude, event subscription, store
  ownership, query-manager ownership, initialized credential access, pairing QR
  data generation, pairing-code request preparation with credential persistence,
  live pairing-code request/completion helper, and exported connection runtime
  types.
- Public example foundation with compile-checked `wa-client` examples for
  SQLite-backed session/pairing setup, live pairing-code request/completion,
  live text/contact/location/poll/event/reaction/edit/delete/pin send plus event
  receive, dedicated live Signal-provider message receive, live history-sync
  notification download/process/persist, live chat-pin app-state mutation
  upload/apply, live
  status/broadcast
  text/poll/event, image/audio/sticker media, and generated-thumbnail
  video/document sends, live image/video/GIF/PTV/audio/PTT/document/sticker media
  upload/send, live link-preview
  thumbnail fetch/upload/send, live generated link-preview thumbnail
  upload/send, live generated video/document remote-thumbnail upload/send, live
  generated profile-picture update, live group metadata/actions, live group
  sender-key text send, live media retry request send, and a custom
  `AuthStore`, with Cargo feature requirements and README quickstart commands.
- Public documentation foundation with a separate API transition guide mapping
  upstream TypeScript reference concepts to Rust client/store/event/connection
  concepts, README linkage, and crate-level `wa-client` docs with a
  private-protocol warning and compile-checked quickstart doctest.
- Release policy foundation with a public feature support matrix, current crate
  feature-flag table, and pre-1.0 compatibility policy linked from the README.
- Ignored-by-default live e2e smoke harness foundation for `wa-client`, with
  opt-in text/contact/location/poll/event/reaction/edit/delete/pin sends,
  Signal-provider receive, history-sync event-pump, chat-pin app-state mutation,
  status text/poll/event sends, status image/audio/sticker media sends, status
  generated video/document remote-thumbnail sends, group sender-key text-send,
  direct image/GIF/PTV/PTT/audio/sticker media sends, media-retry request send,
  generated video/document remote-thumbnail sends, fetched and generated
  link-preview thumbnail sends,
  generated profile-picture update, and group-metadata tests gated by
  `WA_LIVE_E2E` plus target
  environment variables and Cargo feature gates.

Known major remaining gaps before the rewrite can be considered complete:

- Signal provider integration: plug a real maintained and license-compatible
  Signal protocol provider into the `SignalCryptoProvider` interface for
  remaining one-to-one ratchets and sender-key group cryptographic operations,
  or finish proving the project-owned provider against compatibility fixtures.
  The landed repository, opaque provider-state store, local key-material facade,
  outbound/inbound one-to-one pre-key/session chain foundation, and store-backed
  direct send/receive and sender-key codec boundaries are not full Signal
  protocol parity by themselves.
- Receive and sync-state integration: finish wiring remaining inbound/history
  state surfaces beyond the current persisted message/chat/contact/group/
  label/quick-reply/receipt/reaction/media-retry/call/presence/
  business-notification/newsletter/account-state event records, connect the
  remaining automatic raw-node pump surfaces to real Signal-backed
  decryptor/session operations beyond the explicit incoming-node
  Signal-provider, placeholder-resend, retry-resend, and media-retry
  convenience paths, and complete sync-state behavior beyond the current bounded
  broadcast/consolidation foundations.
- Test parity: generate broader fixtures from the local reference implementation,
  add broader golden-vector coverage, more property tests, and fuzz targets
  beyond the focused tests, expanded seeded binary-node fixture harness,
  deterministic Signal golden vectors, and bounded binary-node codec property
  coverage already added.
- Message send/decryption parity: broaden real Signal-backed direct
  encryption/decryption beyond the current outbound/inbound pre-key/session
  chain, high-level wrapper, provider-session reply reuse, and skipped
  one-to-one message-key foundation with current-ratchet preservation for late
  previous-chain messages plus transactional persisted skipped-key replay
  coverage; add remaining one-to-one ratchet edge cases and compatibility
  fixtures, further group sender-key and status/broadcast relay and placeholder
  resend hardening beyond the current transaction-backed and keyed-locked store
  convenience paths plus status user-audience validation, group sender-key
  cryptographic retry/redistribution beyond
  the current prepared resend execution plus session-action refresh/delete, inline
  key-bundle injection, direct store-backed retry resend wrappers, group
  sender-key memory cleanup, and retry sender-key redistribution foundations,
  retry key-bundle device-identity live validation,
  reporting-token edge cases beyond the current allowlisted node generation plus
  direct-send, group sender-key, status/broadcast attachment, and retry-replay
  suppression foundation,
  remaining tctoken orchestration beyond the current typed native-store,
  one-to-one attachment, explicit issuance facade, bounded automatic post-send
  issuance scheduler, identity-change reissue foundation, bounded pruning facade
  plus connection-open maintenance task, profile-picture URL IQ attachment,
  presence-subscribe attachment, and remaining poll/event send/receive edge
  cases beyond the message-secret-derived encrypted update and explicit
  receive-decryption helpers.
- Media parity: broaden large-media validation into performance/memory
  profiling and live/e2e-sized fixtures beyond the current multi-chunk and
  file-backed streamed file-path tests; broaden background media retry orchestration beyond the
  current typed event, retry-download, bounded pending coordinator, batch
  handling, lazy persisted pending-store handoff, opt-in incoming-node
  processing, and spawned raw-node processing helpers into live/e2e validation
  beyond the current live request-send and explicit pending-descriptor
  response-processing smokes; broaden optional generated thumbnail/profile-picture processing beyond the current bounded
  JPEG image-thumbnail, parent-key video/document remote-thumbnail upload,
  generated video/PDF remote-thumbnail extraction-plus-upload, live generated
  video/document remote-thumbnail send validation, link-preview image, live
  fetched/generated link-preview thumbnail send validation, and live generated
  profile-picture update validation into broader live/e2e validation.
- App-state, history, and chat parity: implement broader app-state retry/resync
  orchestration beyond the current inline patch application, bounded has-more
  pagination, external snapshot recovery, native sync-key storage, missing-key
  blocked-state, and key-share-triggered resync path; finish live history sync
  event-pump coverage and broader edge cases beyond the current bounded decode,
  external-download, deduplicated inline-notification scanning, event-mapping,
  native-store persistence, and facade foundation; broaden live/e2e chat/profile
  modification workflows beyond the current pin/archive/mute/read/delete/star/
  profile-name/contact/quick-reply/label upload-and-apply facades; and broaden
  recovered remote snapshot coverage through the current native-state
  persistence and decoded sync-action event mapping.
- Group parity: expand group notification edge-case coverage beyond the initial
  typed event mapper, and complete remaining group protocol edge cases beyond
  the landed group IQ, participant-operation, multi-child dirty-refresh, v4
  invite query, and persisted opt-in side-effect event foundations.
- Extended surface parity: complete broader newsletter send/receive, broader
  view tracking, broader linked-profile mapping edge cases, and
  admin/participant workflow edge cases beyond the current newsletter
  metadata/action/query/fetched-message/plaintext-message/reaction/view/
  reaction-result/participant/settings/MEX-admin-promotion/linked-profile event
  and receive-side persistence foundation; complete
  missing business notifications, business media edge cases, and broader
  workflows beyond the current
  profile/catalog/product/collection/order/product-image/cover-photo foundation;
  complete broader community edge cases beyond the current community IQ/facade,
  dirty-refresh, notification-triggered refresh, and persisted opt-in
  side-effect event foundations.
- Release/user-facing readiness: broaden public examples, API docs, the
  transition guide, support matrix, and ignored-by-default live e2e coverage as
  live workflows stabilize beyond the current compile-checked/public-doc/smoke
  foundations. Live e2e tests remain in this repository and ignored by default
  unless explicitly configured.

## VM Handoff Snapshot

This section is the fast source of truth when moving the work to a new VM. It
summarizes the current state without replacing the detailed progress ledger
above.

### Current State Summary

The Rust workspace has broad foundations in place across protocol types, binary
node handling, auth/key storage, Noise setup, Signal repository/provider-state
boundaries, connection/query management, message construction, direct-device
relay scaffolding, receive-side parsing, event buffering, native-store
persistence, media helper foundations, app-state/history foundations, groups,
newsletters, business helpers, communities, and account restriction/capping
helpers.

Recent completed work added a public `wa-client` example and documentation
foundation: README quickstart commands, a separate API transition guide from
upstream TypeScript reference concepts to Rust concepts, a feature support
matrix with pre-1.0 compatibility policy, crate-level `wa-client` docs, and
examples covering SQLite session/pairing setup, live text send plus event
receive, live status/broadcast text send, live image media upload/send, live
group metadata/actions, live group sender-key text send, and a custom
`AuthStore`; each example is feature-gated so CI can compile the supported API
shape without enabling unavailable optional surfaces. It also added an
ignored-by-default live e2e smoke harness for configured text-send,
status/broadcast text-send, group sender-key text-send, image media, and group
metadata checks. Earlier app-state work added high-level chat/profile
mutation helpers with explicit
upload-and-apply
variants that retain the existing upload-only behavior for compatibility, then
after server acceptance persist the advanced app-state cursor, map the submitted
mutation through the decoded sync-action event mapper, save typed native-store
side effects for chat/contact/message/label/quick-reply records, and emit the
same local batch through the client event hub. Earlier media work added
streaming AES-CBC/HMAC media encrypt/decrypt state machines, chunked file upload,
streamed HTTP download-to-file verification, bounded JPEG thumbnail generation
for image/video/PTV/document messages and profile pictures, configurable
ffmpeg-backed video frame extraction, and `pdftoppm`-backed PDF page extraction.
Spawned incoming-node processing can automatically drive the client-owned
pending-media retry coordinator and emit typed in-memory retry download
outcomes. The optional `link-preview` feature maps uploaded `ThumbnailLink`
media into high-quality preview metadata, supports cached/file upload variants,
and composes bounded preview-image generation with upload. The optional
`link-preview,image` combination can fetch preview images, then generate and
upload inline/high-quality preview thumbnails into message-ready
`LinkPreviewContent`. Pending media retry descriptors can be handed off to the
native store, restored into the bounded coordinator after restart, and removed
from storage after successful retry downloads, with stored replay coverage for
image, video, and document media kinds plus SQLite-backed failed-retry
retention, later success cleanup, multi-page replay cleanup, and malformed
stored retry-event/pending-descriptor cleanup.
Video/document remote thumbnail upload has a parent-media-key foundation for
`ThumbnailVideo`/`ThumbnailDocument` descriptors that can be attached to message
protos, and the optional `noise,image` path can generate those remote thumbnail
uploads from configured video-frame and PDF-page extractors.
File-backed large-media transfer coverage now exercises an e2e-sized
multi-chunk upload/download path without using the in-memory ciphertext
transport, proving chunked temp-file encryption, file-backed upload, streamed
download-to-file, and final decrypt verification on the same fixture.
Group dirty-refresh orchestration now scans all dirty children in a wrapped
notification node, so unrelated dirty markers cannot suppress later group or
community refreshes.
Full media parity still needs broader performance/memory profiling beyond the
current file-backed fixture, live/e2e media retry response/roundtrip validation
beyond request-send smoke, live/e2e validation for fetched/generated preview
thumbnails, and live/e2e coverage for video/document remote thumbnail
extraction/upload.

The project is not production-complete. The biggest remaining blockers are real
Signal provider integration or fixture-proven completion of the project-owned
provider, broader one-to-one ratchet edge cases, full Signal-backed send/decrypt
relay behavior, remaining group/status send edge cases, broader media retry
live/e2e validation and storage edge cases, broader receive/sync-state integration,
compatibility fixtures, fuzz/property coverage, broader API docs/transition
coverage, and broader ignored-by-default live e2e coverage.

### Worktree Transfer Note

Before moving to a VM, transfer the full workspace state, not only files already
known to the original git baseline. In the current environment, key rewrite
files show as untracked relative to that baseline. Either copy the whole working
directory, create a patch bundle, or commit the current state before moving.

At minimum, preserve these paths:

- `Cargo.toml`
- `rust-toolchain.toml`
- `crates/`
- `docs/`
- `tools/`
- `tests/`
- the local TypeScript reference directory used only as protocol reference input

### Latest Verified Gates

Recent broad implementation slices have been verified with:

- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --all-features --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --all-features --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-store -p wa-core -p wa-client --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --workspace --all-features --quiet`
- naming scan over `docs`, `crates`, root manifests, toolchain file, and `tools`
- trailing-whitespace scan over touched Rust files and this plan
- `git diff --check` over touched Rust files and this plan

The deterministic Signal fixture hardening slice added after the
release-readiness reverify added pre-key base-key/inner-ratchet mismatch and
provider-session active receiving-chain invalid skipped-key rejection vectors,
provider-session local ratchet key-pair mismatch, receiving-chain/remote-ratchet
pairing, skipped-keys-without-remote-ratchet, and uninitialized send-chain
rejection vectors, a provider-session skipped-key zero-counter rejection vector,
plus sender-key distribution malformed-wire rejection, sender-key message
malformed-wire rejection, and sender-key signing-key-pair mismatch,
duplicate-state, duplicate skipped-iteration, and future skipped-iteration
rejection vectors, and repaired fixture data so valid pre-key base keys match
their inner ratchet keys and sender-key records with private signing material use
the derived public signing key. It was verified in the current VM with:

The Signal provider-session bounded skipped-key pruning fixture slice added
after the whisper-message required-field fixture work promoted the one-to-one
provider ratchet retention boundary into the public Signal manifest. The new
fixture derives deterministic first, second, and far-ahead whisper messages,
asserts that decrypting the far-ahead message retains exactly the bounded
skipped-key window, rejects the pruned first message with the stable old-counter
error, and still decrypts the oldest retained second message. The core unit
test now exact-matches the same pruned-message error. It was verified in the
current VM with:

processing through `SignalMessageCodec` and persisted `SignalProviderSenderKey`

The status/broadcast device-target validation hardening slice added after the
store-backed sender-key distribution work rejects device-scoped status audience
targets before USync device discovery. The focused regression now covers numeric
device JIDs, malformed device suffixes, and empty targets in the same no-query
path as group and `status@broadcast` rejection, keeping status audiences at the
account/user level before fanout expands them to devices. It was verified in the
current VM with:

The app-state explicit-key blocked-marker cleanup slice added after the
status/broadcast device-target validation work closes a resync stale-state edge
case. Applying a sync response with caller-supplied key data now deletes any
persisted missing-key blocked marker for that collection after the collection
successfully applies, matching the store-key sync path and preventing later
key-share recovery from retrying an already-unblocked collection. It was
verified in the current VM with:

The group notification join-request edge-form slice added after the invite
lifecycle work broadens the typed `w:gp2` notification mapper for
`membership_approval_request(s)` and `membership_requests_action` children.
Pending join requests now surface ordered requester JIDs plus timestamp and
request-method metadata, while approve/reject action notifications reuse the
participant metadata projection under dedicated `join_requests_approve` and
`join_requests_reject` fields. The shared first-non-empty attribute helper now
correctly falls through empty preferred attributes to later valid fallback
attributes for these notification forms. It was verified in the current VM with:

The group notification membership-request lifecycle slice added after the
join-request edge-form work broadens the typed `w:gp2` notification mapper for
`created_membership_requests` and `revoked_membership_requests` children.
Lifecycle notifications now preserve fallback actor participant JIDs, PN
aliases, usernames, request methods, timestamps, and ordered created/revoked or
admin-rejected outcomes under dedicated `join_requests_created` and
`join_requests_revoked` fields. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core maps_group_notification_membership_request_lifecycle_forms --quiet`

The group notification leave/modify participant edge-form slice added after the
membership-request lifecycle work broadens the typed `w:gp2` notification

instead of being dropped by the group update mapper, covering the participant
leave and change-number notification shapes. It was verified in the current VM

The group notification self-remove-as-leave slice added after the picture/actor
alias work aligns `remove` participant notifications with Baileys' group stub
behavior when the single removed participant is the acting participant. These
notifications now retain the raw `participants_remove` projection, add a
`participants_remove_is_leave` marker, and also project the same participant
metadata under `participants_leave`; multi-participant removes continue to be

The devices notification session-cleanup slice added after the picture
contact-update work wires Baileys-style `devices` notifications into receive
processing. Core now parses `add`/`remove`/`update` device-list notifications
while skipping malformed device JIDs like Baileys, and `wa-client` runs the
side effect used by Baileys for `remove` notifications by deleting Signal
sessions for removed device JIDs across direct and spawned incoming processors.

The account-sync default disappearing-mode slice added after the blocklist
notification work wires Baileys-style `account_sync/disappearing_mode`
notifications into typed receive state. Core now parses the `duration` and
optional `t` timestamp attributes into `DefaultDisappearingModeUpdate`, exposes
a stored `DefaultDisappearingMode` account-state record with its own native
store namespace, and `wa-client` persists the latest receive-side value

The link-code companion registration slice added after the server-sync
notification work wires the next Baileys `messages-recv.ts` notification
branch into the Rust pairing flow. Core now parses
`link_code_companion_reg` notifications, unwraps the primary ephemeral key with
the stored pairing code, derives the companion key-bundle encryption key and
new adv secret, builds the `companion_finish` IQ, and returns updated
registered credentials. `wa-client` exposes an explicit mutable
`respond_to_link_code_companion_reg_notification` API that queries the finish
IQ, persists the updated credentials, and emits `CredentialsUpdated`. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core link_code --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features link_code --quiet`

- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`

The privacy-token notification storage slice added after the link-code
companion registration work wires Baileys-style `privacy_token` notifications
into the Rust tctoken store. Core now exposes a sender-LID parser that only
accepts normalized LID JIDs, plus notification-specific trusted-contact token
record parsing/storage that reuses the existing stale-token and
sender-timestamp preservation rules. `wa-client` resolves the notification
storage JID as `sender_lid` first, then PN-to-LID fallback from `from`, exposes
`handle_privacy_token_notification`, and invokes it from all direct and spawned
incoming-node processors. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core privacy_token --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features privacy_token --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise handle_privacy_token_notification_prefers_sender_lid --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_stores_privacy_token_notification --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise handle_privacy_token_notification_prefers_sender_lid --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_stores_privacy_token_notification --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-core --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-core --no-default-features --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`

The ACK 463 tctoken recovery slice added after the privacy-token notification
storage work wires the Baileys missing-privacy-token/account-restriction ACK
side effect into Rust receive processing. `ClientConfig` now has a
`lid_trusted_token_issue_to_lid` flag matching the upstream server-prop switch
with a conservative default of `false`; `wa-client` parses message ACKs with
error 463, resolves storage and issuance JIDs through the existing PN/LID
tctoken helpers, skips self/non-regular targets, coalesces duplicate in-flight
issuance through the existing bounded tctoken issuance set, and invokes the
handler from all direct and spawned incoming-node processors. ACK recovery
coverage now also pins the cross-source case where an already pending
post-send issuance for the mapped storage JID causes recovery to report
`scheduled: false` without emitting a duplicate privacy IQ. It was verified in
the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_ack_463_schedules_tc_token_recovery --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_ack_463_schedules_tc_token_recovery --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-core --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-core --no-default-features --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The offline-wrapper side-effect routing slice added after ACK 463 recovery
keeps Baileys-style receive side effects active for child stanzas inside local
`<offline>` batches. `wa-client` now centralizes incoming side-effect handling
for ACK 463 tctoken recovery, server-sync resync, privacy-token storage,
device-list session cleanup, and identity-change refresh, expanding offline
wrappers to their child nodes before invoking those handlers. This prevents
offline ACK/notification batches from only being parsed for normal events while
missing their storage/session/network side effects. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_ack_463_schedules_tc_token_recovery --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise offline_wrapper_child_ack_463_schedules_tc_token_recovery --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_ack_463_schedules_tc_token_recovery --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise offline_wrapper_child_ack_463_schedules_tc_token_recovery --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-core --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-core --no-default-features --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --all-targets -- -D warnings`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`

The call-event metadata enrichment slice added after offline-wrapper
side-effect routing brings receive-side call parsing closer to Baileys'
`handleCall` behavior without changing the stored call-event record layout.
Core now synthesizes stable `CallEvent` fields for offline calls, call origin
(`from`/`call-creator`), caller PN, offer video/group flags, group JID, and
normalized relay latency while preserving the existing raw attribute and child
metadata fields. It was verified in the current VM with:

The call-offer metadata inheritance slice added after call-event metadata
enrichment mirrors Baileys `handleCall` offer-cache behavior from
`src/Socket/messages-recv.ts`. The client now caches persisted call `offer`
events under a call-id key, enriches later call updates with the offer's
`is_video`, `is_group`, and `caller_pn` fields before persistence/emission, and
clears that cache on terminal call events (`reject`, `accept`, `timeout`, or
`terminate`). It covers both store-backed later updates and same-batch
offer/terminal updates. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise receive_persistence_inherits_call_offer_metadata_for_later_terminal_event --quiet`

The mediaretry notification receive slice added after call-offer metadata
inheritance wires the upstream `messages-recv.ts` `notification
type="mediaretry"` branch into Rust's existing media-retry event pipeline.
`parse_media_retry_update` now accepts both receipt and notification nodes that
carry `rmr` retry metadata, encrypted retry payloads, or retry errors, and the
notification router emits the same typed `MediaRetryEvent` batch used by
receipt-driven `rmr` updates. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core parses_media_retry_notification_update --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core processes_media_retry_notification_to_retry_event_and_ack --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features parses_media_retry_notification_update --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features processes_media_retry_notification_to_retry_event_and_ack --quiet`

- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --all-targets -- -D warnings`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`

- `git diff --check`

The call-derived message upsert slice added after mediaretry notification
receive mirrors Baileys' post-`call` event listener in `messages-recv.ts`.
Core now maps enriched call updates into message-upsert events for missed calls
and group-call offers: `timeout` events select the correct missed voice/video
and individual/group stub type, while group `offer` events carry a proto
`Message.call.call_key` payload using the call id. The client appends those
derived message events only after store-backed call-offer metadata inheritance,
so later timeout updates without raw video/group flags still persist and emit
the correct missed-call message. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core maps_call_events_to_missed_and_group_call_message_events --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features maps_call_events_to_missed_and_group_call_message_events --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise receive_persistence_generates_missed_call_message_from_enriched_call_timeout --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise receive_persistence_generates_missed_call_message_from_enriched_call_timeout --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `cargo check -p wa-core --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-core --no-default-features --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The call rejection API parity slice added after call-derived message upserts
mirrors Baileys' `rejectCall(callId, callFrom)` helper in
`src/Socket/messages-recv.ts`. Core now builds and validates the outbound
`<call><reject/></call>` stanza with `from`, `to`, `call-id`,
`call-creator`, and `count="0"` attributes. The client exposes
`reject_call`, sourcing the local account JID from credentials and sending the
stanza through `query_node` so it receives a server result. It was verified in
the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core builds_call_reject_node --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features builds_call_reject_node --quiet`

- `cargo check -p wa-core --no-default-features --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --all-targets -- -D warnings`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The group-notification derived message-stub slice added after call rejection
mirrors Baileys' `w:gp2` notification path that produces user-visible group
stub messages. Core now converts typed `GroupUpdateEvent`s with stable
notification ids into `messages.upsert` events for canonical group stub cases:
create, subject, description, icon, invite link, announce/restrict settings,
participant add/remove/leave/promote/demote/change-number, member-add mode, and
join-approval mode. Participant stubs preserve Baileys-style JSON parameter
strings with LID/PN/username/admin metadata when present. The client now appends
these group-derived message events through the same de-duplicated derived-event
path used for call-derived messages, including direct `GroupsUpdate`
persistence. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core maps_group_updates_to_group_notification_message_stubs --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features maps_group_updates_to_group_notification_message_stubs --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`

The group-notification ephemeral-setting payload slice added after the derived
message-stub foundation closes the payload-bearing branch of Baileys'
`ephemeral`/`not_ephemeral` `w:gp2` handling. Derived group notification
message stubs now emit an encoded `Message.protocol_message` payload with
`ProtocolMessage.Type.EPHEMERAL_SETTING` and the received
`ephemeralExpiration`, while still carrying the stable notification id, actor,
stub type, readable stub name, and stub parameters. The receive persistence test
now verifies the payload round-trips through the native message store. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core maps_group_updates_to_group_notification_message_stubs --quiet`

The group-notification membership-request stub slice added after the
ephemeral-setting payload work closes the remaining Baileys
`created_membership_requests`/`revoked_membership_requests` branches that emit
`GROUP_MEMBERSHIP_JOIN_APPROVAL_REQUEST_NON_ADMIN_ADD` user-visible stubs. Core
now derives these message upserts from parsed `GroupUpdateEvent` lifecycle
fields, preserving Baileys' `{ lid, pn }` participant JSON parameter shape,
`created` plus request-method parameters, and `revoked` versus `rejected`
outcomes. The client persistence regression now verifies that these derived
membership-request stubs round-trip through the native message store alongside
the existing group notification stubs. It was verified in the current VM with:
