- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The Signal provider-session-backed pre-key replay fallback slice added after the
signed-pre-key-only failed-decrypt coverage moves one-to-one pre-key replay
fallback before local key-material loading. Consumed one-time-pre-key replays and
signed-pre-key-only replays now decrypt their inner whisper messages through the
stored provider session first, allowing stable duplicate-counter rejection from
provider session/identity state even when local credential material is missing.
Fresh pre-key decrypts still require local key material before bootstrapping a
new session. The store-backed one-time and signed-pre-key-only replay
regressions now delete the credential schema marker after the session exists,
verify the replay is rejected without mutating the stored provider session, and
then restore credentials for the remaining fresh-decrypt assertions. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_inbound_pre_key_then_session_messages --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_inbound_pre_key_without_one_time_pre_key --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_inbound_pre_key_then_session_messages --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_inbound_pre_key_without_one_time_pre_key --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-core --features noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The Signal orphaned provider-session pre-key replay guard slice added after the
credential-material-independent replay fallback coverage tightens the same
direct decrypt path. If a consumed one-time-pre-key replay or signed-pre-key-only
replay finds a stored provider session but the paired provider identity record is
missing, the provider now rejects with the existing `no provider identity`
diagnostic instead of falling through to a fresh pre-key bootstrap or a
misleading missing-pre-key error. The store-backed replay regressions now delete
only the provider identity key, verify the replay rejection, confirm the stored
provider session bytes are unchanged, and then restore the identity for the
remaining flow. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_inbound_pre_key_then_session_messages --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_inbound_pre_key_without_one_time_pre_key --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_inbound_pre_key_then_session_messages --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_inbound_pre_key_without_one_time_pre_key --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-core --features noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The Signal active-chain future failed-decrypt preservation slice added after the
orphaned provider-session replay guard broadens one-to-one ratchet hardening.
The store-backed failed message decrypt regression now tampers a future
active-chain message that would derive a skipped key before authentication,
proves the persisted provider session remains unchanged after the failed
decrypt, then decrypts the valid future message, consumes the late skipped
message, and continues with the next current-chain message. The
`signal_stateful_records` fuzz target now drives the same structured
future-counter active-chain path instead of only the next-counter path. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`

The group sender-key distribution own PN/LID linked-device dedupe slice added
after the deterministic Signal provider-session future failed-decrypt fixture
coverage hardens the high-level group sender-key send path. The new client
regression fetches group metadata, queries both the local PN and LID account
aliases for linked devices, feeds overlapping USync device results for the same
local companion, and proves the distribution relay emits one device-sent wrapper
for the linked own device while preserving the ordinary remote participant
distribution. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_group_sender_key_distribution_deduplicates_own_pn_lid_linked_device_aliases --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_group_sender_key_distribution_normalizes_legacy_c_us_participant_aliases --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_group_sender_key_distribution_deduplicates_own_pn_lid_linked_device_aliases --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The ignored live status/broadcast e2e smoke slice added after the group
sender-key distribution own-alias dedupe coverage broadens opt-in live
validation. `wa-client`'s `live_e2e` target now accepts comma-separated
`WA_STATUS_JIDS` plus optional `WA_STATUS_TEXT` and exercises
`send_status_text_with_signal_provider` against a paired session when
`WA_LIVE_E2E` is enabled, while still compiling and skipping without live
credentials in normal CI. The README now documents the new live status smoke
environment variables. It was verified in the current VM without live
credentials with:

- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --test live_e2e --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features http-media --test live_e2e --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,rustls --test live_e2e --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --test live_e2e --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --test live_e2e --features http-media -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,rustls --test live_e2e -- -D warnings`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The ignored live group sender-key e2e smoke slice added after the status
live-smoke coverage broadens opt-in live validation around the group send path.
`wa-client`'s `live_e2e` target now accepts `WA_GROUP_SEND_JID` plus optional
`WA_GROUP_TEXT` and exercises `send_group_sender_key_text_with_signal_provider`
against a paired session when `WA_LIVE_E2E` is enabled, while keeping the
existing metadata-only `WA_GROUP_JID` check non-sending. The README now
documents the separate send variable so group metadata smoke runs cannot
accidentally emit group messages. It was verified in the current VM without
live credentials with:

- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --test live_e2e --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,rustls --test live_e2e -- -D warnings`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The public live status example slice added after the group sender-key live-smoke
coverage broadens the documented example surface for status/broadcast sends.
`wa-client` now registers a `live_status` example that reads comma-separated
`WA_STATUS_JIDS`, optional `WA_STATUS_TEXT`, and the shared `WA_SESSION_DB`,
then sends a text status through `send_status_text_with_signal_provider` only
when the required audience variable is set. The README example list now includes
the new command and links the same environment variables used by the ignored
live status e2e smoke. It was verified in the current VM without live

The public live group sender-key example slice added after the live status
example coverage broadens the documented group send surface. `wa-client` now
registers a separate `live_group_send` example that reads `WA_GROUP_SEND_JID`,
optional `WA_GROUP_TEXT`, and the shared `WA_SESSION_DB`, then sends a group
text message through `send_group_sender_key_text_with_signal_provider` only
when the explicit send JID is set. The README example list now includes the new
command and links the same environment variables used by the ignored group

The deterministic Signal provider-session active-chain skipped-key replay
fixture slice added after the public live group sender-key example coverage
extends the checked-in `provider_session_out_of_order` vector. The fixture now
decrypts the second active-chain message first to create a skipped key, consumes
the first message from that skipped-key cache, rejects an exact replay of the
consumed skipped message with the stable duplicate-counter error, and then
decrypts a third current-chain message while pinning the new wire message and
advanced provider-session records. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures signal_fixtures_match_public_signal_primitives --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-testkit --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-testkit --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The public live link-preview thumbnail example and ignored live smoke slice
added after the deterministic active-chain skipped-key replay fixture coverage
broadens live media validation. `wa-client` now registers a `live_link_preview`
example behind `http-media,link-preview,image` that reads `WA_LINK_PREVIEW_URL`,
optional `WA_LINK_PREVIEW_TEXT`, and the shared `WA_TARGET_JID`/`WA_SESSION_DB`,
fetches preview metadata and the preview image, generates and uploads the
high-quality thumbnail, attaches both inline and remote thumbnail metadata to a
text message, and sends it through `send_message_with_signal_provider`. The
ignored `live_e2e` target now has the same opt-in send smoke, and the README
documents the command and environment variables. It was verified in the current
VM without live credentials with:

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --examples --features http-media,link-preview,image -- -D warnings`

The public live generated video remote-thumbnail example and ignored live smoke
slice added after generated link-preview thumbnail live validation broadens

example behind `http-media,image` that reads `WA_VIDEO_PATH`, optional
`WA_VIDEO_MIMETYPE`/`WA_VIDEO_CAPTION`, and optional
`WA_VIDEO_THUMBNAIL_FFMPEG`/`WA_VIDEO_THUMBNAIL_SEEK_TIME` overrides, uploads
the parent video media, extracts a JPEG thumbnail through the configured ffmpeg

The video/document persisted media retry handoff regression slice added after
generated profile-picture live validation broadens stored media retry replay
beyond image-shaped pending descriptors. The `wa-client` regression persists
video and document pending-media descriptors plus stored retry events, rebuilds
a fresh client, downloads both refreshed direct paths through
`handle_stored_media_retry_events`, and verifies that the native pending and
retry-event records are deleted only after successful downloads. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise stored_media_retry_events_replay_video_and_document_pending_media --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise stored_media_retry_events_replay_video_and_document_pending_media --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The SQLite-backed media retry replay slice added after video/document persisted
handoff validates the same native-store semantics against the real application
store instead of only `MemoryAuthStore`. The `wa-client` regression persists a
successful video retry and a failed document retry into a temp SQLite session
database, reopens the store, verifies the video retry downloads and deletes its
pending/retry records while the failed document retry remains queued, overwrites
that queued retry with a later successful document retry, reopens the store
again, and verifies the delayed success downloads and cleans both records. It
was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,bundled-sqlite,noise sqlite_stored_media_retry_events_replay_after_reopen_and_retry_update --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client sqlite_stored_media_retry_events_replay_after_reopen_and_retry_update --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise stored_media_retry_events_replay_video_and_document_pending_media --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,bundled-sqlite,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,bundled-sqlite,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The SQLite stored media retry pagination slice added after real-store
failed-to-success replay validates the `handle_stored_media_retry_events`
cursor path across a native-store page boundary. The `wa-client` regression
persists `MEDIA_RETRY_EVENT_STORE_PAGE_SIZE + 3` successful document retry
events and matching pending descriptors into a temp SQLite session database,
reopens the store, proves a second media-retry page exists before replay,
downloads all retry media in one replay pass, and verifies both the
`MediaRetryEvent` and `PendingMediaRetry` namespaces are fully drained. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,bundled-sqlite,noise sqlite_stored_media_retry_events_replay_paginates_after_reopen --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client sqlite_stored_media_retry_events_replay_paginates_after_reopen --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,bundled-sqlite,noise sqlite_stored_media_retry_events_replay_after_reopen_and_retry_update --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,bundled-sqlite,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,bundled-sqlite,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The malformed stored media retry event cleanup slice added after SQLite
pagination hardens restart replay against corrupt native-store event rows.
`MediaRetryBatchOutcome` now reports `malformed_stored_records`, and
`handle_stored_media_retry_events` deletes malformed stored `MediaRetryEvent`
records while continuing to stage and download valid retry events from the same
SQLite namespace. The `wa-client` regression seeds one corrupt retry-event row
beside a valid persisted retry, reopens the SQLite store, verifies the valid
retry downloads and cleans its pending/event records, and verifies the corrupt
row is deleted and counted. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,bundled-sqlite,noise sqlite_stored_media_retry_events_delete_malformed_record_and_continue --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,bundled-sqlite,noise sqlite_stored_media_retry_events_replay_paginates_after_reopen --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise media_retry --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client sqlite_stored_media_retry_events_delete_malformed_record_and_continue --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,bundled-sqlite,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-core --features noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,bundled-sqlite,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The malformed stored pending media retry cleanup slice added after malformed
event cleanup hardens persisted staging against corrupt native-store pending
descriptors. The persisted media retry staging path now deletes malformed stored
pending descriptors, counts them through `MediaRetryBatchOutcome`, and continues
processing valid retry events from the same replay batch. The `wa-client`
regression seeds a valid persisted retry beside a retry whose
`PendingMediaRetry` row is corrupt, reopens SQLite, verifies the valid retry
downloads and cleans its pending/event records, verifies the corrupt pending row
is deleted and counted, and verifies the retry event itself remains queued for a
future re-registered pending descriptor. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,bundled-sqlite,noise sqlite_stored_media_retry_events_delete_malformed_pending_and_continue --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,bundled-sqlite,noise sqlite_stored_media_retry_events_delete_malformed_record_and_continue --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,bundled-sqlite,noise sqlite_stored_media_retry_events_replay_paginates_after_reopen --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise media_retry --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client sqlite_stored_media_retry_events_delete_malformed_pending_and_continue --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,bundled-sqlite,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-core --features noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,bundled-sqlite,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The file-backed e2e-sized media transfer fixture slice added after live media
retry request validation broadens large-media coverage beyond the existing
in-memory recording transport. `wa-core` now has a test transport that consumes
encrypted upload temp files chunk by chunk, stores ciphertext on disk, overrides
`download_media_to_file` to stream ciphertext back to the transfer path without
allocating it as `Bytes`, and verifies a 16-plus chunk video fixture round-trips
through upload, download-to-file, and streamed decrypt with bounded chunk sizes.
It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise media_transfer_streams_e2e_sized_file_without_in_memory_ciphertext_transport --quiet`

- `cargo check -p wa-core --features noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The Signal active-chain skipped-key failed-decrypt preservation slice added
after the file-backed media transfer fixture strengthens the store-backed
one-to-one provider regression. After a future active-chain message is
decrypted and the late message key is persisted as a skipped key, the regression
now tampers that late message, verifies decrypt rejection without mutating the
stored provider-session bytes, and then decrypts the original skipped message
before continuing the existing current-chain assertions. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_preserves_session_after_failed_message_decrypt --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_preserves_session_after_failed_message_decrypt --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-core --features noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The deterministic Signal provider-session active-chain skipped-key
failed-decrypt fixture slice added after the store-backed skipped-key
failed-decrypt preservation coverage promotes the same edge into the checked-in
compatibility manifest. The `provider_session_out_of_order` vector now pins a
tampered skipped-message wire frame, verifies decrypt rejection from the record
that contains the skipped key, then decrypts the original skipped message and
continues through the existing replay and next-message assertions. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures signal_fixtures_match_public_signal_primitives --quiet`

The deterministic Signal pre-key provider-session replay fixture slice added
after the active-chain skipped-key fixture coverage broadens checked-in
one-to-one replay parity. The one-time and signed-pre-key-only pre-key session
vectors now decrypt the pre-key envelope, then replay the inner whisper message
against the resulting provider-session record and assert the stable duplicate
counter rejection before continuing through the pinned sender/receiver record
assertions. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures signal_fixtures_match_public_signal_primitives --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_inbound_pre_key_then_session_messages --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_inbound_pre_key_without_one_time_pre_key --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_inbound_pre_key_then_session_messages --quiet`

- `cargo check -p wa-core --features noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`

The placeholder resend pending-batch rollback slice added after the deterministic
pre-key replay fixture coverage hardens manual peer-data operation request
tracking. The client regression now pre-marks one placeholder message id as
pending, requests a two-key batch where an earlier key is fresh and the later
key is already pending, verifies the request is rejected without writing any
stanza, proves the fresh id was rolled back from the pending tracker, and
confirms the original pending id remains tracked. It was verified in the current
VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise request_placeholder_resend_rolls_back_batch_when_later_key_is_pending --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise request_placeholder_resend_sends_peer_data_operation_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise request_placeholder_resend_rolls_back_batch_when_later_key_is_pending --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The placeholder resend relay-failure rollback slice added after pending-batch
rollback coverage pins the other pending-tracker cleanup path. A new client
regression drives manual placeholder resend through USync/device discovery and
session assertion with a failing message encryptor, verifies the relay returns
the encryptor error, confirms the message id is removed from the pending
placeholder tracker, and proves no message stanza was emitted. It was verified
in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise request_placeholder_resend_rolls_back_tracker_when_relay_fails --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`

- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The placeholder resend late-participant relay-failure rollback slice added
after the first relay-failure rollback coverage pins the same pending-tracker
cleanup path when direct fanout has already encrypted an earlier participant.
A new client regression seeds own-device sessions, returns both the account's
primary and linked device from USync, lets the first participant encryption
succeed, fails the linked-device encryption, verifies the encryptor error
propagates, proves the placeholder id is removed from the pending tracker, and
confirms no partial peer-data-operation stanza was emitted. It was verified in
the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise request_placeholder_resend_rolls_back_tracker_when_later_participant_encrypt_fails --quiet`

- `git diff --check`

The group retry sender-key redistribution failure slice added after placeholder
relay rollback coverage pins the cached-message finalization boundary for group
retry retries. A new client regression drives a group retry through
requester-device discovery with a failing message encryptor, verifies the
sender-key redistribution error is returned, proves no cached replay stanza is
emitted, confirms retry success/failure counters are not finalized, and
re-prepares the same retry plan to show the cached message remains eligible for
a later attempt. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise handle_group_retry_receipt_keeps_cached_message_when_sender_key_redistribution_fails --quiet`

- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`

The post-send tctoken duplicate in-flight coalescing slice added after group
retry redistribution failure coverage pins the scheduler boundary for repeated
eligible one-to-one sends. A new client regression sends two messages to the
same target while the first spawned trusted-contact privacy IQ is deliberately
left unanswered, proves the second send emits only its message stanza and no
second privacy IQ, then completes the first privacy result and verifies the
token plus sender timestamp are persisted. It was verified in the current VM
with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise post_send_tc_token_issuance_coalesces_duplicate_in_flight_targets --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`

The spawned tctoken issue failure cleanup slice added after duplicate in-flight
coalescing coverage pins the scheduler cleanup path for failed fire-and-forget
trusted-contact issuance. A new client regression schedules a tctoken issue,
proves a duplicate schedule is coalesced while the first privacy IQ is in
flight, returns an error IQ, verifies the in-flight target is cleared without
storing a token, then schedules the same target again and confirms the retry
privacy result persists the token and sender timestamp. It was verified in the
current VM with:

The identity-change tctoken cross-source coalescing slice added after spawned
issue failure cleanup coverage pins interaction between post-send issuance and
identity-change reissue. A new client regression leaves a post-send privacy IQ
in flight for a target with a recent sender marker, processes an identity-change
notification for the same target, verifies the Signal identity refresh still
runs, asserts the outcome reports no new token reissue schedule, proves no
duplicate privacy IQ is emitted, and then completes the original privacy result
to confirm the token is persisted. It was verified in the current VM with:

The ACK 463 tctoken cross-source coalescing slice added after identity-change
coalescing coverage pins the same scheduler boundary for receive-side recovery.
A new client regression leaves a post-send trusted-contact privacy IQ in flight
for the LID-mapped storage JID, handles a Baileys account-restriction ACK from
the PN, verifies recovery resolves the same storage/issue JIDs but reports

original privacy result to confirm the token persists. It was verified in the

derivation, matching the 32-byte secret invariant enforced by poll/event message
builders, and a focused core regression pins the payload error. It was verified

The reporting-token message-key direction coverage slice added after malformed
message-secret validation pins the JID material currently used for HKDF info. A

participant-bearing group keys derive distinct outgoing/incoming tokens and

The post-send tctoken sender-marker freshness slice added after scheduler-gate
coverage pins the client planner's native-store bucket behavior. A new client
regression proves a target with no stored sender marker schedules a post-send

sender marker from an older bucket schedules again while preserving the resolved

The status/broadcast no-recipient-device coverage slice added after the latest
tctoken orchestration coverage pins an early status fanout failure boundary. A
new client regression drives status device discovery through a USync result that
contains the requested audience and own account but no usable device lists,
verifies the send fails with the existing `status send requires at least one
recipient device` protocol error, and proves no session query, message stanza,
or encryptor call follows the empty recipient set. It was verified in the
current VM with:

The group sender-key no-recipient-device coverage slice added after status
no-recipient-device coverage pins the analogous high-level group distribution
failure boundary. A new client regression fetches group metadata successfully,
then returns a USync device result with the group participant and own account but
no usable device lists, verifies the send fails with the existing
`group sender-key distribution requires at least one recipient device` protocol
error, and proves no session query, distribution stanza, group stanza, or
encryptor call follows the empty recipient set. It was verified in the current
VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_group_sender_key_distribution_fails_when_device_lookup_has_no_recipients --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_group_sender_key_distribution_fails_when_device_lookup_has_no_recipients --quiet`

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_group_sender_key_distribution_deduplicates_own_pn_lid_linked_device_aliases --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The status/broadcast session-assertion failure slice added after group
sender-key no-recipient-device coverage pins the next status fanout failure
boundary. A new client regression drives status device discovery to a valid
audience device, returns an encrypt-session assertion error, verifies the send
propagates the existing `E2E session query failed (401): session denied`
failure, and proves no status message stanza or encryptor call is emitted after
the assertion fails. It was verified in the current VM with:

The high-level direct-send fanout failure slice added after group sender-key
session-assertion coverage pins the one-to-one counterpart to the group/status
failure boundaries. The client now rejects an empty discovered recipient set
before session assertion, relay option preparation, ciphertext construction, or
post-send tctoken planning. New regressions cover the no-recipient-device
failure and a valid-device encrypt-session assertion error, verifying the
existing `E2E session query failed (401): session denied` failure propagates
without emitting a message stanza or encryptor call. It was verified in the
current VM with:

The retry all-device resend failure slice added after high-level direct-send
fanout failure coverage pins the prepared-retry replay boundary for requester
device rediscovery. `retry_resend_recipients` now rejects an empty all-device
recipient set before session assertion, retry relay option preparation,
ciphertext construction, or retry-success finalization. New regressions cover
empty requester-device discovery and a valid-device encrypt-session assertion

failure propagates without emitting a retry message stanza, encryptor call, or
successful-retry counter increment. It was verified in the current VM with:

The placeholder resend no-recipient rollback slice added after retry
all-device resend failure coverage pins the pending-tracker cleanup path for
the new high-level direct-send empty-recipient guard. A new client regression
registers a manual placeholder resend request, drives own-device discovery to a
USync result with no usable recipient devices, verifies the direct-send
`message send requires at least one recipient device` failure is returned, and
proves the placeholder id is removed from the pending tracker without emitting
a session query, message stanza, or encryptor call. It was verified in the

The retry participant-target relay-failure slice added after participant
session-assertion failure coverage pins the post-session prepared-replay
boundary. A new client regression prepares a cached participant retry job,
executes it with a failing message encryptor after session assertion succeeds,
verifies the encryptor error is returned, proves no retry message stanza or
success/failure counter finalization occurs, and re-prepares the same retry plan
to show the cached message remains eligible for a later attempt. It was verified
in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise execute_retry_resends_participant_keeps_cached_message_when_relay_fails --quiet`

- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The retry participant-target multi-message replay finalization slice added
after participant relay-failure coverage pins the single-device counterpart to
the all-device and group multi-message boundaries. A new client regression
prepares two cached participant retry jobs, sends the first replay to the
requesting device, then fails the second replay during encryption before any
second stanza is emitted. It records exactly one successful retry with no failed
retry count, and proves the first message is removed from the retry cache while
the failed second message remains preparable. It was verified in the current VM
with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise execute_retry_resends_finalizes_first_participant_replay_when_second_replay_encrypt_fails --quiet`

- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The retry participant-target multi-message session-assertion finalization slice
added after participant multi-message encryption-failure coverage pins the
single-device pre-encryption failure after an earlier cached replay has already
been sent. A new client regression prepares two cached participant retry jobs,
sends the first replay to the requesting device, deletes that local session
through a test encryptor after the first relay encryption completes, then lets
the second replay fail the encrypt-session query with the existing
`session denied` error. It verifies no second replay stanza or second replay
encryption attempt is emitted, records exactly one successful retry with no
failed retry count, and proves the first message is removed from the retry cache
while the failed second message remains preparable. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise execute_retry_resends_finalizes_first_participant_replay_when_second_session_assertion_fails --quiet`

- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`

The retry participant-target multi-message relay-send finalization slice added
after participant session-assertion finalization coverage pins the socket-send
boundary after a later replay payload has already been encrypted. A new client
regression prepares two cached participant retry jobs, sends the first replay to
the requesting device, encrypts the second replay, closes the mock connection
from a test encryptor before the second stanza can be enqueued, and verifies the
existing `ConnectionClosed` error is returned. It proves no second replay stanza
is emitted, records exactly one successful retry with no failed retry count, and
keeps only the failed second message preparable after the first message is
removed from the retry cache. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`

- `git diff --check`

The group retry multi-message relay-send finalization slice added after group
retry multi-message replay finalization coverage pins the socket-send boundary
after a later cached group replay has already been encrypted for every
redistribution recipient. A new client regression sends the sender-key
redistribution stanza and first cached group replay, encrypts both second replay
participant payloads, closes the mock connection from a test encryptor before
the second replay stanza can be enqueued, verifies the existing
`ConnectionClosed` error is returned, records exactly one successful retry with
no failed retry count, and proves only the first message is removed from the
retry cache while the failed second message remains preparable. It was verified
in the current VM with:

The retry all-device late-participant relay-failure slice added after the first
all-device relay-failure coverage pins the partial fanout boundary for cached
retry replays. A new client regression prepares a cached all-device retry,
rediscovers the requester device plus the sender's linked device, lets the
requester-device encryption succeed, fails linked-device encryption, verifies
no partial replay stanza is emitted, keeps retry success/failure counters
unchanged, and proves the cached retry job remains preparable for a later
attempt. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise execute_retry_resends_all_devices_keeps_cached_message_when_later_participant_encrypt_fails --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The retry all-device multi-message replay finalization slice added after
late-participant all-device replay failure coverage pins the direct retry
counterpart to the group retry multi-message boundary. A new client regression
prepares two cached all-device retry jobs, sends the first replay to the
requester and the sender's linked device, then fails the second replay during
linked-device encryption. It verifies no partial second replay stanza is
emitted, records exactly one successful retry with no failed retry count, and
proves the first message is removed from the retry cache while the failed
second message remains preparable. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise execute_retry_resends_finalizes_first_all_device_replay_when_second_replay_encrypt_fails --quiet`

- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The retry all-device multi-message no-recipient finalization slice added after
all-device multi-message replay finalization coverage pins a pre-encryption
failure after an earlier cached replay has already been sent. A new client
regression prepares two cached all-device retry jobs, sends the first replay to
the requester and the sender's linked device, then returns an empty recipient
set for the second replay's device rediscovery. It verifies no second replay
stanza or second encryption attempt is emitted, records exactly one successful
retry with no failed retry count, and proves the first message is removed from
the retry cache while the failed second message remains preparable. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise execute_retry_resends_finalizes_first_all_device_replay_when_second_device_lookup_is_empty --quiet`

The retry all-device multi-message session-assertion finalization slice added
after no-recipient finalization coverage pins the next pre-encryption failure
after an earlier cached replay has already been sent. A new client regression
prepares two cached all-device retry jobs, sends the first replay to the
requester and the sender's linked device, deletes those local sessions through a
test encryptor after the first relay encryptions complete, then lets the second
replay rediscover the same devices and fail the encrypt-session query with the
existing `session denied` error. It verifies no second replay stanza or second
replay encryption attempt is emitted, records exactly one successful retry with
no failed retry count, and proves the first message is removed from the retry
cache while the failed second message remains preparable. It was verified in
the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise execute_retry_resends_finalizes_first_all_device_replay_when_second_session_assertion_fails --quiet`

- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`

- `git diff --check`

The retry all-device multi-message relay-send finalization slice added after
all-device session-assertion finalization coverage pins the socket-send boundary
after a later all-device replay payload has already been encrypted for every
recipient. A new client regression prepares two cached all-device retry jobs,
sends the first replay to the requester and the sender's linked device,
rediscovers the same recipients for the second replay, encrypts both second
replay participant payloads, closes the mock connection from a test encryptor
before the second stanza can be enqueued, and verifies the existing
`ConnectionClosed` error is returned. It proves no second replay stanza is
emitted, records exactly one successful retry with no failed retry count, and
keeps only the failed second message preparable after the first message is
removed from the retry cache. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise execute_retry_resends_finalizes_first_all_device_replay_when_second_relay_send_fails --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise execute_retry_resends_finalizes_first_all_device_replay_when_second_relay_send_fails --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The store-backed Signal sender-key invalid-signature no-rollback slice added
after retry relay-failure coverage broadens group sender-key cryptographic
parity evidence. A new `wa-core` regression decrypts a later group sender-key
message first to persist a skipped sender-message key, then corrupts the skipped
message signature, verifies the invalid-signature error leaves the stored
sender-key record byte-for-byte unchanged, and finally decrypts the original
message to prove the skipped key was not consumed by the failed authentication
attempt. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_preserves_sender_key_after_failed_group_signature --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_preserves_sender_key_after_failed_group_signature --quiet`

- `cargo check -p wa-core --features noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The deterministic Signal sender-key invalid-signature no-rollback fixture slice
added after the store-backed regression promotes that group sender-key edge case
into the public `wa-testkit` Signal manifest. The existing out-of-order
sender-key vector now carries a deterministic invalid-signature skipped-message
frame, the expected invalid-signature error, and the receiver record bytes after
the failed authentication attempt. The fixture harness verifies the failed
signature does not change the skipped-key record and that the original skipped
message still decrypts afterward. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures signal_fixtures_match_public_signal_primitives --quiet`

The Signal stateful-record sender-key invalid-signature fuzz slice added after
the deterministic fixture work extends `signal_stateful_records` with fuzz
pressure for the same no-rollback class. The structured out-of-order sender-key
path now decrypts a later group payload to persist skipped sender-message keys,
tries an invalid-signature skipped message and checks the exact authentication
error, then decrypts the original skipped message and verifies consumed-key
replay rejection. This complements the store-backed regression and deterministic
fixture with generated stateful coverage around failed authentication before
skipped-key consumption. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/fuzz-check cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin signal_stateful_records --quiet`

- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_preserves_sender_key_after_failed_group_signature --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The ACK 463 mapped-self tctoken recovery slice added after the Signal fuzz work
closes the self-target gap for receive-side privacy-token recovery. A new client
regression stores a PN->LID mapping where the ACK sender's PN resolves to the
client's own LID, handles a Baileys account-restriction ACK, verifies recovery
returns no issue plan, and proves no privacy-token IQ is emitted. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise ack_463_tc_token_recovery_skips_mapped_self_targets_without_query --quiet`

- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`

The delayed event-response creation-secret merge slice added after the ACK 463
mapped-self tctoken recovery work closes the symmetric pending RSVP path. A new
client regression receives an encrypted event response before its event creation
message exists, verifies the pending update is stored without decrypted fields,
then persists the event creation message and proves the pending update is merged
into the stored event with decrypted response, timestamp, extra-guest, and
creator metadata while the pending update row is removed. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise late_event_creation_merges_and_decrypts_pending_event_response --quiet`

The mapped-self privacy-token notification slice added after the delayed
event-response merge work extends the self-target tctoken guard to receive-side
token storage. A new client regression stores a PN->LID mapping where an
incoming privacy-token notification's PN sender resolves to the client's own
LID, verifies the handler returns no storage outcome, and proves neither the own
LID nor the sender PN gets a trusted-contact token record. It was verified in
the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise handle_privacy_token_notification_skips_mapped_self_sender --quiet`

The reporting-token future-proof wrapper slice added after mapped-self
privacy-token notification handling fixes wrapper-only sidecar generation for
`FutureProofMessage` payloads. The reporting allowlist now treats wrapped child
field `1` as another full message, so view-once/ephemeral-style wrappers include
their inner allowlisted content instead of dropping to an empty token payload.
A focused core regression proves wrapped text produces a 16-byte reporting token
and that changing the wrapped content changes the token. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core reporting_token_includes_future_proof_wrapped_message_content --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core reporting --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features reporting --quiet` (no matching tests in that feature set)
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise high_level_relay_options_add_reporting_token_for_secret_messages --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-core --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-core --no-default-features --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The reporting-token wrapped-secret lookup slice added after future-proof wrapper
filtering makes token derivation follow the same bounded wrapper chain for
`message_context_info.message_secret`. Wrapped poll/event-style payloads whose
outer message only contains a `FutureProofMessage` now derive their reporting
token from the inner message secret, while malformed inner secrets still fail
with the existing 32-byte validation error. It was verified in the current VM
with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core reporting_token_includes_future_proof_wrapped_message_content --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The reporting-token allowlisted wrapper coverage slice added after wrapped
secret lookup aligns the secret traversal with the modern `FutureProofMessage`
fields already present in the reporting allowlist. Token derivation now unwraps
status mention, group status mention, lottie sticker, poll-v4, and other known
future-proof wrapper fields instead of only the older view-once/ephemeral
family. A focused core regression builds wrapper-only messages with inner-only
secrets for representative allowlisted fields and proves each produces a
16-byte reporting token. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core reporting_token_secret_lookup_covers_allowlisted_future_proof_wrappers --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core reporting_token_includes_future_proof_wrapped_message_content --quiet`

- `cargo check -p wa-core --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-core --no-default-features --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The modern future-proof stanza-type slice added after reporting-token wrapper
coverage aligns outbound stanza classification with the newer wrapper fields in
the generated proto. `message_stanza_type` now unwraps lottie sticker, status
mention, group status mention, poll-v4, and the other known `FutureProofMessage`
wrappers before deciding whether a payload is `media`, `poll`, `event`,
`reaction`, or `text`, preventing wrapper-only media/poll sends from being
classified as text. A focused core regression pins representative lottie,
status-mention, and poll-v4 wrappers. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core message_stanza_type_unwraps_modern_future_proof_wrappers --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core builds_view_once_future_proof_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core builds_poll_event_and_protocol_messages --quiet`

- `cargo check -p wa-core --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-core --no-default-features --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The wrapped poll/event creation-secret extraction slice added after modern
future-proof stanza classification closes the receive-side half of the same
wrapper class. The native-store poll/event secret extractor now walks the shared
future-proof wrapper chain before deciding whether a stored message event is a
poll/event creation and before reading `message_context_info.message_secret`, so
view-once/modern wrapped poll or event creations can seed later encrypted update
decryption. The core regression extends stored event extraction with a view-once
wrapped poll creation whose secret lives only on the inner message. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise extracts_poll_event_creation_secret_from_stored_message_event --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise extracts_poll_event_creation_secret_from_stored_message_event --quiet`

- `cargo check -p wa-core --features noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The wrapped poll/event update receive-mapping slice added after wrapped
creation-secret extraction carries the shared future-proof message view into
live and history update extraction. `message_updates_from_decoded_message` and
its poll/event decryption annotation now inspect the unwrapped content for
`poll_update_message` and `enc_event_response_message`, while history
`message_add_on` processing uses the same view for add-on fields and target-key
fallback. New core regressions cover a live view-once wrapped poll update and a
history add-on whose wrapped inner poll update supplies the target key. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise maps_decoded_poll_update_message_to_message_update_event --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise maps_decoded_poll_update_message_to_message_update_event --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`

The wrapped pending poll/event update delayed-merge slice added after live and
history receive mapping closes the stored-event enrichment path. The client
delayed merge helper now asks the core mapper for updates from the decoded
stored payload and keeps only poll/event update sources, instead of returning
early when the pending update is hidden inside a `FutureProofMessage` wrapper.
The late poll-creation regression now stores a view-once wrapped pending poll
update before the creation message arrives and proves the later merge decrypts
and removes the pending row. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise late_poll_creation_merges_and_decrypts_pending_poll_update --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise late_poll_creation_merges_and_decrypts_pending_poll_update --quiet`

- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The wrapped reaction/pin/revoke receive-mapping slice added after wrapped
pending poll/event delayed merge extends the same future-proof unwrapped
message view beyond poll/event updates. Live decoded reaction extraction,
pin update extraction, and revoke delete extraction now inspect the unwrapped
content, and history `message_add_ons` use that view for wrapped pin/reaction
field detection and fallback target-key extraction. New core regressions cover
view-once wrapped reaction, revoke, and pin payloads plus a history pin add-on
whose wrapped inner pin message supplies the target key. It was verified in
the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core maps_decoded_reaction_message_to_reaction_update_event --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core maps_decoded_protocol_messages_to_update_and_delete_events --quiet`

The deterministic Signal sender-key multi-state decrypt fixture slice added
after wrapped receive mapping promotes the existing later-matching-state sender
key behavior into the checked-in compatibility manifest. `wa-testkit` now
understands a `sender_key_record_multi_state_decrypt` vector that starts with a
receiver record containing replacement and preserved states with the same
sender-key id, rejects a tampered preserved-state message with the stable
invalid-signature error, signs a bad preserved-state ciphertext with the valid
old signing key and checks the stable crypto decrypt error, signs a far-future
preserved-state message and checks the stable future-jump protocol error,
decrypts a signed message from the preserved old state, proves only that old
state advances, rejects an immediate replay of that consumed old-state message
with the stable duplicate-iteration error, then decrypts a replacement-state
message from the resulting record. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures signal_fixtures_match_public_signal_primitives --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core signal_sender_key_record_decrypts_later_matching_key_id_state --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core signal_sender_key_record_processes_distribution_and_preserves_matching_state --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-testkit --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-testkit --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The Signal stateful-record sender-key multi-state decrypt fuzz slice added
after the deterministic multi-state fixture work extends `signal_stateful_records`
with generated replacement/preserved sender-key states that share a sender-key
id but use different signing keys. The structured path decrypts an old-state
message first, rejects a tampered old-state message with the stable
invalid-signature error before any state advancement, asserts the replacement
state remains unchanged while the valid old state advances, rejects an immediate
replay of the consumed old-state message with the stable duplicate-iteration
error, then decrypts a replacement-state message from the advanced record. It
now also signs a bad old-state ciphertext with the valid old signing key and
asserts the stable crypto decrypt error, and signs a far-future old-state
message that is rejected before ratcheting, without changing the later
successful decrypt path. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/fuzz-check cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin signal_stateful_records --quiet`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/fuzz-check cargo clippy --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin signal_stateful_records -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The deterministic Signal sender-key distribution truncation fixture slice added
after the multi-state sender-key fuzz work extends `wa-testkit` with a
`sender_key_distribution_truncate` vector. The fixture starts from a full
five-state sender-key record, applies a new distribution, and pins that the new
state is inserted first while the four most recent existing states remain in
order and the oldest state is dropped. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures signal_fixtures_match_public_signal_primitives --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-testkit --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-testkit --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The store-backed Signal sender-key distribution truncation slice added after
the deterministic truncation fixture promotes the same invariant through
`SignalProviderStateStore`. The provider-state regression seeds a persisted
five-state sender-key record, processes a new distribution through the locked
transactional provider path, and verifies the stored record contains the new
state plus the four most recent previous states while the oldest state is
dropped. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core provider_state_store_truncates_full_sender_key_record_on_distribution --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core provider_state_store_processes_sender_key_distribution_record --quiet`

- `git diff --check`

The store-backed Signal sender-key distribution replacement slice added after
the truncation provider regression promotes same-key different-signer
replacement through `SignalProviderStateStore`. The direct provider-state
regression seeds a persisted sender-key record with a private same-key state
plus an unrelated state, processes a distribution for the same key id with a
different signing key, and verifies the old same-key state and skipped key are
dropped while the unrelated state remains persisted. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core provider_state_store_replaces_same_key_sender_key_distribution --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core provider_state_store_processes_sender_key_distribution_record --quiet`

- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The store-backed Signal sender-key distribution stale no-rollback slice added
after the same-key replacement provider regression pins stale same-signer
distribution handling directly at `SignalProviderStateStore`. The direct
provider-state regression seeds a persisted sender-key record with private
signing material, a skipped sender-message key, and an unrelated state, then
processes a lower-iteration distribution for the same sender-key id/signing key
and verifies the returned and persisted records remain byte-for-byte unchanged.

The store-backed Signal sender-key distribution advancement slice added after
the stale no-rollback provider regression pins newer same-signer distribution
handling directly at `SignalProviderStateStore`. The direct provider-state
regression seeds a persisted sender-key record with private signing material, a
skipped sender-message key, and an unrelated state, then processes a
higher-iteration distribution for the same sender-key id/signing key and

skipped key, and unrelated state remain persisted. It was verified in the

The Signal stateful-record sender-key distribution truncation fuzz slice added
after the store-backed truncation regression extends `signal_stateful_records`
with a generated full five-state sender-key record. The structured path applies
a distinct new distribution and asserts the updated record keeps the new state
plus the four most recent previous states, preserves their chain/signing
material exactly, and drops the oldest state. It was verified in the current VM
with:

The store-backed Signal sender-key multi-state decrypt slice added after
distribution truncation fuzz promotes the preserved-state decrypt invariant
through `SignalProviderStateStore`. The provider-state regression persists a
receiver sender-key record whose first state is a replacement and whose second
state has the same sender-key id with the old signing key, decrypts an old-state
message through the transactional provider path, and verifies the stored record
advances only the matched old state while leaving the replacement state
unchanged. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core provider_state_store_decrypts_later_matching_sender_key_state --quiet`

The store-backed Signal sender-key multi-state replacement-continuity slice
added after the first provider-state multi-state decrypt regression proves the
newer replacement state remains usable after decrypting a preserved old state.
The direct provider-state regression persists a replacement-plus-preserved
sender-key record, decrypts an old-state message through
`SignalProviderStateStore`, then decrypts a replacement-state message through
the same persisted record and verifies the replacement chain advances to the
expected next ratchet while the old state remains at its post-decrypt
iteration. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core provider_state_store_decrypts_replacement_sender_key_state_after_later_state --quiet`

- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The store-backed Signal sender-key multi-state invalid-signature slice added
after the successful provider-state multi-state decrypt regression pins the
failure side of the same provider boundary. The new regression persists a
replacement-plus-preserved sender-key record, corrupts an old-state message
signature, verifies the direct provider-state decrypt path returns the stable
invalid-signature protocol error without changing the stored record, and then
decrypts the original old-state message plus a replacement-state message to
prove the preserved state was not consumed and the replacement state still
advances to the expected next ratchet. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core provider_state_store_preserves_later_sender_key_state_after_invalid_signature --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core provider_state_store_decrypts_replacement_sender_key_state_after_later_state --quiet`

- `cargo check -p wa-core --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The store-backed Signal sender-key multi-state failed-decrypt slice added after
the invalid-signature provider-state regression pins the post-authentication
failure side of the same boundary. The direct provider-state regression signs an
old-state message with a deliberately invalid ciphertext, verifies the stable
crypto decrypt error leaves the replacement-plus-preserved sender-key record
byte-for-byte unchanged, then decrypts the original old-state message plus a
replacement-state message to prove the preserved state still advances normally
and the replacement state still advances to the expected next ratchet. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo check -p wa-core --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The store-backed Signal sender-key multi-state far-future slice added after the
failed-decrypt provider-state regression pins the pre-ratchet future-jump

advances normally and the replacement state still advances to the expected next

The store-backed Signal sender-key multi-state replay slice added after the
far-future provider-state regression pins consumed-message replay rejection at
the same direct provider boundary. The regression decrypts a preserved old-state
message once through `SignalProviderStateStore`, replays the same signed
sender-key message, verifies the stable duplicate-iteration protocol error, and
proves the persisted replacement-plus-preserved sender-key record remains
byte-for-byte unchanged after the replay rejection. The same regression now also
decrypts a replacement-state message after the replay rejection and verifies the
replacement chain advances to the expected next ratchet while the preserved old
state remains at its post-decrypt iteration. It was verified in the current VM
with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core provider_state_store_rejects_later_sender_key_state_replay_without_mutation --quiet`

- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The store-backed Signal direct media-send slice added after the sender-key
multi-state replay hardening broadens the one-to-one send path beyond text and
poll payloads. A new client regression drives `send_image_with_signal_provider`
with image media through USync device discovery, Signal session assertion,
pre-key ciphertext emission, media message node shaping, and remote-side
pre-key decrypt/unpad/decode to verify the image MIME type, caption, and direct
path survive the store-backed provider boundary. It was verified in the current
VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_image_with_signal_provider_encrypts_media_payload --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`

The high-level Signal direct established-session slice added after the direct
media-send coverage promotes the one-to-one pre-key-to-whisper transition
through the client relay path. A new regression sends a first text message with
`send_text_with_signal_provider`, decrypts the emitted `pkmsg` on the remote
side to obtain the receiver session, answers the post-send privacy token issue,
then sends a second text message to the same recipient. The second send now
proves USync device discovery is enough because no fresh key-bundle query is
answered, emits a regular `msg` Signal whisper with counter 2, and decrypts
from the remote session produced by the first pre-key message. It was verified
in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_text_with_signal_provider_reuses_established_session_for_second_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_text_with_signal_provider_reuses_established_session_for_second_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_text_with_signal_provider_fetches_session_and_writes_pre_key_ciphertext --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_image_with_signal_provider_encrypts_media_payload --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The high-level Signal direct receive established-session slice added after the
send-side established-session coverage closes the matching inbound client path.
The existing `process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider` regression now
decrypts an inbound `pkmsg`, verifies the one-time pre-key is consumed and the
provider session is stored, then processes a second inbound regular `msg` from
the sender's advanced session. The test asserts the second message ack, typed
upsert payload, persisted receiving-chain counter, and the follow-up
provider-encrypted reply decrypted from the sender's second-session record. It
was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider_decrypts_pre_key_message_and_replies --quiet`

- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`

- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The high-level Signal direct receive out-of-order slice added after inbound
established-session coverage promotes provider skipped-key behavior through the
client receive boundary. A new `process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider`
regression decrypts an initial inbound `pkmsg`, then processes the sender's
third regular `msg` before the second one. The client now proves the third
message emits a normal ack/event while persisting skipped counter 2, then
decrypts the late second `msg` from the skipped key and verifies the skipped-key
store is empty while the receiving chain remains at counter 3. It was verified
in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider_decrypts_out_of_order_session_messages --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider_decrypts_out_of_order_session_messages --quiet`

- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_out_of_order_session_messages_from_store --quiet`

- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The high-level Signal direct receive consumed-skipped replay slice added after
the client out-of-order receive regression pins the failure side of the same
boundary. The out-of-order `process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider`
regression now replays the already-consumed counter-2 `msg`, verifies the client
returns a parsing nack with the stable duplicate-counter decrypt error, emits no
typed message event, and leaves the persisted provider-session bytes unchanged.
It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider_decrypts_out_of_order_session_messages --quiet`

The high-level Signal direct receive skipped-key failed-decrypt slice added
after consumed-skipped replay coverage pins the no-mutation side of the same
client boundary. The out-of-order `process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider`
regression now tampers the stored counter-2 `msg` after counter 3 has persisted
it as a skipped key, verifies the client returns a parsing nack with the stable
crypto decrypt error and emits no typed event, proves the provider-session bytes
remain unchanged, then decrypts the valid late counter-2 message and continues
through the consumed-replay rejection. It was verified in the current VM with:

The high-level Signal direct receive active-chain failed-decrypt slice added
after skipped-key failed-decrypt coverage pins the pre-skipped-key side of the
same client boundary. The out-of-order
`process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider` regression now tampers the future
counter-3 `msg` before it is accepted, verifies the client returns a parsing
nack with the stable crypto decrypt error and emits no typed event, proves the
provider-session bytes remain at the post-pre-key state, then decrypts the
valid counter-3 message to persist skipped counter 2 and continues through the
skipped-key failed-decrypt, valid late decrypt, and consumed-replay assertions.
It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider_decrypts_out_of_order_session_messages --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider_decrypts_out_of_order_session_messages --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider_decrypts_pre_key_message_and_replies --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_preserves_session_after_failed_message_decrypt --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_preserves_session_after_failed_message_decrypt --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The high-level Signal direct receive new-remote-ratchet slice added after the
active-chain failed-decrypt coverage promotes the store-backed DH ratchet edge
case through `process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider`. The new client
regression receives an initial pre-key message, advances the local session with
a reply, rejects a tampered first message from the new remote ratchet without
mutating the persisted provider session, accepts the valid new-ratchet message
with `previous_counter=3`, rejects a replay of that consumed new-ratchet
message, consumes both late previous-chain skipped messages, and then decrypts
the next current-ratchet message while asserting ACK/nack behavior, typed event
emission, remote-ratchet persistence, skipped-key counters, and receiving-chain
advancement. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider_accepts_new_remote_ratchet --quiet`

- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_consumes_multiple_previous_chain_skipped_keys_after_ratchet --quiet`
