The high-level Signal direct receive stale previous-counter rollback slice added
after the new-remote-ratchet client coverage pins another DH-ratchet failure
edge at the public receive boundary. The same
`process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider` regression now advances through the
valid new remote ratchet, then injects a fresh ratchet key whose
`previous_counter` moves backwards from the active receiving-chain counter. The
client returns a parsing nack with the stable rollback error, emits no typed
event, leaves the provider-session bytes unchanged, and then decrypts the next
valid current-ratchet message to prove the stale attempt did not mutate the
session. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider_accepts_new_remote_ratchet --quiet`

- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The high-level Signal direct receive far-future ratchet rejection slice added
after stale previous-counter rollback coverage closes two more one-to-one
provider-session receive edges at the client boundary. The same
`process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider` regression now advances through a
valid current remote ratchet, rejects a fresh-ratchet message whose
`previous_counter` would skip too far through the old chain, rejects a
same-ratchet message whose current counter is too far in the future, verifies
both failures return parsing nacks with the stable protocol errors and emit no

failure, then decrypts the next valid current-ratchet message. It was verified

has persisted skipped old-chain keys, emits a parsing nack with no typed event,

consumed and a provider session exists, replays the same `pkmsg`, verifies the

The high-level Signal direct receive signed-pre-key-only slice added after
pre-key identity-change coverage promotes the store-backed no-one-time-pre-key
`pkmsg` path into `process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider`. The client
regression receives a valid signed-pre-key-only pre-key message, verifies the
typed plaintext event and ACK, proves an unrelated local one-time pre-key remains
available, and asserts the persisted provider identity plus receiver session
ratchet state match the sender identity and base key. It now also replays the
same signed-only `pkmsg`, verifies the parsing nack and no typed event, proves
the provider-session bytes and unrelated local pre-key remain unchanged, then
decrypts the next session `msg` to prove the accepted signed-only session

The high-level Signal direct receive failed-pre-key-decrypt slice added after
the signed-pre-key-only receive coverage promotes another store-backed first
`pkmsg` failure class through `process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider`. The
client regression rejects missing local Signal key material, a pre-key message
rewritten to name a different existing local one-time pre-key, a tampered
pre-key ciphertext, and a signed-pre-key id mismatch with parsing nacks, emits
no typed events, proves the local one-time pre-keys plus provider session and
identity records remain unchanged after each failure, restores credentials after
the missing-material branch, then decrypts the original valid `pkmsg` to prove
the failed attempts did not poison session setup or consume the unrelated
pre-key. The core store-backed regressions now also pin the
missing-local-material and wrong-pre-key-material failures before any pre-key
consumption or provider session/identity creation. It was verified in the
current VM with:

The high-level Signal direct receive provider-identity validation slice added
after the failed pre-key decrypt coverage promotes a persisted provider-session
runtime validation guard through `process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider`.
The client regression now accepts an initial `pkmsg`, deletes the raw provider
identity row for that sender while preserving the provider-session bytes, rejects
the next session `msg` with a parsing nack and no typed event, proves the session
bytes remain unchanged, corrupts the identity row to a mismatched key and repeats
the same rejection, restores the correct identity, and then decrypts the same
session `msg` successfully. The core store-backed coverage now also pins the
missing-identity runtime decrypt failure without mutating the persisted session.

The high-level Signal direct receive skipped-key pruning slice added after the
missing provider-session guard promotes the provider-session retained-key cap
through `process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider`. A new client regression
accepts the initial `pkmsg`, then receives a same-ratchet session `msg` far
enough ahead to fill and prune the skipped-message-key cache. It verifies the
typed target message and ACK, proves the stored provider-session cache is capped
with counter 2 pruned and counter 3 retained, rejects the pruned counter with a
parsing nack and no typed event, proves the session bytes remain unchanged after
that rejection, then decrypts the retained counter and observes the cache shrink.
The existing core store-backed pruning regression was rerun alongside the new
client boundary coverage. It was verified in the current VM with:

The high-level Signal direct receive malformed provider-identity slice added
after the skipped-key pruning coverage promotes another persisted-state runtime
guard through `process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider`. The existing client
pre-key/session receive regression now accepts an initial `pkmsg`, restores the
session after the missing-session branch, deletes the identity, then writes a
raw malformed provider-identity row before the next session `msg`. It verifies
the malformed identity produces a parsing nack with no typed event and the
stable invalid public-key error, proves the provider-session bytes remain
unchanged and the malformed identity row is preserved, then continues through the
existing mismatched-identity, malformed-session, and successful second `msg`
branches. The core store-backed regression now also pins malformed-identity
runtime decrypt without mutating session or identity state. It was verified in
the current VM with:

The high-level Signal group receive invalid sender-key record slice added after
the remote-ratchet pairing coverage promotes hostile persisted sender-key record
validation through `process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider`. The new client
regression seeds a valid receiver-side sender-key record for a group `skmsg`,
raw-overwrites that store row with mismatched-signing-key, duplicate-state,
duplicate skipped-iteration, and future skipped-iteration
`SenderKeyRecordStructure` bytes, rejects each incoming group message with a
parsing nack and no typed event, proves each invalid record is preserved
byte-for-byte, restores the valid record, and then decrypts the same sender-key
payload to prove the normal client-boundary path still advances the stored
record and emits the group message event. The same client-boundary regression
now also rejects replayed, far-future, and invalid-signature group sender-key
messages after that valid decrypt, asserting parsing nacks, no typed events,
and no mutation of the advanced sender-key row. It was verified in the current
VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider_rejects_invalid_sender_key_record_without_mutation --quiet`

- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The high-level Signal group receive out-of-order sender-key slice added after
the invalid sender-key record coverage promotes skipped sender-message-key
handling through `process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider`. The new client
regression processes the second group `skmsg` before the first, verifies the
receiver-side sender-key record advances while retaining the first iteration as
a skipped key, rejects a valid-signature but undecryptable first message without
consuming that skipped key, decrypts the original first message from the stored
skipped key, and then rejects a replay of that first message while preserving
the advanced sender-key row. It was verified in the current VM with:

The high-level Signal group receive sender-key distribution recovery slice added
after the out-of-order group receive coverage promotes the persisted
distribution fallback through `process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider`.
The new client regression stores a sender-key distribution in the client Signal
repository, decrypts a group `skmsg` with no provider sender-key row, deletes
the repaired row and decrypts a later group `skmsg`, then corrupts the provider
row and decrypts another later group `skmsg`. Each branch proves the group event
is emitted, the sender-key row is repaired from the persisted distribution, and
the repaired row has the expected sender-chain iteration and skipped-key
iterations. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider_recovers_sender_key_record_from_distribution --quiet`

- `git diff --check`

The high-level Signal group receive stale sender-key distribution recovery
slice added after the missing/corrupt distribution recovery coverage promotes
the decodable-but-stale provider sender-key repair path through
`process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider`. The new client regression seeds a
stale receiver-side sender-key record, stores a fresh sender-key distribution in
the client Signal repository, decrypts a fresh group `skmsg` that cannot match
the stale record, and proves the stale row is repaired by inserting the fresh
state while retaining the old state. It also verifies the emitted group message
event and that the fresh distribution remains persisted. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The high-level Signal group receive sender-key distribution message slice added
after the stale distribution recovery coverage promotes the inbound
distribution-processing hook through
`process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider`. The new client regression
processes a group message payload that carries both regular and fast-ratchet
`SenderKeyDistributionMessage` fields, verifies the emitted message event
counts both distributions, and proves the client wrote both provider sender-key
states while the repository keeps the last processed fast-ratchet distribution
bytes for later `skmsg` decrypt recovery. It was verified in the current VM

- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The group/status fanout encryption-failure slice added after inbound
sender-key distribution processing hardens the send-edge parity gaps called out
for group sender-key and status/broadcast relay. The new client regressions use
a recording encryptor that succeeds for the first participant and fails on the
second, proving `send_group_sender_key_text_with_distribution` does not emit
either the partially built distribution stanza or the root `skmsg` stanza, and
`send_status_text` does not emit a partial `status@broadcast` stanza after
participant encryption fails mid-fanout. The group regression also proves the
local sender-key distribution record can be initialized without treating the
failed relay as sent, and both group/status regressions now verify the failed
message ids are absent from the retry cache so later retry preparation reports
them as missing instead of replaying unsent relays. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_group_sender_key_distribution_stops_when_participant_encryption_fails --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_status_text_stops_when_participant_encryption_fails --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_group_sender_key_distribution_stops_when_participant_encryption_fails --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_status_text_stops_when_participant_encryption_fails --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The direct-send mid-fanout encryption-failure slice added after the
group/status fanout failure coverage closes the one-to-one counterpart beyond
the earlier no-recipient and session-assertion failures. A new client regression
discovers a remote recipient plus an own linked device, lets the first
participant encryption succeed, fails the second participant encryption, and
proves no partial direct message stanza is emitted. It also plans a retry for
the failed message id and verifies the unsent relay was not cached as a recent
message, so later retry preparation reports the id as missing instead of
replaying a message that never reached the socket. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_text_stops_when_participant_encryption_fails_without_retry_cache --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_text_stops_when_participant_encryption_fails_without_retry_cache --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The direct-send relay-send failure slice added after direct-send mid-fanout
encryption-failure coverage pins the adjacent post-encryption socket boundary.
A new client regression discovers a remote recipient plus an own linked device,
encrypts both participant payloads, closes the mock connection from a test
encryptor before the fully built message stanza can be enqueued, verifies the
existing `ConnectionClosed` error is returned, and proves the unsent direct
relay is still absent from the retry cache so later retry preparation reports
the id as missing instead of replaying a message that never reached the socket.
It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_text_stops_when_relay_send_fails_without_retry_cache --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_text_stops_when_relay_send_fails_without_retry_cache --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The status relay-send failure slice added after direct-send relay-send failure
coverage applies the same post-encryption socket boundary to
`status@broadcast` fanout. A new client regression discovers two status
audience recipients, encrypts both participant payloads, closes the mock
connection from a test encryptor before the fully built status stanza can be
enqueued, verifies the existing `ConnectionClosed` error is returned, and proves
the unsent status message id remains absent from the retry cache so later retry
preparation reports it as missing rather than replaying a message that never
reached the socket. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_status_text_stops_when_relay_send_fails_without_retry_cache --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_status_text_stops_when_relay_send_fails_without_retry_cache --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The group sender-key distribution relay-send failure slice added after status
relay-send failure coverage pins the first socket boundary in the two-stanza
group sender-key send flow. A new client regression discovers two group
participants, encrypts both sender-key distribution payloads, closes the mock
connection from a test encryptor before the distribution stanza can be enqueued,
verifies the existing `ConnectionClosed` error is returned, proves the local
sender-key distribution record can exist without treating the relay as sent, and
keeps both the distribution id and following group message id absent from the
retry cache so later retry preparation reports them as missing. It was verified
in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_group_sender_key_distribution_stops_when_distribution_relay_send_fails --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_group_sender_key_distribution_stops_when_distribution_relay_send_fails --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The group sender-key root relay-send failure slice added after distribution
relay-send failure coverage pins the second socket boundary in the two-stanza
group sender-key send flow. A small private relay helper now keeps production
behavior unchanged while allowing tests to wrap the internally created
sender-key encryptor. A new client regression sends and caches the sender-key
distribution stanza, encrypts the root group `skmsg`, closes the mock connection
after that encryption but before the root stanza enqueue, verifies the existing
`ConnectionClosed` error is returned, and proves retry preparation can replay
only the sent distribution id while reporting the unsent group message id as
missing. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_group_sender_key_distribution_finalizes_distribution_when_group_relay_send_fails --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_group_sender_key_distribution_finalizes_distribution_when_group_relay_send_fails --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The group sender-key root encryption-failure slice added after root relay-send
failure coverage pins the earlier root `skmsg` boundary in the same two-stanza
flow. A new client regression sends and caches the sender-key distribution
stanza, then fails the root group sender-key encryptor before any root stanza is
built or enqueued. It verifies the existing encryptor error is returned, proves
the distribution relay reached the socket, and shows retry preparation can
replay only the sent distribution id while reporting the unsent group message id
as missing. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_group_sender_key_distribution_finalizes_distribution_when_group_encrypt_fails --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_group_sender_key_distribution_finalizes_distribution_when_group_encrypt_fails --quiet`

- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The placeholder resend post-encryption relay-send rollback slice added after
group sender-key root failure coverage pins the same socket boundary for
peer-data-operation resend requests. A new client regression discovers the
account primary device plus linked device, encrypts both placeholder resend
payloads, closes the mock connection from a test encryptor before the fully
built peer-data-operation stanza can be enqueued, verifies the existing
`ConnectionClosed` error is returned, proves no partial stanza was emitted, and
confirms the placeholder id is removed from the pending tracker. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`

- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The direct-send post-send tctoken failure-suppression slice added after
placeholder resend relay-send rollback coverage pins the scheduler side of a
failed send. A new client regression makes a one-to-one text send eligible for
post-send trusted-contact-token issuance, fails the second participant
encryption before any relay stanza can be emitted, verifies no privacy-token IQ
is spawned, proves no tctoken issue remains in flight, and confirms no
trusted-contact token record was stored for the failed remote. It was verified
in the current VM with:

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`

- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The direct-send post-encryption relay-send tctoken suppression slice added after
participant-encryption suppression coverage pins the adjacent socket-send
failure boundary. A new client regression makes a one-to-one text send eligible
for post-send trusted-contact-token issuance, encrypts both participant
payloads, closes the mock connection before the message stanza can be enqueued,
verifies the existing `ConnectionClosed` error is returned, rejects any outbound
privacy-token IQ, proves no tctoken issue remains in flight, and confirms no

The automatic placeholder resend wrapper relay-send rollback slice added after
direct-send tctoken send-failure suppression coverage pins the receive-triggered
wrapper around the same peer-data-operation failure boundary. A new client
regression turns an eligible unavailable-stub `WebMessageInfo` into a placeholder
resend request, encrypts both account-device payloads, closes the mock
connection before the peer-data-operation stanza can be enqueued, verifies the
existing `ConnectionClosed` error is returned, confirms no partial stanza was
emitted, proves the placeholder id is removed from the pending tracker, and then
retries the same web message through a fresh mock connection to show the wrapper
no longer suppresses it as a duplicate. It was verified in the current VM with:

The store-backed Signal direct-send relay-failure slice added after automatic
placeholder resend wrapper rollback coverage promotes the same unsent-message
cache boundary from mock encryption to the real client Signal codec. A new
client regression drives direct text send device discovery, fetches a remote
pre-key bundle, encrypts through `ClientSignalMessageCodec`, closes the mock
connection immediately after the successful Signal encryption and before the
message stanza can be enqueued, verifies the existing `ConnectionClosed` error,
confirms no partial stanza was emitted, proves the provider session was written
for retry/session planning, and shows retry preparation reports the unsent
message id as missing instead of replaying a message that never reached the
socket. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`

- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The store-backed Signal direct-send own-linked-device relay-failure slice added
after the single-recipient real Signal failure coverage broadens that boundary
to normal companion fanout. A new client regression discovers the remote primary
plus the sender's linked device, fetches pre-key bundles for both
`123@s.whatsapp.net` and `999:8@s.whatsapp.net`, encrypts both payloads through
`ClientSignalMessageCodec`, closes the mock connection immediately after the
linked-device Signal encryption and before the message stanza can be enqueued,
verifies the existing `ConnectionClosed` error, confirms no partial stanza was
emitted, proves both provider sessions were written, and shows retry preparation
reports the unsent message id as missing instead of replaying a message that
never reached the socket. It was verified in the current VM with:

The store-backed Signal direct-send own-linked-device success slice added after
the linked-device relay-failure coverage pins the normal companion fanout shape
with real Signal encryption. A new client regression discovers the remote
primary plus the sender's linked device, fetches pre-key bundles for
`123@s.whatsapp.net` and `999:8@s.whatsapp.net`, sends the message successfully,
verifies the emitted participants list and recipient count, decrypts the remote
participant `pkmsg` as the plain extended-text message, decrypts the own linked
device `pkmsg` as a `device_sent_message` wrapper whose destination is the
remote chat, and confirms both provider sessions were stored. It was verified
in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_text_with_signal_provider_encrypts_remote_and_own_linked_device_payloads --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_text_with_signal_provider_encrypts_remote_and_own_linked_device_payloads --quiet`

- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The store-backed Signal direct-send own-linked-device established-session slice
added after the first-send companion fanout coverage pins the ratchet reuse path
for both participants. A new client regression sends an initial direct message
to the remote primary plus the sender's linked device, decrypts both `pkmsg`
payloads to capture the remote and own-linked provider session records, answers
the post-send trusted-contact-token issue, sends a second direct message to the
same chat, and proves the second send completes after only device discovery with
no key-bundle query. It verifies both second-send participant payloads are
normal `msg` ciphertext at counter 2, decrypts the remote payload as a plain
extended-text message, and decrypts the own-linked payload as a
`device_sent_message` wrapper whose destination remains the remote chat. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_text_with_signal_provider_reuses_established_sessions_for_own_linked_device --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_text_with_signal_provider_reuses_established_sessions_for_own_linked_device --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The store-backed Signal status own-linked-device success slice added after
direct established-session companion fanout coverage moves the same real Signal
shape onto `status@broadcast`. A new client regression sends a text status to an
audience recipient while USync also returns the sender's linked device, fetches
pre-key bundles for `111@s.whatsapp.net` and `999:8@s.whatsapp.net`, verifies
the emitted status participant list and recipient count, decrypts the audience
`pkmsg` as the plain extended-text status payload, decrypts the own linked
device `pkmsg` as a `device_sent_message` wrapper whose destination is

`pkmsg` payloads to capture the audience and own-linked provider session
records, sends a second status to the same audience, and proves the second send

The store-backed Signal group sender-key own-linked-device distribution slice
added after status established-session companion fanout coverage moves the real
Signal companion fanout shape onto group sender-key distribution. A new client
regression discovers a group audience member plus the sender's linked device,
fetches pre-key bundles for `111@s.whatsapp.net` and `999:8@s.whatsapp.net`,
verifies the distribution participant list and recipient count, decrypts the
audience distribution `pkmsg` as a plain sender-key distribution, decrypts the
own linked distribution `pkmsg` as a `device_sent_message` wrapper whose

distribution to verify the group plaintext. It was verified in the current VM

The store-backed Signal group sender-key own-linked-device established-session
slice added after first-send group companion distribution coverage pins

audience member plus the sender's linked device, decrypts both first-send
distribution `pkmsg` payloads to capture the audience and own-linked provider
session records, decrypts the first root `skmsg`, sends a second group
sender-key message to the same group, and proves the second distribution send
completes after only group metadata and device discovery with no key-bundle
query. It verifies both second-send distribution payloads are normal `msg`
ciphertext at counter 2, decrypts the audience payload as a plain sender-key
distribution, decrypts the own-linked payload as a `device_sent_message`
wrapper whose destination remains the group, confirms both second distribution
bytes match, then decrypts the second root group `skmsg` with that distribution
to verify the group plaintext. It was verified in the current VM with:

The store-backed Signal group sender-key own-linked-device distribution
relay-failure slice added after the status real-Signal relay-failure coverage
promotes the unsent distribution boundary from mock encryption to the real
client Signal codec. A new client regression sends a group sender-key message
with a distribution fanout to a group audience member plus the sender's linked
device using `ClientSignalMessageCodec` wrapped by a connection-closing
encryptor, fetches pre-key bundles for `111@s.whatsapp.net` and
`999:8@s.whatsapp.net`, closes the connection after the own-linked distribution
encryption and before the distribution stanza can be enqueued, verifies the
existing `ConnectionClosed` error, confirms no partial stanza was emitted,
checks the audience plaintext is a plain sender-key distribution and the
own-linked plaintext is a `device_sent_message` wrapper whose destination is
the group, confirms both distribution bytes match and decode, proves both
provider sessions were stored, verifies the local sender-key distribution
record can exist without treating the relay as sent, and shows retry
preparation reports both the unsent distribution id and following group message
id as missing. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_group_sender_key_distribution_with_signal_codec_stops_after_linked_device_encrypt_without_retry_cache --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_group_sender_key_distribution_with_signal_codec_stops_after_linked_device_encrypt_without_retry_cache --quiet`

- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The store-backed Signal group sender-key own-linked-device root relay-failure
slice added after the real-Signal distribution relay-failure coverage promotes
the post-distribution root `skmsg` send boundary onto the full real Signal
fanout shape. A new client regression sends a group sender-key message with
distribution fanout to a group audience member plus the sender's linked device
using `ClientSignalMessageCodec`, fetches pre-key bundles for
`111@s.whatsapp.net` and `999:8@s.whatsapp.net`, successfully enqueues the
real Signal distribution stanza, closes the connection after root sender-key
encryption and before the root group stanza can be enqueued, verifies the
existing `ConnectionClosed` error, confirms no partial root stanza was emitted,
decrypts both distribution `pkmsg` payloads to verify the plain audience
distribution and the own-linked `device_sent_message` wrapper, proves both
provider sessions were stored, checks the root encryptor saw the intended group
plaintext, and shows retry preparation can replay the sent distribution while
reporting the unsent root group id as missing. It was verified in the current
VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_group_sender_key_signal_finalizes_distribution_when_group_relay_send_fails --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_group_sender_key_signal_finalizes_distribution_when_group_relay_send_fails --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The store-backed Signal group sender-key own-linked-device root
encryption-failure slice added after the real-Signal root relay-failure
coverage promotes the remaining post-distribution root failure boundary onto
real Signal distribution fanout. A new client regression sends a group
sender-key message with distribution fanout to a group audience member plus the
sender's linked device using `ClientSignalMessageCodec`, fetches pre-key
bundles for `111@s.whatsapp.net` and `999:8@s.whatsapp.net`, successfully
enqueues the real Signal distribution stanza, fails the root sender-key
encryptor before the root group stanza can be built or enqueued, verifies no
partial root stanza was emitted, decrypts both distribution `pkmsg` payloads to
verify the plain audience distribution and the own-linked `device_sent_message`
wrapper, proves both provider sessions were stored, checks the root encryptor
saw the intended group plaintext, and shows retry preparation can replay the
sent distribution while reporting the unsent root group id as missing. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_group_sender_key_signal_finalizes_distribution_when_group_encrypt_fails --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_group_sender_key_signal_finalizes_distribution_when_group_encrypt_fails --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The store-backed Signal group sender-key own PN/LID linked-device dedupe slice
added after the real-Signal root failure coverage promotes the earlier mock
own-alias recipient collapse onto the full Signal distribution path. A new
client regression sends a group sender-key message from an account that has
both `999:7@s.whatsapp.net` and `ownlid@lid`, receives USync device results
that advertise the same own linked device through both account identifiers,
asserts the key-bundle query only requests `111@s.whatsapp.net` and
`999:8@s.whatsapp.net`, verifies the distribution participant list and
recipient count collapse to those two recipients, decrypts the audience
distribution `pkmsg` as a plain sender-key distribution, decrypts the
own-linked distribution `pkmsg` as a `device_sent_message` wrapper whose
destination is the group, proves both provider sessions were stored, and
decrypts the root group `skmsg` to verify the group plaintext. It was verified
in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_group_sender_key_signal_deduplicates_own_pn_lid_linked_device_aliases --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_group_sender_key_signal_deduplicates_own_pn_lid_linked_device_aliases --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The store-backed Signal status own PN/LID linked-device dedupe slice added

`999:7@s.whatsapp.net` and `ownlid@lid`, receives USync device results that
advertise the same own linked device through both account identifiers, asserts
the key-bundle query only requests `111@s.whatsapp.net` and
`999:8@s.whatsapp.net`, verifies the status participant list and recipient
count collapse to those two recipients, decrypts the audience `pkmsg` as the
plain extended-text status payload, decrypts the own-linked `pkmsg` as a
`device_sent_message` wrapper whose destination remains `status@broadcast`,
and proves both provider sessions were stored. It was verified in the current

`111@c.us` and canonical `111@s.whatsapp.net` USync device results, asserts the
key-bundle query only requests `111@s.whatsapp.net`, verifies the distribution
participant list and recipient count collapse to the canonical PN JID, decrypts

the lookup to `123@s.whatsapp.net`, accepts duplicate `123@c.us` and canonical
`123@s.whatsapp.net` USync device results, asserts the key-bundle query only
requests `123@s.whatsapp.net`, verifies the outbound message `to` attribute,
participant list, and recipient count collapse to the canonical PN JID,

The store-backed Signal status media own PN/LID linked-device dedupe slice
added after media companion fanout coverage pins the same local-alias collapse
for uploaded image statuses. A new client regression sends a JPEG
`status@broadcast` from an account that has both `999:7@s.whatsapp.net` and
`ownlid@lid`, receives USync results that expose the same sender linked device
through both identities, and asserts the key-bundle query only targets
`111@s.whatsapp.net` and `999:8@s.whatsapp.net`. It verifies the emitted media
broadcast participant list and recipient count collapse to those two
participants, decrypts the audience `pkmsg` as the plain image status payload,
decrypts the own-linked `pkmsg` as a `device_sent_message` wrapper whose
destination remains `status@broadcast`, proves the duplicate own-LID provider
session was not stored, and keeps the media `phash` relay shape intact. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_status_image_signal_deduplicates_own_pn_lid_linked_device_aliases --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_status_image_signal_deduplicates_own_pn_lid_linked_device_aliases --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`

- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The store-backed Signal status media own-linked-device established-session
slice added after first-send media companion fanout coverage pins
`status@broadcast` media ratchet reuse for both participants. A new client
regression sends an initial uploaded JPEG status to the audience plus the
sender's linked device, decrypts both first-send `pkmsg` payloads to capture
the audience and own-linked provider session records, sends a second uploaded
JPEG status to the same audience, and proves the second send completes after
only device discovery with no key-bundle query. It verifies both second-send
participant payloads are normal `msg` ciphertext at counter 2, decrypts the
audience payload as a plain image status, and decrypts the own-linked payload
as a `device_sent_message` wrapper whose destination remains
`status@broadcast`. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_status_image_signal_reuses_established_sessions_for_own_linked_device --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_status_image_signal_reuses_established_sessions_for_own_linked_device --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The same status media established-session regression now also runs from an
account that has both `999:7@s.whatsapp.net` and `ownlid@lid`. Both the initial
`pkmsg` fanout and the second established-session send receive USync rows that
advertise the same own linked device through PN and LID identities, while the
session query and emitted participant lists stay collapsed to
`111@s.whatsapp.net` and `999:8@s.whatsapp.net`. The tightened test proves the
second send still completes with no key-bundle query and that no duplicate
`ownlid:8@lid` provider session exists after either ratchet step. The PN/LID
tightening was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_status_image_signal_reuses_established_sessions_for_own_linked_device --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_status_image_signal_reuses_established_sessions_for_own_linked_device --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

from an account that has both `999:7@s.whatsapp.net` and `ownlid@lid`. The
initial distribution `pkmsg` fanout and the second established-session
distribution send both receive USync rows that advertise the same own linked
device through PN and LID identities, while the session query and emitted
distribution participant lists stay collapsed to `111@s.whatsapp.net` and
`999:8@s.whatsapp.net`. The tightened test proves no duplicate `ownlid:8@lid`
provider session exists after the initial pre-key distribution or the second
ratchet step, while preserving root `skmsg` media decrypt coverage for both
group sends. The PN/LID tightening was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_group_sender_key_media_signal_reuses_established_sessions_for_own_linked_device --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_group_sender_key_media_signal_reuses_established_sessions_for_own_linked_device --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The store-backed Signal direct poll own-linked-device slice added after group
media established-session coverage moves the existing single-recipient poll
Signal test onto companion fanout. A new client regression sends a poll through
`send_poll_with_signal_provider` while USync also returns the sender's linked
device, asserts the key-bundle query targets `123@s.whatsapp.net` and
`999:8@s.whatsapp.net`, verifies the emitted poll stanza still carries the
reporting node plus both participants, decrypts the remote `pkmsg` as the plain
poll creation payload with a 32-byte message secret, decrypts the own-linked
`pkmsg` as a `device_sent_message` wrapper whose destination remains the remote
chat, verifies the wrapped poll payload and message secret, and proves both
provider sessions were stored. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_poll_with_signal_provider_encrypts_poll_and_own_linked_device --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_poll_with_signal_provider_encrypts_poll_and_own_linked_device --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The store-backed Signal inbound poll-update secret-decryption slice added
after poll/event send facade coverage moves poll update receive enrichment from
plaintext fixtures onto the real Signal provider path. A new client regression
stores a direct poll creation with its message secret, encrypts an inbound poll
update as a remote Signal pre-key message, processes it through
`process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider`, verifies the ACK and emitted poll
update payload, proves the stored creation secret decrypts the enclosed vote,
confirms the merged target message event carries the decrypted vote fields with
no pending update record left behind, and verifies the local one-time pre-key
was consumed while the provider session was stored. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider_decrypts_poll_update_from_stored_creation_secret --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider_decrypts_poll_update_from_stored_creation_secret --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

after inbound poll-update provider coverage moves encrypted RSVP receive
enrichment from plaintext fixtures onto the real Signal provider path. A new
client regression stores a direct event creation with its message secret,
encrypts an inbound event response as a remote Signal pre-key message,
processes it through `process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider`, verifies the
ACK and emitted encrypted RSVP payload, proves the stored event secret decrypts
the enclosed response fields, confirms the merged target message event carries
the decrypted RSVP fields with no pending update record left behind, and
verifies the local one-time pre-key was consumed while the provider session was
stored. It was verified in the current VM with:

The store-backed Signal inbound late poll/event creation merge slice added
after provider-backed poll/event receive enrichment moves the delayed creation
path onto real Signal-decrypted pending updates. New client regressions process
a direct poll update and event response through
`process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider` before the referenced creation
message exists, verify the pending encrypted update records are written without
decrypted fields, then persist the corresponding poll/event creation messages
with their message secrets and prove the pending records are merged into the
target message events, decrypted, and removed. Both tests also verify ACK
emission, local one-time pre-key consumption, and provider session storage. It
was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise late_poll_creation_merges_signal_provider_pending_poll_update --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise late_poll_creation_merges_signal_provider_pending_poll_update --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise late_event_creation_merges_signal_provider_pending_event_response --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise late_event_creation_merges_signal_provider_pending_event_response --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The spawned raw-node Signal poll-update enrichment slice added after the
provider-backed late poll/event merge coverage moves the automatic incoming
processor onto the same stored-secret decrypt path. A new client regression
stores a direct poll creation with its message secret, starts
`spawn_incoming_processor_with_signal_provider`, injects a Signal-encrypted
poll update through the mock connection stream, verifies the spawned task emits
the ACK and decrypted poll update batch, confirms the target message event is
merged with decrypted vote fields and no pending update record remains, and
proves local one-time pre-key consumption plus provider session storage. It was
verified in the current VM with:

The spawned offline raw-node Signal poll-update enrichment slice added after

The spawned offline raw-node Signal event-response enrichment slice added
after the live spawned event-response coverage moves the automatic incoming
processor's offline branch onto the stored-secret RSVP decrypt path. A new
client regression stores a direct event creation with its message secret,
starts `spawn_incoming_processor_with_signal_provider`, injects an `offline`
node containing a Signal-encrypted event response through the mock connection
stream, verifies the spawned task emits the child ACK and decrypted
event-response batch, confirms the target message event is merged with
decrypted RSVP fields and no pending update record remains, and proves local
one-time pre-key consumption plus provider session storage. It was verified in
the current VM with:

The explicit offline Signal-provider facade slice added after spawned offline
poll/event coverage exposes the same real Signal offline processing path
without requiring placeholder, retry, or media orchestration. A new
`process_offline_node_with_signal_provider` convenience builds the
client-owned Signal codec, processes offline child nodes, sends child ACKs,
enriches/persists/emits receive events, and runs the usual incoming side
effects. A new client regression stores a direct event creation with its
message secret, encrypts an offline event response as a remote Signal pre-key
message, processes the offline node through the new facade, verifies the child
ACK and decrypted event-response batch, confirms the target message event is
merged with decrypted RSVP fields and no pending update record remains, and
proves local one-time pre-key consumption plus provider session storage. It was
verified in the current VM with:

The explicit offline Signal missing-one-time-pre-key slice added after the
offline facade success coverage promotes the matching provider failure path into
`process_offline_node_with_signal_provider`. The new client regression builds a
valid inbound offline `pkmsg`, consumes the referenced local one-time pre-key
before processing, verifies the offline child result returns a parsing NACK
with the stable missing-pre-key error, emits no typed receive event, and proves
the provider state still has no local pre-key, sender session, or sender
identity record. It was verified in the current VM with:

The explicit offline Signal signed-pre-key-only slice added after the explicit
offline identity-change coverage promotes the no-one-time-pre-key inbound

client regression stores an unrelated local one-time pre-key, processes an
offline signed-pre-key-only `pkmsg` through the explicit facade, verifies the
ACK and plaintext typed batch, proves the unrelated pre-key remains available
and the provider identity/session records are persisted, replays the same

The spawned live raw-node Signal signed-pre-key-id mismatch preservation slice
added after the spawned offline wrong-pre-key-material coverage promotes
another failed first-`pkmsg` class into

The spawned live raw-node Signal missing-provider-identity runtime validation
slice added after the spawned failed first-`pkmsg` preservation matrix promotes
the direct provider-session identity guard into
`spawn_incoming_processor_with_signal_provider`. The new client regression
seeds a real provider session through the spawned processor, removes only the
raw provider identity row while preserving the session bytes, injects a live
session `msg`, verifies the spawned processor sends a parsing NACK without any
typed batch, proves the provider-session bytes remain unchanged and the
identity remains absent, restores the matching provider identity, then injects
the same session `msg` through the same processor and verifies ACK, plaintext
typed batch persistence, provider-session advancement, and provider identity
persistence. It was verified in the current VM with:

The spawned live raw-node Signal invalid skipped-key counter runtime validation
slice added after the spawned invalid provider-session invariant coverage
promotes the next decodable corrupted-session guard into
`spawn_incoming_processor_with_signal_provider`. The new client regression seeds
a real provider session through the spawned processor, injects session counter
3 before counter 2 to persist a retained skipped message key, raw-corrupts the
retained skipped-key counter to zero, injects the skipped counter-2 session
`msg`, verifies the spawned processor sends a parsing NACK without any typed
batch, proves the invalid session bytes and provider identity remain unchanged,
restores the accepted out-of-order session bytes, then injects the same skipped
counter-2 session `msg` through the same processor and verifies ACK, plaintext
typed batch persistence, skipped-key consumption, and provider identity
persistence. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_preserves_state_after_invalid_skipped_key_counter --quiet`

- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The spawned offline raw-node Signal invalid skipped-key counter runtime
validation slice added after the spawned live invalid skipped-key counter
coverage promotes the same retained skipped-key counter guard into the
automatic incoming processor's offline branch. The new client regression seeds
a real provider session through an `offline` node, injects an offline session
counter 3 before counter 2 to persist a retained skipped message key,
raw-corrupts the retained skipped-key counter to zero, injects an `offline`
node containing the skipped counter-2 session `msg`, verifies the spawned
processor sends a child parsing NACK without any typed batch, proves the invalid
session bytes and provider identity remain unchanged, restores the accepted
out-of-order session bytes, then injects the same offline skipped counter-2
session `msg` through the same processor and verifies ACK, plaintext typed
batch persistence, skipped-key consumption, and provider identity persistence.
It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_preserves_state_after_offline_invalid_skipped_key_counter --quiet`

The spawned live/offline raw-node Signal out-of-order failed-decrypt
preservation slice added after the spawned invalid skipped-key counter pair
promotes the active-chain and retained skipped-key failed-decrypt boundaries
into both branches of `spawn_incoming_processor_with_signal_provider`. The live
and offline invalid skipped-counter regressions now reject a tampered future
counter-3 session `msg` before it can persist any skipped key, prove the
post-pre-key provider-session bytes remain unchanged, then accept the valid
counter-3 `msg` to retain counter 2 as a skipped key. After the malformed
skipped-counter guard restores the accepted session bytes, both regressions
reject a tampered retained counter-2 `msg` without any typed batch or
provider-session mutation, then decrypt the valid retained counter-2 `msg` and
verify skipped-key consumption, message persistence, and provider identity
persistence. The explicit client out-of-order boundary and the core
store-backed out-of-order/failed-message-decrypt regressions were rerun
alongside the spawned coverage. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider_decrypts_out_of_order_session_messages --quiet`

- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_preserves_session_after_failed_message_decrypt --quiet`

The spawned live raw-node Signal skipped-key pruning slice added after the
spawned invalid skipped-key counter coverage promotes the provider-session
retained-key cap into `spawn_incoming_processor_with_signal_provider`. The new
client regression seeds a real provider session through the spawned processor,
injects a same-ratchet session `msg` far enough ahead to fill and prune the
skipped-message-key cache, verifies the target plaintext typed batch and stored
message persistence, proves the persisted provider session is capped with
counter 2 pruned and counter 3 retained, rejects the pruned counter with a
parsing NACK and no typed batch while preserving the session bytes, then
decrypts the retained counter and observes the skipped-key cache shrink. The
existing explicit client and core store-backed pruning regressions were rerun
alongside the new spawned boundary coverage. It was verified in the current VM
with:

offline same-ratchet session `msg` far enough ahead to fill and prune the
skipped-message-key cache, verifies the target plaintext typed batch and stored
message persistence, proves the persisted provider session is capped with

The spawned live raw-node Signal new-remote-ratchet receive slice added after
the spawned skipped-key pruning pair promotes the store-backed DH ratchet edge
into `spawn_incoming_processor_with_signal_provider`. The new client regression
seeds a real provider session through the spawned processor, advances the local
session with an outbound reply, rejects a tampered first message from the new
remote ratchet without any typed batch or provider-session mutation, accepts
the valid new-ratchet `msg` with `previous_counter=3`, rejects a replay of that
consumed new-ratchet message, rejects a tampered late previous-chain `msg`
without consuming the retained skipped keys, then decrypts both late
previous-chain messages and the next current-ratchet message while asserting
ACK/NACK behavior, typed batch persistence, remote-ratchet persistence,
skipped-key consumption, receiving-chain advancement, and provider identity
persistence. The explicit client ratchet regression and the core store-backed
new-ratchet preservation/previous-chain consumption regressions were rerun
alongside the spawned coverage. It was verified in the current VM with:

current-ratchet message through offline children while asserting child
ACK/NACK behavior, typed batch persistence, remote-ratchet persistence,
skipped-key consumption, receiving-chain advancement, and provider identity
persistence. The paired spawned live/offline accept-path regressions and the

The spawned live/offline raw-node Signal stale previous-counter rollback slice
added after the spawned new-remote-ratchet pair promotes the DH-ratchet rollback
guard into both branches of `spawn_incoming_processor_with_signal_provider`.
The live and offline spawned ratchet regressions now continue after the first
current-ratchet follow-up, inject a fresh-ratchet `msg` whose
`previous_counter` moves backwards from the active receiving-chain counter,
verify a parsing NACK with no typed batch and unchanged provider-session bytes,
then decrypt the next valid current-ratchet `msg` to prove the stale attempt
did not mutate the receiving chain, remote ratchet key, skipped-key state, or
provider identity. The explicit client stale-counter boundary and the core
store-backed/record-level stale previous-counter regressions were rerun
alongside the spawned live/offline accept-path coverage. It was verified in the

The spawned live/offline raw-node Signal far-future ratchet rejection slice
added after the spawned stale previous-counter coverage closes the next two
DH-ratchet receive edges in both branches of
`spawn_incoming_processor_with_signal_provider`. The live and offline spawned
ratchet regressions now advance through a valid current remote ratchet, reject
a fresh-ratchet `msg` whose `previous_counter` would skip too far through the
old chain, reject a same-ratchet `msg` whose current counter is too far in the
future, verify both failures send parsing NACKs with no typed batch and leave
the provider-session bytes unchanged, then decrypt the next valid
current-ratchet `msg` to prove the far-future attempts did not mutate the
receiving chain, remote ratchet key, skipped-key state, or provider identity.

skipped counter-2 session `msg` through the same processor and verifies ACK,

The spawned live raw-node Signal group invalid sender-key record validation
slice added after the spawned remote-ratchet pairing coverage promotes hostile
persisted sender-key record validation into
`spawn_incoming_processor_with_signal_provider`. The new client regression
seeds a valid receiver-side sender-key record for a group `skmsg`,
raw-overwrites that store row with mismatched-signing-key, duplicate-state,
duplicate skipped-iteration, and future skipped-iteration
`SenderKeyRecordStructure` bytes, injects the group sender-key payload through
the spawned processor for each invalid row, verifies message parsing NACKs
without any typed batch, and proves each invalid record is preserved
byte-for-byte. It then restores the valid record, injects the same group
`skmsg` through the same processor, and verifies ACK, plaintext group typed
batch persistence, sender participant attribution, and sender-key record
advancement. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_preserves_state_after_invalid_sender_key_record --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_preserves_state_after_invalid_sender_key_record --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

slice added after the spawned live invalid sender-key record coverage promotes

row with mismatched-signing-key, duplicate-state, duplicate skipped-iteration,
and future skipped-iteration `SenderKeyRecordStructure` bytes, injects an
`offline` node containing the group sender-key payload for each invalid row,
verifies child message parsing NACKs without any typed batch, and proves each
invalid record is preserved byte-for-byte. It then restores the valid record,
injects the same offline group `skmsg` through the same processor, and verifies
ACK, plaintext group typed batch persistence, sender participant attribution,
and sender-key record advancement. It was verified in the current VM with:

The spawned live raw-node Signal group out-of-order sender-key slice added
after the spawned invalid sender-key record coverage promotes skipped
sender-message-key handling into `spawn_incoming_processor_with_signal_provider`.
The new client regression processes the second group `skmsg` before the first
through the spawned processor, verifies the receiver-side sender-key record
advances while retaining the first iteration as a skipped key, rejects a
valid-signature but undecryptable first message without any typed batch or
sender-key row mutation, decrypts the original first message from the stored
skipped key, and then rejects a replay of that first message while preserving
the advanced sender-key row. It verifies ACKs, sender participant attribution,
plaintext group typed batch persistence, and message event storage for both
successful group messages. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_decrypts_out_of_order_sender_key_messages --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_decrypts_out_of_order_sender_key_messages --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

sender-key record advances while retaining the first iteration as a skipped

The spawned live raw-node Signal group sender-key distribution recovery slice
added after the spawned offline out-of-order sender-key coverage promotes
repository-backed distribution fallback into
`spawn_incoming_processor_with_signal_provider`. The new client regression
stores only the sender-key distribution for a group sender, then injects live
group `skmsg` payloads through the spawned processor while the provider
sender-key row is missing, deleted, and corrupted. Each case verifies a message
ACK, plaintext group typed batch persistence, sender participant attribution,
message event storage, unchanged repository distribution bytes, and recovered
provider sender-key advancement with the expected skipped sender-message keys.

The spawned live/offline raw-node Signal group stale sender-key distribution
recovery slice added after the distribution recovery pair promotes
stale-record fallback into both branches of
`spawn_incoming_processor_with_signal_provider`. The new client regressions seed
a stale receiver-side sender-key record, store a fresh sender-key distribution
in the repository, inject a fresh group `skmsg` through the spawned live path
and through an `offline` child message, and verify ACKs, plaintext group typed
batch persistence, sender participant attribution, message event storage,
unchanged repository distribution bytes, and repaired provider sender-key
records that prefer the fresh state while preserving the stale state. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_recovers_stale_sender_key_record_from_distribution --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_recovers_stale_sender_key_record_from_distribution --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_recovers_offline_stale_sender_key_record_from_distribution --quiet`
