- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The spawned live/offline raw-node Signal group sender-key distribution-message
processing slice added after the stale distribution recovery pair promotes the
inbound distribution-processing hook into both branches of
`spawn_incoming_processor_with_signal_provider`. The new client regressions
inject live and `offline` plaintext group messages carrying both regular and
fast-ratchet `SenderKeyDistributionMessage` fields, verify ACKs, plaintext
typed batch persistence, sender participant attribution, message event storage,
and `sender_key_distribution_count`, then prove the provider sender-key record
contains both states while the repository keeps the last processed fast-ratchet
distribution bytes for later `skmsg` recovery. It was verified in the current
VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_processes_sender_key_distribution_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_processes_sender_key_distribution_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_processes_offline_sender_key_distribution_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_processes_offline_sender_key_distribution_message --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The spawned live raw-node Signal signed-pre-key-only slice added after the
explicit offline signed-only facade coverage promotes the same
no-one-time-pre-key inbound `pkmsg` success path into
`spawn_incoming_processor_with_signal_provider`. The new client regression
stores an unrelated local one-time pre-key, injects a live signed-pre-key-only
`pkmsg` through the mock connection stream, verifies the spawned processor
sends the message ACK and emits/persists the plaintext typed batch, proves the
unrelated pre-key remains available and provider identity/session records are
persisted, replays the same signed-only `pkmsg` and verifies a parsing NACK
without any typed batch or state mutation, then injects a following session
`msg` to prove the accepted session continues through the automatic receive
loop. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_accepts_signed_pre_key_only_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_accepts_signed_pre_key_only_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_inbound_pre_key_without_one_time_pre_key --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_inbound_pre_key_without_one_time_pre_key --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The spawned offline raw-node Signal signed-pre-key-only slice added after the
spawned live signed-only coverage promotes the same no-one-time-pre-key inbound
`pkmsg` success path into the automatic incoming processor's offline branch.
The new client regression stores an unrelated local one-time pre-key, injects
an `offline` node containing a signed-pre-key-only `pkmsg` through the mock
connection stream, verifies the spawned processor sends the child message ACK
and emits/persists the plaintext typed batch, proves the unrelated pre-key
remains available and provider identity/session records are persisted, replays
the same signed-only `pkmsg` inside another `offline` node and verifies a
parsing NACK without any typed batch or state mutation, then injects a following
offline session `msg` to prove the accepted session continues through the
automatic offline receive loop. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_accepts_offline_signed_pre_key_only_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_accepts_offline_signed_pre_key_only_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_inbound_pre_key_without_one_time_pre_key --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_inbound_pre_key_without_one_time_pre_key --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The spawned offline raw-node Signal missing-one-time-pre-key slice added after
the explicit offline failure coverage promotes the same provider failure class
into the automatic incoming processor. A new client regression starts
`spawn_incoming_processor_with_signal_provider`, injects an `offline` node
containing a valid `pkmsg` whose local one-time pre-key has already been
consumed, verifies the spawned processor sends a parsing NACK for the child
message, proves no local pre-key, sender session, or sender identity record is
created, then injects a plaintext follow-up message and verifies the same
processor still ACKs, emits, and persists that message. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_rejects_offline_missing_one_time_pre_key --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_rejects_offline_missing_one_time_pre_key --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_rejects_inbound_pre_key_when_one_time_pre_key_missing --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_rejects_inbound_pre_key_when_one_time_pre_key_missing --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The spawned live raw-node Signal missing-one-time-pre-key slice added after the
spawned offline failure coverage promotes the same provider failure class into
the non-offline automatic incoming processor path. A new client regression
starts `spawn_incoming_processor_with_signal_provider`, injects a live `pkmsg`
whose local one-time pre-key has already been consumed, verifies the spawned
processor sends a parsing NACK for the message, proves no local pre-key, sender
session, or sender identity record is created, then injects a plaintext
follow-up message and verifies the same processor still ACKs, emits, and
persists that message. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_rejects_missing_one_time_pre_key --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_rejects_missing_one_time_pre_key --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_rejects_inbound_pre_key_when_one_time_pre_key_missing --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_rejects_inbound_pre_key_when_one_time_pre_key_missing --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The spawned live raw-node Signal pre-key identity-change slice added after the
spawned live missing-pre-key coverage promotes the store-backed inbound
identity transition guard into the non-offline automatic incoming processor
path. A new client regression seeds an existing provider session and identity
for the sender, injects a live `pkmsg` from a different sender identity through
`spawn_incoming_processor_with_signal_provider`, verifies the spawned processor
sends a parsing NACK, proves the local one-time pre-key plus existing session
and identity records remain unchanged, then injects a plaintext follow-up
message and verifies the same processor still ACKs, emits, and persists that
message without mutating the provider records. It was verified in the current
VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_rejects_pre_key_identity_change --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_rejects_pre_key_identity_change --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_preserves_pre_key_after_inbound_identity_change --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_preserves_pre_key_after_inbound_identity_change --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The spawned offline raw-node Signal pre-key identity-change slice added after
the spawned live identity-change coverage promotes the same store-backed
inbound identity transition guard into the automatic incoming processor's
offline branch. A new client regression seeds an existing provider session and
identity for the sender, injects an `offline` node containing a `pkmsg` from a
different sender identity through `spawn_incoming_processor_with_signal_provider`,
verifies the spawned processor sends a parsing NACK for the child message,
proves the local one-time pre-key plus existing session and identity records
remain unchanged, then injects a plaintext follow-up message and verifies the
same processor still ACKs, emits, and persists that message without mutating
the provider records. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`

The ignored live Signal-provider receive smoke slice added after the spawned
pre-key identity-change coverage broadens the live e2e harness from send-only
validation into the automatic raw-node receive pump. The new ignored
`live_signal_receive_smoke` test connects a paired SQLite-backed client, starts
`spawn_incoming_processor_with_signal_provider` on the validated live
connection, waits with a bounded `WA_RECEIVE_TIMEOUT_SECS` timeout for a typed
`MessagesUpsert` or batch message-upsert event, and optionally filters the
manual run to `WA_RECEIVE_REMOTE_JID`. This does not replace the broader live
send/receive parity work, but it gives operators a compile-checked opt-in path
for proving that live websocket raw nodes can be decrypted, persisted, and
emitted through the public typed event stream. The slice also narrowed a test
module import so the sqlite/websocket live-e2e feature mix remains warning-free.
It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket live_signal_receive_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket live_signal_receive_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket --test live_e2e -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket --test live_e2e -- -D warnings`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`

The public live Signal receive example slice added after the ignored live
receive smoke turns the compile-checked live receive harness into an operator
workflow under `crates/wa-client/examples/live_receive.rs`. The example opens a
paired SQLite-backed client only when `WA_RECEIVE=1` is set, starts
`spawn_incoming_processor_with_signal_provider` on the validated live
connection, waits for typed `MessagesUpsert` or batch message-upsert events,
and supports the same `WA_RECEIVE_TIMEOUT_SECS` and `WA_RECEIVE_REMOTE_JID`
controls as the ignored smoke test. The `wa-client` example manifest, README
commands/environment notes, feature support matrix, and public-example
foundation summary were updated so live receive usage is discoverable while
remaining explicit and opt-in. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo check -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket --example live_receive --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket --example live_receive --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket --example live_receive -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket --example live_receive -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket live_signal_receive_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket live_signal_receive_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`

The public live pairing-code example slice added after the live Signal receive
example promotes the existing pairing-code request and link-code completion
helpers into an operator workflow under
`crates/wa-client/examples/live_pairing_code.rs`. The example opens a
SQLite-backed client only when `WA_PHONE_NUMBER` is set, rejects already
registered sessions so users pick a fresh `WA_SESSION_DB`, opens a live
websocket, sends `send_pairing_code_request`, prints the generated or custom
pairing code, then waits with bounded `WA_PAIRING_TIMEOUT_SECS` for the
`link_code_companion_reg` notification and persists the completed credentials
through `respond_to_link_code_companion_reg_notification`. The `wa-client`
example manifest, README commands/environment notes, feature support matrix,
and foundation summary were updated so pairing-code login has a discoverable
live workflow while broader live pairing validation remains open. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo check -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket --example live_pairing_code --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket --example live_pairing_code --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket --example live_pairing_code -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket --example live_pairing_code -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_pairing_code_request_writes_encoded_node --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_pairing_code_request_writes_encoded_node --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise respond_to_link_code_companion_reg_notification_finishes_pairing --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise respond_to_link_code_companion_reg_notification_finishes_pairing --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise pairing --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise pairing --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The public live history-sync event-pump slice added after the live pairing-code
example promotes the history-sync notification scanner and
`download_process_and_emit_history_sync_events` facade into both an operator
workflow and an ignored live smoke. The new
`crates/wa-client/examples/live_history_sync.rs` example opens a paired
SQLite-backed client only when `WA_HISTORY_SYNC=1` is set, fetches media
connection hosts, starts the Signal-provider incoming processor, watches live
`MessagesUpsert`/batch events for history-sync notifications, downloads
external history payloads through `HttpMediaTransport`, and emits/persists the
processed history batches with bounded `WA_HISTORY_SYNC_TIMEOUT_SECS` plus
optional `WA_HISTORY_SYNC_FALLBACK_HOST` and `WA_HISTORY_SYNC_LATEST` controls.
The ignored `live_history_sync_smoke` test covers the same event-pump path under
`WA_LIVE_E2E=1` and `http-media`. The example manifest, README, feature support
matrix, live-smoke summary, and public-example foundation summary were updated
so live history-sync processing is discoverable while broader history/app-state
parity remains open. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo check -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --example live_history_sync --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --example live_history_sync --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --example live_history_sync -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --example live_history_sync -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media live_history_sync_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media live_history_sync_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --test live_e2e -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --test live_e2e -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise,http-media history_sync_event_scanner_processes_message_notification --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise,http-media history_sync_event_scanner_processes_message_notification --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise,http-media history_sync_event_pump_downloads_persists_and_emits_batch --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise,http-media history_sync_event_pump_downloads_persists_and_emits_batch --quiet`

The public live chat-pin app-state mutation slice added after the live
history-sync event-pump slice promotes the existing upload-and-apply chat
mutation facade into a guarded operator workflow and ignored live smoke. The
new `crates/wa-client/examples/live_chat_pin.rs` example requires
`WA_CHAT_PIN_JID` and `WA_APP_STATE_KEY_ID_HEX`, optionally loads
`WA_APP_STATE_KEY_DATA_HEX` from the environment when the matching app-state
sync key is not already stored, opens a paired SQLite-backed websocket session,
uploads a `set_chat_pinned_and_apply` patch against the `regular_low`
collection, persists the next app-state patch state, and emits/persists the
local chat update after server acceptance. The ignored `live_chat_pin_smoke`
test covers the same path under `WA_LIVE_E2E=1`, with optional
`WA_CHAT_PINNED=false` and `WA_CHAT_PIN_TIMESTAMP_MS` controls. The example
manifest, README, feature support matrix, live-smoke summary, and
public-example foundation summary were updated so app-state chat mutation live
validation is discoverable while broader app-state retry/resync and chat/profile
workflow parity remains open. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo check -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket --example live_chat_pin --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket --example live_chat_pin --quiet`

- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket live_chat_pin_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket --test live_e2e -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket --test live_e2e -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise chat_mutation_upload_and_apply_persists_state_and_events --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise chat_mutation_upload_and_apply_persists_state_and_events --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise maps_decoded_app_state_mutations_to_store_event_batch --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise maps_decoded_app_state_mutations_to_store_event_batch --quiet`

The group invite-accept fallback notification slice added after the live
chat-pin app-state mutation slice tightens receive-side group notification
parity for protocol forms that carry the accepted member only on the parent
`notification participant` attribute. `accept` children without explicit
`participant` children now synthesize validated `participants_accept` fields from
the parent participant plus PN/LID/username aliases, and the derived
`GroupParticipantAccept` message stub keeps the same serialized participant
metadata as explicit-child accept notifications. It was verified in the current
VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise maps_group_updates_to_group_notification_message_stubs --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise maps_group_notification_invite_lifecycle_forms --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise maps_group_updates_to_group_notification_message_stubs --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise maps_group_notification_invite_lifecycle_forms --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise group_notification --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise group_notification --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise receive_persistence_generates_group_notification_message_stub --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise receive_persistence_generates_group_notification_message_stub --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The group/community v4 invite-accept return-shape slice added after the
invite-accept fallback notification slice aligns the Rust facades with Baileys'
accepted-invite result semantics. Core now exposes
`parse_group_invite_v4_accept_result` and the community wrapper to read and
validate the accepted group/community JID from the result stanza `from` field
with nested group fallback, while the existing generic v4 parser remains for
boolean revoke success checks. The group and community client
`accept_*_invite_v4*` facades now return `Option<String>` and still emit/persist
accepted-invite message side effects when the accepted JID is present. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise parses_participant_and_join_request_results --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise parses_participant_and_join_request_results --quiet`

The normal community invite-accept result slice added after the v4 invite-accept
return-shape work fixes another Baileys response-shape mismatch in the community
facade. `parse_community_accept_invite_result` now reads and validates
`<community jid="...">` and bare `id` result children before returning the
accepted community JID, instead of delegating to the group-only `<group>` parser.
The client community IQ regression now covers `accept_community_invite` end to
end against that result shape. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise builds_community_links_membership_invites_and_settings --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise builds_community_links_membership_invites_and_settings --quiet`

- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The group/community join-request metadata result slice added after the normal
community invite-accept result work closes another query-response parity gap
with Baileys membership approval request lists. `GroupJoinRequest` now carries
optional PN, LID, and username metadata, and the shared group/community list
parser preserves `phoneNumber`/`pn`, `lidJid`, and `participantUsername` aliases
alongside the already-supported `requestMethod` alias. Focused core tests cover
the raw parser, and group/community client mock-transport tests cover facade
round trips. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise parses_participant_and_join_request_results --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise parses_participant_and_join_request_results --quiet`

- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise community_methods_use_community_iqs_and_parse_results --quiet`

The community linked-groups parent-resolution slice added after the
join-request metadata result work aligns another community facade edge with
Baileys' `communityFetchLinkedGroups` behavior. The new
`CommunityLinkedGroups` result carries the resolved `community_jid`,
`is_community` flag, and linked groups, while
`Client::fetch_community_linked_groups_resolved` first fetches group metadata
for the supplied JID and, when it is a subgroup, queries `sub_groups` on the
`linked_parent` community instead of the subgroup itself. Existing
`fetch_community_linked_groups` remains the low-level direct query helper. It
was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise community_methods_use_community_iqs_and_parse_results --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise community_methods_use_community_iqs_and_parse_results --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The community metadata child addressing-mode slice added after the linked-groups
parent-resolution work aligns the shared metadata parser with Baileys'
`extractCommunityMetadata` child-node `addressing_mode` shape. Group metadata now
falls back from the legacy `addressing_mode` attribute to an
`<addressing_mode>` child text value before deriving `GroupAddressingMode`, and
community core/client regressions cover a LID-addressed community response. It
was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise parses_community_metadata_participating_and_linked_groups --quiet`

The participant action result content slice added after the community
addressing-mode metadata work aligns group and community participant update
results with Baileys' `content: p` return shape. `GroupParticipantChange` now
keeps an optional raw participant result node, populated for add/remove/promote/
demote participant operations while membership approval approve/reject results
remain status/JID-only like Baileys. Core and client regressions cover both the
preserved raw content on participant updates and the absent content on
join-request action results. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise parses_participant_and_join_request_results --quiet`

The community create-result stub slice added after the participant result content
work aligns `create_community` and `create_community_group` with Baileys'
`parseGroupResult` flow. Core now parses the returned `<group>` child id/jid
stub into a normalized group JID, and the client fetches full group metadata for
that JID before returning while retaining the direct `<community>` metadata
fallback used by older mocks. Core and client regressions cover bare-id and full
JID stub forms, invalid non-group JIDs, follow-up metadata IQs, and subgroup
linked-parent preservation. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise builds_community_links_membership_invites_and_settings --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise builds_community_links_membership_invites_and_settings --quiet`

The group participating error propagation slice added after the community
create-result stub work hardens the group dirty-refresh invariant. Group
participating snapshot parsing now returns stanza errors instead of silently
treating error IQs as empty group lists, and the client dirty-refresh regression
verifies the shared group dirty bit is not cleaned after a failed participating
query. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise parses_group_metadata --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise parses_group_metadata --quiet`

- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise refresh_dirty_groups_fetches_groups_then_cleans_dirty_bit --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`

The group metadata alias validation slice added after the group participating
error propagation work tightens parser invariants for metadata used by
sender-key, retry, and community-resolution paths. Group metadata participants
and join-request rows now validate optional PN/LID aliases as participant/account
JIDs, and `linked_parent` metadata is rejected unless it is a group JID. Focused
core regressions cover malformed participant aliases, malformed join-request
aliases, and invalid linked-parent metadata. It was verified in the current VM
with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise parses_group_metadata --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise parses_group_metadata --quiet`

The Signal pre-key whisper required-field wire validation slice added after the
group metadata alias validation work tightens the project-owned Signal provider
boundary. The internal pre-key whisper wire struct now preserves protobuf field
presence for `registration_id` and `signed_pre_key_id`, so malformed frames that
omit either field fail with explicit protocol errors instead of silently
defaulting the missing value to zero. Focused core regressions cover both
missing-field decoder paths, and the deterministic Signal manifest now includes
matching malformed-wire vectors exercised by `wa-testkit`. It was verified in
the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise signal_wire_pre_key_whisper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise signal_wire_pre_key_whisper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise pre_key_session --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise pre_key_session --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-testkit --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The Signal inner whisper counter-presence slice added after the pre-key
required-field validation work aligns the project-owned whisper message codec
with libsignal's protobuf presence behavior. The internal whisper wire struct
now stores `counter` and `previous_counter` with field presence, emits explicit
zero counter fields on encode, rejects a missing `counter` after ratchet-key
normalization, and decodes an absent `previous_counter` as zero. Focused core
tests cover explicit zero encoding, missing-counter rejection, and the
missing-previous-counter fallback. The deterministic Signal manifest now
includes a malformed missing-counter vector and refreshed provider/pre-key
message goldens for the explicit `previous_counter = 0` wire tag. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise signal_wire_whisper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise signal_wire_whisper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise pre_key_session --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise pre_key_session --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-testkit --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The Signal sender-key record required-field fixture slice added after the inner
whisper counter-presence work broadens deterministic provider-state wire
coverage. Existing decoder branches for malformed `SenderKeyRecordStructure`
frames are now pinned in core regressions and the shared Signal manifest for
missing state chain-key containers, chain-key seeds, signing-key containers,
and skipped-message-key seeds, alongside the already-covered missing state id,
chain iteration, signing public key, and skipped iteration cases. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise signal_sender_key_record_rejects_missing_required_wire_fields --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise signal_sender_key_record_rejects_missing_required_wire_fields --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise signal_sender_key_record --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise signal_sender_key_record --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-testkit --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The stateful Signal sender-key required-field fuzz slice added after the
deterministic sender-key record required-field fixtures extends the
`signal_stateful_records` fuzz target with structured malformed
`SenderKeyRecordStructure` records. The fuzz driver now manually emits compact
protobuf rows for missing chain-key containers, chain-key seeds, signing-key
containers, and skipped-message-key seeds, then asserts the public sender-key
record decoder preserves the expected stable protocol errors while also driving
a matching valid structured row through the decoder. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bins`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target cargo clippy --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bins -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --check`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The Signal wire required-field fuzz slice added after the stateful sender-key
required-field fuzz work extends `signal_wire_decode` with structured
protobuf frames for whisper and pre-key whisper messages. The fuzz driver now
asserts that valid generated whisper frames round-trip, missing whisper
`counter` fields keep the stable protocol error, missing whisper
`previous_counter` fields decode as zero, explicit zero counters remain present
on the wire, and pre-key whisper frames without `registration_id` or
`signed_pre_key_id` keep their stable protocol errors. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin signal_wire_decode`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target cargo clippy --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin signal_wire_decode -- -D warnings`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bins`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target cargo clippy --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bins -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise signal_wire_whisper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise signal_wire_whisper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise signal_wire_pre_key_whisper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise signal_wire_pre_key_whisper --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --check`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The persisted provider-session malformed-wire fixture slice added after the
Signal wire required-field fuzz work promotes native Signal provider-session
record truncation coverage from core-only unit tests into the shared Signal
fixture manifest. `wa-testkit` now understands `provider_session_invalid_wire`
vectors and checks raw persisted-record bytes for missing remote identity-key
length, truncated root keys, missing previous counters, missing remote-ratchet
flags, missing skipped-key counts, and missing remote-ratchet flags after a
receiving-chain section while preserving the stable decoder error text. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise signal_provider_session_record --quiet`

- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`

The Signal provider-session malformed-record fuzz slice added after the
persisted provider-session malformed-wire fixture work extends
`signal_wire_decode` with structured native persisted-record frames. The fuzz
driver now asserts a generated valid provider-session record decodes with the
expected registration, sending counter, and previous counter, then synthesizes
the malformed missing remote identity-key length, truncated root key, missing
previous counter, missing remote-ratchet flag, missing skipped-key count, and a
missing remote-ratchet flag after a receiving-chain section while preserving the
stable decoder error text. It was verified in the current VM

The Signal sender-key distribution malformed-wire fuzz slice added after the
provider-session malformed-record fuzz work extends `signal_wire_decode` with

key, and signing key, then synthesizes bad message-version, bad
ciphertext-version, missing id, missing iteration, missing chain-key, missing

The Signal sender-key message malformed-wire fuzz slice added after the
sender-key distribution malformed-wire fuzz work extends `signal_wire_decode`
with structured sender-key message protobuf frames plus fixed-size signatures.
The fuzz driver now asserts a generated valid sender-key message decodes with
the expected id, iteration, ciphertext, and signature, then synthesizes

The Signal sender-key record required-field malformed-wire fuzz slice added

The Signal pre-key whisper inner explicit-zero counter fixture and fuzz slice
added after the plain whisper explicit-zero work extends that field-presence
invariant through pre-key envelopes. The manifest now pins a
`pre_key_whisper_message` whose inner whisper explicitly encodes both counter
fields as zero, and `signal_wire_decode` asserts that generated pre-key
whisper frames with explicit inner zero counters are already the public
encoder's canonical output. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise signal_wire_pre_key_whisper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise signal_wire_pre_key_whisper --quiet`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin signal_wire_decode`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target cargo clippy --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin signal_wire_decode -- -D warnings`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bins`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target cargo clippy --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bins -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --check`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`
- `rm -rf /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The Signal whisper unknown-field canonicalization fixture and fuzz slice added
after the pre-key whisper inner explicit-zero work promotes another protobuf
compatibility invariant into the shared manifest. The new
`whisper_message_unknown_field` fixture decodes a valid whisper frame with a
trailing unknown varint field, verifies the public decoder ignores that field,
and pins the public encoder's canonical re-encoding without the unknown data;
`signal_wire_decode` now asserts the same generated accepted-wire path. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise signal_wire_whisper --quiet`

- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target cargo clippy --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bins -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --check`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`
- `rm -rf /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

whisper message, verifies both decode to the expected message, and checks that
the public pre-key whisper encoder drops the unknown data on canonical

The Signal sender-key message unknown-field verification slice added after the
nested sender-key record unknown-field work aligns the byte-verification API
with the canonical decoded sender-key message verifier. The byte verifier now
checks the canonical protobuf payload reconstructed from decoded fields, so a
valid signed sender-key message remains verifiable when an accepted unknown
protobuf field is present before the fixed signature trailer. The focused core
regression also proves decode drops that field and canonical re-encode restores
the original signed bytes. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise signal_sender_key_message_signs_verifies_and_decodes_wire_frame --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise signal_sender_key_message_signs_verifies_and_decodes_wire_frame --quiet`

- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`
- `rm -rf /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The Signal provider-session unknown-field decrypt fixture and fuzz slice added
after the sender-key message unknown-field verification work extends accepted
whisper-message unknown-field behavior through stateful one-to-one provider
decrypt. The shared Signal manifest now pins an existing provider-session
message with an appended unknown protobuf field, proves canonical decode drops
the field, and verifies decrypting that unknown-field wire message yields the
same plaintext and advanced provider record as the canonical message. The
`signal_stateful_records` fuzz target now generates the same current-chain
decrypt invariant. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --check`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`
- `rm -rf /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The Signal pre-key session unknown-field decrypt fixture and fuzz slice added
after the provider-session unknown-field decrypt work extends the same accepted
protobuf behavior through inbound pre-key session decrypt. The shared Signal
manifest now pins both one-time and signed-pre-key-only pre-key session
messages with an unknown outer pre-key envelope field and a separate generated
envelope carrying an unknown field inside the embedded whisper message; all
forms canonicalize back to the original pre-key message and decrypt to the same
plaintext and receiver provider-session record. The `signal_stateful_records`
fuzz target now generates the same one-time and signed-pre-key-only outer and
inner unknown-field pre-key decrypt invariants, using the generated protobuf
envelope shape through an explicit local `wa-proto` fuzz dependency. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise pre_key_session --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise pre_key_session --quiet`

- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target cargo clippy --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin signal_stateful_records -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --check`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`
- `rm -rf /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The store-backed Signal pre-key unknown-field receive slice added after the
pre-key session unknown-field decrypt fixture and fuzz work promotes that
accepted wire behavior through the `SignalMessageCodec` runtime boundary. The
one-time and signed-pre-key-only store-backed inbound pre-key tests now append
an accepted unknown protobuf field to the initial `pkmsg`, prove it
canonicalizes back to the original pre-key message, then decrypt through the
store-backed provider while preserving the existing plaintext, local pre-key
consumption or retention, identity/session persistence, replay rejection, and
replacement-session assertions. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_inbound_pre_key_then_session_messages --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_inbound_pre_key_then_session_messages --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_inbound_pre_key_without_one_time_pre_key --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_inbound_pre_key_without_one_time_pre_key --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise pre_key_session --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise pre_key_session --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`
- `rm -rf /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The spawned incoming-processor Signal pre-key unknown-field receive slice added
after the store-backed provider boundary work carries the same accepted `pkmsg`
wire form through the high-level client stream processor. The live and offline
raw one-time pre-key processor tests, plus the live and offline signed-pre-key
only processor tests, now wrap the initial and replayed `pkmsg` payloads with
an accepted unknown protobuf field and assert canonical re-encoding before the
message enters the client node processor; the existing plaintext, event
persistence, one-time pre-key consumption or signed-only retention, session and
identity persistence, replay rejection, and follow-up `msg` decrypt assertions
remain unchanged. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise raw_pre_key_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise signed_pre_key_only_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise raw_pre_key_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise signed_pre_key_only_message --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`
- `rm -rf /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The explicit client receive-facade Signal pre-key unknown-field receive slice
added after the spawned incoming-processor boundary work carries the same
accepted `pkmsg` wire form through `process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider`
and `process_offline_node_with_signal_provider`. The live one-time pre-key
facade, live and offline signed-pre-key-only facades, and the offline
one-time event-response facade now wrap their accepted `pkmsg` payloads with
an unknown protobuf field and assert canonical re-encoding before the node is
processed; the existing ACK/NACK, plaintext event persistence, one-time pre-key
consumption or signed-only retention, session and identity persistence, replay
rejection, follow-up `msg` decrypt, and event-response update assertions remain
unchanged. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider_decrypts_pre_key_message_and_replies --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider_accepts_signed_pre_key_only_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_offline_node_with_signal_provider_accepts_signed_pre_key_only_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_offline_node_with_signal_provider_decrypts_event_response_from_stored_creation_secret --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider_decrypts_pre_key_message_and_replies --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider_accepts_signed_pre_key_only_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_offline_node_with_signal_provider_accepts_signed_pre_key_only_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_offline_node_with_signal_provider_decrypts_event_response_from_stored_creation_secret --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`
- `rm -rf /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The status/broadcast retry-resend caching slice added after the explicit
receive-facade work hardens the public status send path. A new client
regression sends a text status to a device-specific audience recipient,
verifies the outbound stanza targets `status@broadcast`, then plans and
executes a retry receipt whose chat JID is `status@broadcast`. The retry replay
uses the cached original story payload, sends it only to the requesting device,
keeps the payload as a regular status message instead of a `device_sent`
wrapper, marks the retry successful, and removes the cached message after the
successful replay. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_status_text_caches_status_broadcast_for_retry_resend --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_status_text_caches_status_broadcast_for_retry_resend --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`
- `rm -rf /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The store-backed Signal-provider status retry-resend caching slice added after
the mock-encryptor status retry work promotes the same `status@broadcast`
retry path through the real provider-backed encryptor. A new client regression
sends a text status with `send_status_text_with_signal_provider`, proves the
initial status is a pre-key `pkmsg`, decrypts it as a regular unwrapped status
message, then executes a retry resend for the same `status@broadcast` chat and
proves the replay uses the established-session `msg` path to the requesting
device while still decrypting to the original unwrapped story payload. It also
pins successful retry accounting and removal of the cached status after replay.
It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_status_text_with_signal_provider_replays_cached_status_retry --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_status_text_with_signal_provider_replays_cached_status_retry --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`
- `rm -rf /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The all-device status retry-resend caching slice added after the
store-backed participant status retry coverage hardens `status@broadcast`
replays when a bare requester asks for all of its devices. A new client
regression sends one text status to both a remote primary and companion device
plus the local linked device, proves the initial status fanout stores the
unwrapped remote payloads and a `device_sent_message` wrapper for the linked
own device, then executes a bare-requester retry receipt for
`status@broadcast`. The retry path performs a fresh all-device lookup, replays
the cached story to the same three recipients, preserves the linked-own-device
wrapper destination as `status@broadcast`, marks the retry successful, and
removes the cached status after replay. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_status_text_caches_status_broadcast_for_all_device_retry_resend --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_status_text_caches_status_broadcast_for_all_device_retry_resend --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`
- `test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The store-backed Signal-provider all-device status retry-resend caching slice
added after the mock all-device status retry coverage promotes the same bare
requester `status@broadcast` replay through the real provider-backed encryptor.
A new client regression sends one text status through
`send_status_text_with_signal_provider` to a remote primary, remote companion,
and local linked device, decrypts the initial `pkmsg` fanout for all three
recipients, then executes a bare-requester retry receipt for
`status@broadcast`. The retry path performs a fresh all-device lookup, emits
established-session `msg` replays at counter 2 for every recipient, preserves
the linked-own-device `device_sent_message` destination as `status@broadcast`,
marks the retry successful, and removes the cached status after replay. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_status_text_with_signal_provider_replays_cached_status_all_device_retry --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_status_text_with_signal_provider_replays_cached_status_all_device_retry --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`
- `test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The media status retry-resend caching slice added after the store-backed
all-device text status retry coverage broadens the mock-encryptor
`status@broadcast` retry path beyond text payloads. A new client regression
sends an uploaded JPEG status through the generic `send_status_message` facade,
verifies the initial broadcast stanza is a media status with the expected
participant and image metadata, then plans and executes a device-scoped retry
receipt for the same `status@broadcast` chat. The replay uses the cached image
payload, keeps the stanza as media, sends only to the requesting device, marks
the retry successful, and removes the cached media status after replay. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_status_image_caches_media_status_broadcast_for_retry_resend --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_status_image_caches_media_status_broadcast_for_retry_resend --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`
- `test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The store-backed Signal-provider media status retry-resend caching slice added
after the mock media status retry work promotes the same non-text
`status@broadcast` replay through the real provider-backed encryptor. A new
client regression sends an uploaded JPEG status through
`send_status_message_with_signal_provider` to a device-specific audience
recipient, decrypts the initial media `pkmsg`, then executes a retry receipt
for the same `status@broadcast` chat. The retry replay uses the established
provider session `msg` path at counter 2, decrypts to the original image status
payload, keeps successful retry accounting, and removes the cached media status
after replay. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_status_image_with_signal_provider_replays_cached_media_status_retry --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_status_image_with_signal_provider_replays_cached_media_status_retry --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`
- `test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The all-device media status retry-resend caching slice added after the
device-scoped media retry work closes the remaining media half of the
`status@broadcast` all-device retry path for both mock and store-backed Signal
sends. New client regressions send uploaded JPEG statuses to a remote primary,
remote companion, and local linked device, then execute bare-requester retry
receipts for the same `status@broadcast` chat. The mock-encryptor regression
proves cached media payload replay and linked-own-device wrapper preservation
across a fresh all-device lookup. The provider-backed regression decrypts the
initial media `pkmsg` fanout for all three recipients, verifies established
`msg` replays at counter 2 for every recipient, preserves the linked-own-device
`device_sent_message` destination as `status@broadcast`, marks retries
successful, and removes the cached media statuses after replay. It was verified
in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_status_image_caches_media_status_broadcast_for_all_device_retry_resend --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_status_image_with_signal_provider_replays_cached_media_status_all_device_retry --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_status_image_caches_media_status_broadcast_for_all_device_retry_resend --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_status_image_with_signal_provider_replays_cached_media_status_all_device_retry --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`
- `test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The store-backed provider-backed half of that media status all-device retry
slice now also runs from an account with both `999:7@s.whatsapp.net` and
`ownlid@lid`. Its shared USync fixture returns the same local linked device
through both identities during the initial media status fanout and during retry
rediscovery, while the session query and replay participant lists remain
collapsed to `111@s.whatsapp.net`, `111:1@s.whatsapp.net`, and
`999:8@s.whatsapp.net`. The regression also proves no duplicate
`ownlid:8@lid` provider session is stored after the all-device replay succeeds.
The PN/LID tightening was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_status_image_with_signal_provider_replays_cached_media_status_all_device_retry --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_status_image_with_signal_provider_replays_cached_media_status_all_device_retry --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The status poll retry sidecar-suppression slice added after media all-device
retry coverage pins the `status@broadcast` reporting-token boundary during
cached retry replays. A new client regression sends a secret poll status through
the generic status facade, verifies the original broadcast carries a
`reporting/reporting_token` sidecar and the cached encrypted payload still
contains the poll message secret, then executes a device-scoped retry receipt
for the same `status@broadcast` chat. The replay keeps the cached poll payload
and message secret, drops `reporting` and `tctoken` sidecars, marks the retry
successful, and removes the cached status poll after replay. It was verified in
the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_status_poll_retry_replays_cached_poll_without_reporting_sidecar --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_status_poll_retry_replays_cached_poll_without_reporting_sidecar --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`
- `test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The store-backed Signal-provider status poll retry sidecar-suppression slice
added after the mock status poll retry work promotes the same poll replay
boundary through the real provider-backed encryptor. A new client regression
sends a secret poll status with `send_status_message_with_signal_provider`,
decrypts the initial `pkmsg` poll payload while proving the broadcast carries
`reporting/reporting_token`, then executes a device-scoped retry receipt for
the same `status@broadcast` chat. The retry replay uses the established
provider session `msg` path at counter 2, preserves the cached poll payload and
message secret, suppresses `reporting` and `tctoken` sidecars, marks the retry
successful, and removes the cached status poll after replay. It was verified in
the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_status_poll_with_signal_provider_replays_cached_poll_without_reporting_sidecar --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_status_poll_with_signal_provider_replays_cached_poll_without_reporting_sidecar --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`
- `test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The all-device status poll retry sidecar-suppression slice added after the
device-scoped poll retry coverage closes the bare-requester `status@broadcast`
poll replay path for both mock and store-backed Signal sends. New client
regressions send secret poll statuses to a remote primary, remote companion,
and local linked device, then execute bare-requester retry receipts for the
same `status@broadcast` chat. The mock-encryptor regression proves cached poll
payload replay, linked-own-device wrapper preservation, and `reporting`/
`tctoken` suppression across a fresh all-device lookup. The provider-backed
regression decrypts the initial `pkmsg` fanout for all three recipients,
verifies established-session `msg` replays at counter 2 for every recipient,
preserves the linked-own-device `device_sent_message` destination as
`status@broadcast`, suppresses `reporting` and `tctoken`, marks retries
successful, and removes the cached status polls after replay. It was verified
in the current VM with:

The status event reporting-token and all-device retry sidecar-suppression slice
added after poll retry coverage closes the event half of the
`status@broadcast` secret-message replay boundary. The core reporting-token
filter now includes `event_message` content so event statuses with message
secrets can produce deterministic `reporting/reporting_token` sidecars. New
client regressions send secret event statuses to a remote primary, remote
companion, and local linked device, prove the original broadcast carries
`reporting`, then execute bare-requester retry receipts for the same
`status@broadcast` chat. The mock-encryptor regression verifies cached event
payload replay, linked-own-device wrapper preservation, and `reporting`/
`tctoken` suppression across a fresh all-device lookup. The provider-backed
regression decrypts the initial `pkmsg` fanout for all three recipients,
verifies established-session `msg` replays at counter 2 for every recipient,
preserves the linked-own-device `device_sent_message` destination as
`status@broadcast`, suppresses `reporting` and `tctoken`, marks retries
successful, and removes the cached status events after replay. It was verified
in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core reporting_token_includes_event_message_content --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_status_event --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_status_event --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`
- `test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The status event facade and device-scoped retry sidecar-suppression slice added
after the all-device event retry work exposes typed
`send_status_event`/`send_status_event_with_signal_provider` helpers and closes
the participant-requester `status@broadcast` event replay path for both mock
and store-backed Signal sends. New client regressions send secret event
statuses through the typed facades to a device-specific remote audience
recipient, prove the original broadcasts carry deterministic `reporting`
sidecars, then execute device-scoped retry receipts for the same
`status@broadcast` chat. The mock-encryptor regression verifies cached event
payload replay, message-secret preservation, retry accounting, cache removal,
and `reporting`/`tctoken` suppression on replay. The provider-backed
regression decrypts the initial event `pkmsg`, verifies the established-session
`msg` replay at counter 2, preserves the event payload and message secret,
suppresses retry sidecars, marks the retry successful, and removes the cached
status event after replay. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_status_event --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_status_event --quiet`

- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`
- `test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The status poll facade slice added after the typed status event work exposes
`send_status_poll`/`send_status_poll_with_signal_provider` helpers so callers
no longer need to route poll statuses through the generic status-message
facade. The existing status poll reporting-token, participant retry, and
all-device retry regressions now send through the typed facades directly while
retaining coverage for deterministic `reporting` attachment on original poll
statuses, `reporting`/`tctoken` suppression on retry replays, linked-own-device
wrapper preservation, store-backed Signal `pkmsg` fanout, established-session
`msg` replays at counter 2, retry accounting, and cache removal. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_status_poll --quiet`

The status image facade slice added after the typed status poll work exposes
`send_status_image`/`send_status_image_with_signal_provider` helpers for the
image status path used by live media examples and the status media retry
coverage. Existing mock and store-backed image status regressions now send
through the typed facades directly while retaining coverage for
`status@broadcast` participant fanout, media stanza shape, participant-hash
attachment, linked-own-device `device_sent_message` wrapping, store-backed
Signal `pkmsg` fanout, established-session `msg` reuse, participant and
all-device retry replay, retry accounting, and cache removal. Historical ledger
command references for the renamed image-status regressions were updated to the

The status video/document facade slice added after the typed status image work
exposes `send_status_video`/`send_status_video_with_signal_provider` and
`send_status_document`/`send_status_document_with_signal_provider` helpers so
callers can send uploaded video and document statuses without manually wrapping
`MessageContent`. Focused mock-encryptor regressions cover `status@broadcast`

The ignored live status image-media smoke slice added after the status media
facade work extends the `live_e2e` harness with
`live_status_image_media_send_smoke`. The ignored test uploads
`WA_STATUS_MEDIA_PATH` through `http-media`, sends it to the comma-separated
`WA_STATUS_JIDS` audience via `send_status_image_with_signal_provider`, accepts
optional `WA_STATUS_MEDIA_MIMETYPE`/`WA_STATUS_MEDIA_CAPTION`, and asserts a
non-empty message id plus recipient fanout. This gives live operators a direct
manual validation path for the uploaded media `status@broadcast` relay rather
than only normal image sends or text statuses. It was verified in the current VM
without live credentials with:

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media live_status_image_media_send_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media live_status_image_media_send_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`
- `test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

after the live status image-media smoke extends `live_e2e` with
`live_status_video_remote_thumbnail_send_smoke` and
`live_status_document_remote_thumbnail_send_smoke`. The tests upload
`WA_STATUS_VIDEO_PATH`/`WA_STATUS_DOCUMENT_PATH`, generate and upload remote
thumbnails through the existing ffmpeg/pdftoppm-backed helpers, then send the
resulting `VideoContent`/`DocumentContent` through the typed
`send_status_video_with_signal_provider` and
`send_status_document_with_signal_provider` facades to the comma-separated
`WA_STATUS_JIDS` audience. They assert thumbnail upload material plus non-empty
status relay ids and recipient fanout, giving live operators a direct manual
validation path for generated remote thumbnails on `status@broadcast`. It was
verified in the current VM without live credentials with:

The public live status image-media example slice added after the live status
smoke documentation work extends the existing `live_status` example with an
optional `WA_STATUS_MEDIA_PATH` mode behind the `http-media` feature. The
example still sends text statuses by default, but when `WA_STATUS_MEDIA_PATH` is
set it uploads the image bytes, builds `ImageContent`, applies optional
`WA_STATUS_MEDIA_MIMETYPE`/`WA_STATUS_MEDIA_CAPTION`, and relays the media
status through `send_status_image_with_signal_provider` to `WA_STATUS_JIDS`.
The README command list, feature notes, and feature support matrix now document
the new example mode. It was verified in the current VM without live
credentials with:

- `cargo check -p wa-client --example live_status --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features http-media --example live_status --quiet`

- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --example live_status --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`
- `test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The public live status poll/event example slice added after the status
image-media example work extends `live_status` with `WA_STATUS_KIND`
dispatch for `text`, `image`, `poll`, and `event`. Text remains the default,
`WA_STATUS_MEDIA_PATH` still implies image mode, and poll/event modes construct
`PollContent`/`EventContent` from the same names, options, timestamps, join
link, and message-secret environment variables used by the ignored live smokes
before relaying through the typed Signal-provider status facades. The README
now documents `WA_STATUS_KIND`, and the feature support matrix has an explicit
poll/event send row. It was verified in the current VM without live credentials
with:

The public live status audio/sticker example slice added after the status

The public live status video/document thumbnail example slice added after the

`WA_STATUS_VIDEO_PATH` and `WA_STATUS_DOCUMENT_PATH`. When built with

generates and uploads remote thumbnails with the existing ffmpeg/pdftoppm-backed

The direct contact/location live-send slice added after the live status
video/document example modes extends `live_text` with `WA_MESSAGE_KIND`
dispatch for `text`, `contact`, and `location`. The example still sends text by
default, can build contact messages from `WA_CONTACT_DISPLAY_NAME` plus optional
`WA_CONTACT_VCARD`, and can send location messages from latitude/longitude plus
optional name/address/URL metadata through the generic Signal-provider message
facade. The ignored live e2e harness now includes `live_contact_send_smoke` and
`live_location_send_smoke`, gated by explicit contact/location environment
variables so bulk ignored-test runs do not accidentally send extra payloads. The
README and feature support matrix document the new direct contact/location live
surface. It was verified in the current VM without live credentials with:

- `cargo check -p wa-client --example live_text --quiet`

The direct poll/event live-send slice added after the direct contact/location
live-send work extends `live_text` with `WA_MESSAGE_KIND=poll` and
`WA_MESSAGE_KIND=event`. The example builds `PollContent`/`EventContent` from
optional direct poll/event names, options, timestamps, join-link, and 32-byte
message-secret environment variables before relaying through the typed
Signal-provider direct-send facades. The ignored live e2e harness now includes
`live_poll_send_smoke` and `live_event_send_smoke`, giving operators a manual
validation path for direct poll/event sends alongside the existing status
poll/event smokes. The README and feature support matrix document the expanded
direct poll/event live surface. It was verified in the current VM without live

The direct reaction live-send slice added after the direct poll/event live-send

from-me, participant, and reaction-text environment variables before sending
through `send_reaction_with_signal_provider`. The ignored live e2e harness now

The direct edit/delete/pin protocol live-send slice added after direct reaction
live-send work extends `live_text` with `WA_MESSAGE_KIND=edit`,
`WA_MESSAGE_KIND=delete`, and `WA_MESSAGE_KIND=pin`. The example builds
validated target `MessageKey` values from per-mode message-id, remote-JID,
from-me, and participant environment variables, sends edited text through
`send_edit_with_signal_provider`, sends revokes through
`send_delete_with_signal_provider`, and sends pin/unpin protocol messages
through `send_pin_with_signal_provider` with optional timestamp/action
configuration. The ignored live e2e harness now includes
`live_edit_send_smoke`, `live_delete_send_smoke`, and
`live_pin_message_send_smoke`, giving operators manual validation paths for the
remaining direct protocol send facades. The README and feature support matrix
document the protocol live-send surface. It was verified in the current VM
without live credentials with:
