The trusted-contact-token parser fuzz slice added after the direct
edit/delete/pin live-send work adds a `tc_token_parse` fuzz target. The target
drives raw binary-node decoded inputs, structured privacy-token issue results,
structured privacy-token notifications, generated tctoken issue queries, raw
stored-record bytes, and synthesized stored token/marker records through the
public tctoken parser and encoder surfaces. Successful parsed records are
re-encoded and decoded again, and send-node generation is exercised with a
bounded timestamp so malformed privacy-token and stored-token inputs join the
network parser fuzz surface. The fuzz README and manifest document the new
target. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise tctoken --quiet`
- `cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bins`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target cargo clippy --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bins -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`
- `test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The live media-retry response smoke slice added after the
trusted-contact-token parser fuzz work extends the ignored live e2e harness with
`live_media_retry_response_roundtrip_smoke`. The new smoke reconstructs the
pending uploaded-media descriptor from explicit SHA-256/media-key/length
environment variables, persists it through the media retry coordinator, spawns
the Signal-provider incoming processor with HTTP media retry handling, sends the
encrypted retry request, and waits for a non-empty `MediaRetryProcessed` outcome
with downloaded refreshed media and no processing errors. The `live_media_retry`
example keeps the request-only default and adds `WA_MEDIA_RETRY_WAIT_RESPONSE=1`
for the same response-processing path when compiled with `http-media`. README
and the feature support matrix document the new live response knobs. It was
verified in the current VM without live credentials with:

- `cargo check -p wa-client --example live_media_retry --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --example live_media_retry --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media live_media_retry_response_roundtrip_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media live_media_retry_response_roundtrip_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --example live_media_retry --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --test live_e2e --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media -- -D warnings`

- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target`
- `test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The media-retry parser/application fuzz slice added after the live media-retry

payloads, valid encrypted media-retry notifications, inbound receipt and

pending-media retry records through the public parser/encoder surfaces. The

The non-blocking history-sync mapping slice added after the chat/account parser
fuzz work broadens receive/history processing for `NON_BLOCKING_DATA` payloads.
`process_history_sync` now maps non-blocking pushnames into contact history
events, non-blocking status messages into message history events, history
accounts into LID contact events with username/country/deletion metadata,
recent stickers into persisted sticker events with hash/media-key bytes and
scalar metadata, history call-log records into non-live `history_log` call
events, and global-settings default disappearing mode into the existing
account-state event path. The follow-up past participants extension maps history
`past_participants` records into group-update events with leave/remove
participant lists, timestamps, and reason maps, while preserving the direct
LID/PN mapping extraction that already runs for all history sync types. Account
LIDs now accept full LID JIDs or bare LID users and reject malformed or non-LID
domains. A client integration regression now runs the mapped result through
`process_history_sync_result`, verifies emitted LID mapping, batch, and default
disappearing-mode events, and confirms the native store persists the contact,
status message, account-contact, recent-sticker, call-log, past-participant
group, default disappearing-mode, and LID mapping side effects. It was verified
in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core stored_recent_sticker_event_round_trips --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core processes_non_blocking_history_pushnames_statuses_and_lid_mappings --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core rejects_invalid_history_global_settings_disappearing_mode --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core rejects_invalid_history_account_lids --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features stored_recent_sticker_event_round_trips --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features processes_non_blocking_history_pushnames_statuses_and_lid_mappings --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features rejects_invalid_history_global_settings_disappearing_mode --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features rejects_invalid_history_account_lids --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise history_sync_non_blocking_data_persists_and_emits_contact_status_and_mapping --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise history_sync_non_blocking_data_persists_and_emits_contact_status_and_mapping --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`

The history sync fuzz harness follow-up now supplements raw/compressed arbitrary
history bytes with a structured encoded `NON_BLOCKING_DATA` payload that drives

The history sync account-settings follow-up added after the recent-sticker and
default-disappearing-mode work introduces a durable `AccountSettingsEvent` for
stable global-settings fields and scalar history metadata. `process_history_sync`
now emits an account-settings batch entry for media visibility, wallpaper,
auto-download, notification-preview, notification, avatar FBID/password-presence
marker, quality, font, auto-unarchive, chat DB LID migration, AI waitlist,
thread offset, companion nonce, and secret-byte presence/length metadata while
avoiding persistence of the actual secret bytes or avatar password. The client
persists those entries in the native store under `AccountSettingsEvent`, merges
subsequent updates field-wise, and the structured history fuzz payload now
exercises the same fields. It was verified in the current VM with:

The store-backed sender-key distribution retry slice added after the history
sync account-settings work tightens group Signal-provider recovery. When a
stored sender-key distribution is used to recover from a stale decodable
sender-key record during group payload decrypt, the provider now applies the
distribution candidate and decrypts the ciphertext inside one sender-key
transaction, committing the recovered record only after the retry decrypt
succeeds. Tampered or otherwise invalid group ciphertext can no longer roll the
stored sender-key record forward merely because a newer distribution is
available, while valid fresh group ciphertext still repairs stale records. It
was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core store_sender_key_provider_preserves_stale_record_after_failed_distribution_retry --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core store_sender_key_provider_recovers_stale_record_from_distribution_on_decrypt_failure --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_sender_key_provider_preserves_stale_record_after_failed_distribution_retry --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_sender_key_provider_recovers_stale_record_from_distribution_on_decrypt_failure --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core store_sender_key_provider --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_sender_key_provider --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The attached sender-key distribution atomicity follow-up added after the
store-backed sender-key distribution retry slice extends the same commit-after-
decrypt rule to group payloads that arrive while the local sender-key record is
missing or undecodable. Attached/stored distributions are now applied as
transaction-local candidates first, and the provider writes the recovered
sender-key record only after the candidate decrypts the group ciphertext. New
coverage proves tampered group ciphertext leaves a missing record missing and a
corrupt record unchanged, while a subsequent valid group payload still repairs
the record from the distribution. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core store_sender_key_provider_preserves_missing_or_corrupt_record_after_failed_attached_distribution --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core store_sender_key_provider_recovers_corrupt_record_from_distribution --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core store_sender_key_provider_processes_distribution_and_decrypts_group_payload --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core store_sender_key_provider --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_sender_key_provider --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The sender-key distribution memory validation slice hardens the store-backed
repository boundary used by sender-key distribution retry memory. Direct
`StoreSignalRepository::store_sender_key_distribution` callers now must provide
decodable Signal sender-key distribution bytes before the durable
`KeyNamespace::SenderKey` cache is updated, preserving the codec path's existing
provider-side rejection behavior and preventing malformed retry memory from
being persisted. New coverage proves malformed direct repository writes leave no
sender-key memory behind while valid encoded distributions still round-trip. It
was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core store_signal_repository_rejects_malformed_sender_key_distribution_memory --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core store_sender_key_provider_does_not_persist_rejected_distribution --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core store_sender_key_provider --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_repository_rejects_malformed_sender_key_distribution_memory --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_sender_key_provider_does_not_persist_rejected_distribution --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_sender_key_provider --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The typed direct contact/location facade slice added after the sender-key
distribution memory validation work broadens the public direct-send API surface
without requiring callers to manually construct `MessageContent` for common
non-text messages. `wa-client` now exposes direct `send_contact`,
`send_contacts`, `send_location`, and `send_live_location` helpers, plus
store-backed Signal-provider variants for each helper. The existing generic
direct content regression now exercises the typed contact, contact-array,
location, and live-location facades, while new Signal-provider coverage sends a
contact as an initial `pkmsg`, sends a location over the established `msg`
session, and decrypts both on the simulated remote side. The feature support
matrix documents the typed facade surface. It was verified in the current VM
with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_direct_content_facades_relay_contact_location_and_media_content --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_direct_content_facades_with_signal_provider_encrypt_contact_and_location --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_direct_content_facades_relay_contact_location_and_media_content --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_direct_content_facades_with_signal_provider_encrypt_contact_and_location --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_text_does_not_schedule_tc_token_when --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_text_does_not_schedule_tc_token_when --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The typed direct media facade slice added after the direct contact/location
facades extends the same public API shape to media sends. `wa-client` now
exposes direct `send_image`, `send_video`, `send_audio`, `send_document`, and
`send_sticker` helpers, plus store-backed Signal-provider variants for each
helper, so callers no longer need to wrap common direct media payloads in
`MessageContent` by hand. The direct content regression now uses the typed image
facade, two smaller media regressions cover video/document and audio/sticker
relay shaping without recreating the earlier oversized async test future, and
the store-backed Signal-provider media regression now drives the typed image
facade through remote-side decrypt/decode. The feature support matrix documents
the typed media facade surface. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_direct_content_facades_relay_contact_location_and_media_content --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_direct_media_facades_relay_video_and_document_content --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_direct_media_facades_relay_audio_and_sticker_content --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_image_with_signal_provider_encrypts_media_payload --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_direct_content_facades_relay_contact_location_and_media_content --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_direct_media_facades_relay_video_and_document_content --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_direct_media_facades_relay_audio_and_sticker_content --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_image_with_signal_provider_encrypts_media_payload --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The live direct media facade slice added after the typed direct media facade
work moves the public live media workflows onto the same typed API surface and
broadens opt-in e2e validation. The `live_media` example now accepts
`WA_MEDIA_KIND=image|video|gif|ptv|audio|ptt|document|sticker`, uploads with the
matching media kind, builds typed media payload metadata from environment
variables, and sends through the typed store-backed Signal-provider media
facades. The ignored live e2e harness now uses typed image/video/document
facades for the existing direct image and generated video/document
remote-thumbnail smokes, and adds direct GIF, PTV, audio, and sticker media
smokes. README notes and the feature support matrix document the expanded live
media modes. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --example live_media --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --example live_media --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media live_image_media_send_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media live_gif_media_send_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media live_ptv_media_send_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media live_audio_media_send_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media live_sticker_media_send_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media,image live_video_remote_thumbnail_send_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media,image live_document_remote_thumbnail_send_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media live_image_media_send_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media live_gif_media_send_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media live_ptv_media_send_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media live_audio_media_send_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media live_sticker_media_send_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media,image live_video_remote_thumbnail_send_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media,image live_document_remote_thumbnail_send_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --example live_media -- -D warnings`

The typed direct PTV facade slice added after the live direct media work closes
the remaining uploaded-media direct facade named in the media foundation
checklist. `wa-client` now exposes direct `send_ptv` and
`send_ptv_with_signal_provider` helpers that use `VideoContent` but relay the
payload as a `ptv_message` rather than a normal `video_message`. Focused
coverage verifies mock relay stanza classification, proto shape, and
store-backed Signal-provider pre-key encryption/decrypt/decode for the video
note payload. The live media example and ignored e2e harness expose
PTV/video-note mode through `WA_MEDIA_KIND=ptv` and `WA_PTV_PATH`. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_direct_ptv_facade_relay_video_note_content --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_ptv_with_signal_provider_encrypts_video_note_payload --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_direct_ptv_facade_relay_video_note_content --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_ptv_with_signal_provider_encrypts_video_note_payload --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --example live_media --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --example live_media --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media live_ptv_media_send_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media live_ptv_media_send_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --example live_media -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --example live_media -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --test live_e2e -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --test live_e2e -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The typed direct GIF facade slice added after the direct PTV work closes the
remaining GIF media send facade named in the media checklist. `wa-client` now
exposes direct `send_gif` and `send_gif_with_signal_provider` helpers that take
`VideoContent`, force `gif_playback`, and relay the payload as a normal
`video_message`, matching the WhatsApp/Baileys GIF transport shape. Focused
coverage verifies mock relay stanza classification, proto shape, and
store-backed Signal-provider pre-key encryption/decrypt/decode for the GIF
playback flag. The live media example and ignored e2e harness expose GIF mode
through `WA_MEDIA_KIND=gif` and `WA_GIF_PATH`. It was verified in the current
VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`

- `cargo check -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --example live_media --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --example live_media --quiet`

- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media live_gif_media_send_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --example live_media -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --example live_media -- -D warnings`

The typed direct PTT facade slice added after the direct GIF work closes the
remaining push-to-talk media send facade named in the media checklist.
`wa-client` now exposes direct `send_ptt` and
`send_ptt_with_signal_provider` helpers that take `AudioContent`, force the
`ptt` flag, and relay the payload as an `audio_message`. Focused coverage
verifies mock relay stanza classification, proto shape, and store-backed
Signal-provider pre-key encryption/decrypt/decode for the PTT flag and waveform
metadata. The live media example and ignored e2e harness expose PTT mode through
`WA_MEDIA_KIND=ptt` and `WA_PTT_PATH`. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo check -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --example live_media --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --example live_media --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media live_ptt_media_send_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media live_ptt_media_send_smoke --no-run --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --example live_media -- -D warnings`

The typed direct view-once media facade slice added after the direct PTT work
promotes the already-landed core `FutureProofMessage` view-once wrapper into
the high-level one-to-one send API without adding a full combinatorial wrapper
surface. `wa-client` now exposes direct `send_view_once_image`,
`send_view_once_image_with_signal_provider`, `send_view_once_video`, and
`send_view_once_video_with_signal_provider` helpers. The helpers set the inner
media `view_once` flag and relay the payload inside `view_once_message`, while
the existing generic `send_message` path remains available for rarer wrapped
content. Focused coverage verifies mock relay stanza classification, wrapper
proto shape for image and video, and store-backed Signal-provider
pre-key encryption/decrypt/decode for wrapped image media. It was verified in
the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_direct_view_once_media_facades_relay_image_and_video_content --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_view_once_image_with_signal_provider_encrypts_wrapped_media_payload --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_direct_view_once_media_facades_relay_image_and_video_content --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_view_once_image_with_signal_provider_encrypts_wrapped_media_payload --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The live view-once media workflow slice extends that facade work into the
operator-facing live surfaces. The `live_media` example now accepts
`WA_MEDIA_VIEW_ONCE=1` for `WA_MEDIA_KIND=image` or `video`, dispatches through
the Signal-provider view-once image/video helpers, and rejects the switch for
media kinds where the current API has no view-once facade. The ignored live e2e
harness now has dedicated `live_view_once_image_media_send_smoke` and
`live_view_once_video_media_send_smoke` tests driven by
`WA_VIEW_ONCE_IMAGE_PATH` and `WA_VIEW_ONCE_VIDEO_PATH`, with optional MIME,
caption, and video dimension metadata. README and the feature-support matrix
now document the live view-once image/video flow. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --example live_media --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --example live_media --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --test live_e2e live_view_once --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --test live_e2e live_view_once --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --example live_media -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --example live_media -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --test live_e2e -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --test live_e2e -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The direct video/document live media smoke slice added after the live view-once
media workflow broadens ignored live validation for direct media sends that
already existed in the `live_media` example and typed Signal-provider API.
`live_e2e` now has `live_video_media_send_smoke` and
`live_document_media_send_smoke`, using `WA_VIDEO_MEDIA_PATH` and
`WA_DOCUMENT_MEDIA_PATH` so direct media validation does not collide with the
existing generated-thumbnail smokes that use `WA_VIDEO_PATH`/`WA_DOCUMENT_PATH`
and external thumbnail tools. Video smokes support optional MIME, caption,
duration, and dimensions; document smokes support optional MIME, caption, title,
file name, and page count. README examples/notes and the feature-support matrix
now document the direct video/document live e2e coverage. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --test live_e2e live_video_media_send_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --test live_e2e live_document_media_send_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --test live_e2e live_video_media_send_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --test live_e2e live_document_media_send_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --test live_e2e -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --test live_e2e -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The live business-profile read workflow slice added after the direct
video/document live media smokes broadens the business-helper foundations into
an operator-facing, ignored-by-default live validation path. `wa-client` now has
a `live_business_profile` example gated by `WA_BUSINESS_JID`, and `live_e2e`
has `live_business_profile_smoke` using the same environment variable. The
workflow fetches the business profile through the existing
`fetch_business_profile` facade and treats a missing profile as a valid server
response while still validating any returned JID is nonempty. README and the
feature-support matrix now document the example and live smoke. It was verified
in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket --example live_business_profile --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket --example live_business_profile --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket --test live_e2e live_business_profile_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket --test live_e2e live_business_profile_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket --example live_business_profile -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket --example live_business_profile -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --test live_e2e -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --test live_e2e -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The no-`http-media` live-e2e no-run builds emitted the pre-existing dead-code
warnings for helper functions only used by `http-media` media-retry tests; the
commands exited successfully, and the warning-as-error live-e2e clippy gates
were run with `http-media` enabled.

The live business-catalog read workflow slice added after the business-profile
live validation broadens the business-helper live surface from profile metadata
to catalog pagination. `wa-client` now has a `live_business_catalog` example
gated by `WA_BUSINESS_CATALOG_JID`, with optional `WA_BUSINESS_CATALOG_LIMIT`
and `WA_BUSINESS_CATALOG_CURSOR`, and `live_e2e` has a matching
`live_business_catalog_smoke`. The smoke validates successful catalog query
parsing and nonempty returned pagination cursors when present without mutating
the target business account. README and the feature-support matrix now document
the catalog example and ignored smoke. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket --example live_business_catalog --quiet`

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket --test live_e2e live_business_catalog_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket --test live_e2e live_business_catalog_smoke --no-run --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket --example live_business_catalog -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --test live_e2e -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --test live_e2e -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The no-`http-media` live-e2e no-run builds still emitted pre-existing dead-code
warnings for media-retry helpers only compiled when `http-media` is disabled;
the commands exited successfully, and the warning-as-error live-e2e clippy gates
were run with `http-media` enabled.

The live business-media upload workflow slice added after order-details live
validation extends business helper live coverage from read-only IQs to the
upload-only media helpers used by product and cover-photo mutations. `wa-client`
now has a `live_business_media` example gated by `WA_BUSINESS_MEDIA_PATH` and
`WA_BUSINESS_MEDIA_KIND=product_image|cover_photo`, with optional
`WA_BUSINESS_MEDIA_FALLBACK_HOST` for product-image URL construction. The
ignored live e2e harness now has `live_business_product_image_upload_smoke` and
`live_business_cover_photo_upload_smoke`, driven by
`WA_BUSINESS_PRODUCT_IMAGE_PATH` and `WA_BUSINESS_COVER_PHOTO_PATH`. These
smokes validate upload result shape without mutating any business profile or
catalog state. README and the feature-support matrix now document the
business-media example and ignored smokes. It was verified in the current VM
with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --example live_business_media --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --example live_business_media --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --test live_e2e live_business_product_image_upload_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --test live_e2e live_business_cover_photo_upload_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --test live_e2e live_business_product_image_upload_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --test live_e2e live_business_cover_photo_upload_smoke --no-run --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features sqlite-store,noise,websocket,http-media --example live_business_media -- -D warnings`

The live community read workflow slice added after newsletter direct-IQ
validation broadens ignored live coverage into the community facade without

queries. README and the feature-support matrix now document community read live
coverage. It was verified in the current VM with:

The no-`http-media` live-e2e no-run builds still emitted pre-existing dead-code

The native Signal session validation/lookup hardening slice added after the
community read live workflow tightens the store-backed Signal boundary called
out in the recommended implementation order.
`StoreSignalRepository::validate_session` now validates the native session and
identity key as an atomic pair, returning invalid results for missing,
malformed, or mismatched identity records instead of accepting any decodable
native session. The adjacent `get_session_info` path now applies the same
atomic pairing check before
returning a session for direct encryption, mirroring provider-state session
lookup behavior. This prevents stale/corrupt identity records from producing
false-positive direct-send session assertions or reusable encryption session
info. A focused core regression covers missing identity, identity mismatch, and
repaired identity for both validation and lookup, and representative
`wa-client` Signal session assertion/direct-send tests still pass. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise repository_validate_session_requires_matching_identity_record --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise repository_validate_session_requires_matching_identity_record --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise repository_ --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise repository_ --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise assert_sessions_fetches_missing_sessions_and_persists_them --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_text_with_signal_provider_fetches_session_and_writes_pre_key_ciphertext --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_text_with_signal_provider_mirrors_mapped_lid_session_to_pn_recipient --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise assert_sessions_fetches_missing_sessions_and_persists_them --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_text_with_signal_provider_fetches_session_and_writes_pre_key_ciphertext --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The focused `wa-client` default-feature tests still emitted the pre-existing
no-`http-media` live-e2e dead-code warnings for media-retry helpers because
Cargo builds the ignored live e2e target as part of the package test selection;
the commands exited successfully. The no-default `wa-client` runs did not emit
those warnings.

The native Signal PN/LID migration hardening slice added after session
validation/lookup hardening closes the adjacent alias-maintenance gap. Native
`StoreSignalRepository::migrate_session` now migrates project-owned session and
identity records as a valid atomic pair, skips orphaned or mismatched native
sessions instead of moving unusable state, and avoids mixing a source pair with
any destination session or identity conflict. Identity-only native records can
still migrate when no session participates. Focused core regressions cover
missing source identity, mismatched source identity, repaired identity migration,
and destination identity conflicts. A focused `wa-client` assertion regression
now proves a PN recipient mapped to a LID with a stale descriptor identity
performs a fresh encrypt key query instead of reusing the unusable mapped
session. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise native --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise native --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise assert_sessions_fetches_when_mapped_lid_descriptor_identity_mismatches --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise assert_sessions_fetches_when_mapped_lid_descriptor_identity_mismatches --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise repository_ --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise repository_ --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise assert_sessions_ --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise assert_sessions_ --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The retry Signal session snapshot stale-alias slice added after native PN/LID
migration hardening extends the same atomic identity-pair invariant into retry
planning evidence. Focused `wa-client` regressions now corrupt mapped LID
descriptor and provider identity records for a PN participant, verify
`retry_session_snapshot` treats both as missing instead of returning a stale
base key/registration snapshot, and pin the missing-session retry receipt
planner to schedule a refresh rather than a delete/collision action from
unusable state. The broader retry snapshot filter still covers valid descriptor
and provider mapped LID snapshots. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise retry_session_snapshot_ignores_mapped_lid_ --quiet`

- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise retry_session_snapshot_ --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise retry_session_snapshot_ --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The default-feature `wa-client` retry snapshot tests still emitted the
pre-existing no-`http-media` live-e2e dead-code warnings for media-retry helpers

selection; the commands exited successfully. The no-default `wa-client` runs
did not emit those warnings.

The retry inline key-bundle legacy-alias cleanup slice added after stale-alias
snapshot coverage normalizes retry participants through the Signal session JID
normalizer before computing PN/LID cleanup aliases. This prevents an inline
bundle injected for canonical `s.whatsapp.net` from treating the same
participant received as legacy `c.us` as a separate session to report/delete,
while still clearing mapped LID aliases and preserving the injected canonical
session. A focused `wa-client` regression covers a `123:1@c.us` retry
participant with an injected `123:1@s.whatsapp.net` bundle and stale mapped LID
provider state. The broader inline key-bundle and delete-and-refresh retry
cleanup filters still pass for PN, LID, and hosted aliases. It was verified in
the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise retry_inline_key_bundle_normalizes_legacy_c_us_alias_cleanup --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise retry_inline_key_bundle_normalizes_legacy_c_us_alias_cleanup --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise retry_inline_key_bundle_ --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise retry_inline_key_bundle_ --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise retry_delete_and_refresh_clears_ --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise retry_delete_and_refresh_clears_ --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The default-feature `wa-client` retry cleanup tests still emitted the
pre-existing no-`http-media` live-e2e dead-code warnings for media-retry helpers
because Cargo builds the ignored live e2e target as part of the package test
selection; the commands exited successfully. The no-default `wa-client` runs
did not emit those warnings.

The retry resend planning legacy-requester normalization slice added after
inline key-bundle cleanup moves the same canonical Signal JID handling into the
`Client::plan_retry_resend` facade. The client now normalizes retry receipt
`from` and `participant` requester JIDs through the Signal session normalizer
before delegating to the generic retry manager, leaving `wa-core` protocol
planning generic while ensuring client retry plans, resend targets, prepared
jobs, session assertions, and encryption calls use canonical
`s.whatsapp.net` device JIDs instead of legacy `c.us` aliases. A focused
`wa-client` regression covers a cached direct-message retry whose requester is
`123:1@c.us` and proves the plan, prepared job, and replay encryption target all
use `123:1@s.whatsapp.net`. The broader retry resend execution filter still
passes. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`

The parsed retry inline key-bundle canonicalization slice added after retry
resend planning normalization closes the adjacent incoming-node path. Parsed
retry receipts now normalize the requester JID before constructing the inline
`RetryReceiptSessionBundle`, so an incoming key-bundle retry from legacy
`c.us` injects and replays through the canonical Signal session JID while the
raw receipt metadata is preserved for callers. The existing inline key-bundle
incoming retry regression now uses `123:1@c.us` as the receipt requester and
asserts the plan participant, resend target, injected bundle session, stored
session, and replay all use `123:1@s.whatsapp.net`. The broader incoming retry
resend and retry execution filters still pass. It was verified in the current
VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_retry_resend_injects_inline_key_bundle --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_retry_resend_injects_inline_key_bundle --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_retry_resend_ --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_retry_resend_ --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise execute_retry_resends_ --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise execute_retry_resends_ --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`

The parsed retry participant-attribute key-bundle canonicalization slice extends
that incoming-node coverage to group retry receipts whose requester comes from a
legacy `participant` attribute rather than direct `from`. Parsed retry receipts
still preserve raw receipt metadata for callers, but inline key-bundle session
construction now receives the normalized requester JID, so a group retry with
`participant="123:1@c.us"` constructs any injected bundle for
`123:1@s.whatsapp.net`. A parser-level regression pins raw receipt preservation
alongside canonical bundle session construction, and the Signal-provider group
retry replay regression now covers the same legacy participant alias through
ACK, session query, sender-key redistribution, replay encryption, and stored
session validation. The broader incoming retry resend and retry execution
filters still pass. It was verified in the current VM with:

The default-feature `wa-client` incoming retry/key-bundle tests still emitted
the pre-existing no-`http-media` live-e2e dead-code warnings for media-retry

The Signal codec legacy-PN canonicalization slice added after retry
participant-attribute key-bundle canonicalization moves the same `c.us` to
`s.whatsapp.net` normalization into the generic core Signal codec/provider
boundary. `SignalMessageCodec` now canonicalizes one-to-one encrypt recipients,
inbound decrypt senders, and sender-key distribution authors before repository
lookups and provider calls, and the store-backed provider plus sender-key store
key builder apply the same normalization when used directly. This prevents
ordinary incoming encrypted messages or group sender-key distributions received
with legacy PN aliases from creating provider-facing request IDs or
sender-key records separate from the canonical Signal session JID. A focused
core regression proves codec encrypt/decrypt provider requests receive
`123:7@s.whatsapp.net` when callers provide `123:7@c.us`, while raw direct chat
metadata remains preserved, and that sender-key distributions are stored only
under the canonical author key. The provider-state sender-key distribution test
now also proves a local `999:7@c.us` lookup reuses the existing canonical
`999:7@s.whatsapp.net` sender-key record instead of creating a parallel state.
It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core signal_message_codec_normalizes_legacy_c_us_signal_jids --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core signal_message_codec_ --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise signal_message_codec_ --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core provider_state_store_loads_or_creates_local_sender_key_distribution_record --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise provider_state_store_loads_or_creates_local_sender_key_distribution_record --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core store_sender_key_provider_ --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_sender_key_provider_ --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core provider_state_store_ --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise provider_state_store_ --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core signal_provider_ --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise signal_provider_ --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The client inbound sender-key distribution legacy-PN canonicalization slice
added after core Signal codec canonicalization carries the same `c.us` to
`s.whatsapp.net` behavior through high-level receive surfaces. The direct
`process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider` regression now feeds a group
message with `participant="127:7@c.us"` and proves the ACK participant is
canonical, the in-memory event key preserves the raw participant, distribution
repository lookup succeeds through both legacy and canonical aliases, provider
sender-key state is stored only under
`559@g.us|127:7@s.whatsapp.net`, and no legacy provider record is created. The
spawned incoming-processor and offline-frame regressions feed the same legacy
participant through encoded frames and prove decoded-frame ACK/event metadata is
canonical while retaining the same dual repository lookup and no-legacy-state
invariant. It was verified in the current VM with:

The client direct Signal receive legacy-PN canonicalization slice added after
inbound sender-key distribution canonicalization extends the same alias handling
to one-to-one encrypted messages. A focused direct-node regression now receives a
pre-key message and follow-up session message from `123:7@c.us`, proves the
store-backed Signal provider creates/reuses the canonical `123:7@s.whatsapp.net`
session and identity, advances the receiving chain, emits ACKs to the canonical
sender after binary round-trip, preserves the raw direct in-memory event
`remote_jid`, and can encrypt a canonical reply that the sender-side session
decrypts. A matching spawned incoming-processor regression feeds a legacy
direct pre-key message through encoded frames and proves decoded-frame ACK/event
metadata is canonical while the same canonical session/identity state is stored.

The client placeholder-resend legacy-PN metadata slice added after offline
direct receive canonicalization hardens the Signal-provider placeholder
request path without changing raw message metadata. New direct and spawned
incoming-processor regressions feed unavailable placeholder messages from
`123@c.us`, verify the Signal-provider resend request is encrypted to the
client's own devices, decrypt the peer-data operation request, and prove the
embedded missing `MessageKey`, placeholder event key, ACK target, resend tracker
entry, and stored event metadata all preserve the raw legacy remote JID. This
pins the intended distinction from Signal session storage canonicalization:
placeholder resend requests reference the original missing message key while
Signal encryption still targets canonical own-device sessions. It was verified
in the current VM with:

The direct Signal-provider media-retry legacy-PN metadata slice added after the
media-capable placeholder/retry pump coverage hardens the receive/media retry
boundary outside placeholder resend. A focused
`process_incoming_node_with_media_retry_with_signal_provider` regression
registers pending retried media under a legacy `123@c.us` remote and
`456@c.us` participant, receives a successful encrypted media retry
notification carrying the same `<rmr>` metadata, downloads/decrypts the retried
media, clears the pending coordinator entry, and proves the emitted
`MediaRetryEvent` preserves the raw legacy retry key while the successful event
is not left queued in the native media-retry store. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_media_retry_with_signal_provider_preserves_legacy_retry_key --quiet`

The retry-resend Signal-provider legacy-PN chat normalization slice added after
startup media-retry replay coverage closes another retry receive/replay alias
gap. `normalize_retry_receipt_signal_jids` now canonicalizes the retry planning
copy of `to`, `recipient`, and `chat_jid` legacy `c.us` values in addition to
the requester fields, so retry replay lookup matches the canonical direct-send
cache keys while the raw inbound receipt still flows through the receive
processor. The existing planner regression now feeds a legacy `123@c.us`
recipient/chat with a canonical cached message and proves replay planning,
preparation, encryption, and relay use `123@s.whatsapp.net` plus
`123:1@s.whatsapp.net`. A new spawned
`spawn_incoming_processor_with_retry_resend_with_signal_provider` regression
feeds a raw retry receipt from `123:1@c.us` to `123@c.us`, observes the
canonical ACK/session query/replay boundary, decrypt-validates the Signal
provider replay, confirms the canonical provider session exists, and proves the
cached retry is finalized. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_retry_resend_signal_provider_normalizes_legacy_retry_receipt --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_retry_resend_signal_provider_normalizes_legacy_retry_receipt --quiet`

- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise plan_retry_resend_normalizes_legacy_c_us_requester_and_chat_before_replay --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise retry_resend --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise retry_resend --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The direct/offline combined Signal-provider retry-receipt legacy-PN chat
normalization slice added after retry-only spawned coverage carries the same
alias fix through the media-capable combined receive facades. New focused
regressions feed retry receipts from `123:1@c.us` with legacy `123@c.us`
recipient/chat metadata into
`process_incoming_node_with_placeholder_retry_and_media_retry_with_signal_provider`
and
`process_offline_node_with_placeholder_retry_and_media_retry_with_signal_provider`.
Both tests cache the original send under canonical `123@s.whatsapp.net`, prove
the combined facade emits the canonical retry ACK/session query, decrypt-verify
the Signal-provider replay to `123:1@s.whatsapp.net`, assert the retry plan
uses canonical chat/participant JIDs, and confirm the retry cache is finalized
without triggering placeholder or media-retry side effects. It was verified in
the current VM with:
