The binary-node compatibility fixture AD-JID domain slice added after the
retry-receipt legacy-PN normalization coverage broadens the neutral checked-in
fixture set and the local reference-generator sample set with
`jid_device_domains`. The new sample pins device-scoped `@lid`, `@hosted`, and
`@hosted.lid` USync user entries in addition to the existing WhatsApp device
JID coverage. The wa-testkit fixture test now asserts all four device-domain
families so future reference-generated fixture churn cannot silently drop the
AD-JID domain bytes that Baileys-compatible binary-node codecs need to preserve.
It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test binary_node_fixtures --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-binary --quiet`
- `cargo check -p wa-testkit --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-testkit --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The app-state parser structured fuzz slice added after the binary-node AD-JID
fixture work broadens `fuzz/fuzz_targets/app_state_node_parse.rs` beyond raw
decoded binary-node frames. The target now synthesizes realistic dirty
notifications, valid sync query results with snapshot and patch bytes,
missing-version patch responses, malformed raw patch bytes, success IQs, attr
and child error IQs, and invalid non-IQ query responses from each fuzz input.
Those structured nodes drive `parse_dirty_notification_node`,
`parse_dirty_notification_nodes`, `parse_app_state_sync_response`, and both
`parse_app_state_query_result` variants through the branches needed for app-state
retry/resync parser hardening even when arbitrary bytes do not decode to a
protocol-shaped stanza. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin app_state_node_parse --quiet`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target cargo clippy --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin app_state_node_parse -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise app_state --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features app_state --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The Noise frame decode fuzz slice added after structured app-state parser
fuzzing closes the planned `noise_frame_decode` target gap. The new
`fuzz/fuzz_targets/noise_frame_decode.rs` target drives raw fragmented input
through `NoiseFrameCodec::push`, re-encodes successfully decoded frames, feeds
structured single and concatenated frames across fuzz-derived chunk boundaries,
exercises oversized length-prefix rejection, and runs deterministic
`NoiseTransport` encrypt/decrypt, tamper, and raw-decrypt paths. The fuzz
manifest and README now list `noise_frame_decode` alongside the other
network-facing parser targets. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin noise_frame_decode --quiet`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target cargo clippy --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin noise_frame_decode -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test -p wa-crypto noise --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise noise --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The app-state patch decode fuzz slice added after Noise frame fuzzing closes the
planned `app_state_patch_decode` target gap. The new
`fuzz/fuzz_targets/app_state_patch_decode.rs` target drives raw `SyncdPatch` and
`SyncdSnapshot` protobuf bytes through decode attempts and re-encode/decode
passes, then synthesizes encrypted app-state patch bundles and snapshots using
the public chat-mutation builders. The structured path decodes valid
multi-mutation patches into event batches, decodes valid snapshots into event
batches, and exercises patch-MAC, operation, previous-state, key-data,
snapshot-MAC, and snapshot-key mismatch rejection paths. The fuzz manifest and
README now list `app_state_patch_decode` with the other planned parser targets.
It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin app_state_patch_decode --quiet`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target cargo clippy --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin app_state_patch_decode -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise app_state --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features app_state --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The binary-node decompression fuzz slice added after app-state patch decode
fuzzing closes the planned `decompressing_if_required` target gap. The new
`fuzz/fuzz_targets/decompressing_if_required.rs` target feeds malformed
zlib-marked frames, compresses arbitrary decoded binary nodes back through the
codec, and compresses protocol-shaped IQ, message, retry receipt, and history
notification nodes before decoding them. This gives the private
`decompress_if_required` branch explicit fuzz pressure with both invalid zlib
payloads and valid compressed binary-node frames instead of relying on raw
`decode_binary_node` inputs to stumble into that path. The fuzz manifest and
README now list `decompressing_if_required` with the other planned parser
targets. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin decompressing_if_required --quiet`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target cargo clippy --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin decompressing_if_required -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test -p wa-binary --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The file-backed large-media profiling slice added after decompression fuzzing
tightens the e2e-sized streamed media fixture. The file-backed transport now
records the encrypted upload length handed to `upload_media_stream`, fills
regular-file chunks deterministically up to the configured fixture buffer, and
the e2e-sized test asserts that upload and download both account for the exact
encrypted file length, exact expected chunk counts, bounded max chunk sizes, and
no fallback to the in-memory upload transport. This narrows the remaining media
parity profiling gap by turning the existing large round-trip into a bounded
streaming regression check instead of only a functional ciphertext/plaintext
round-trip. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise media_transfer_streams_e2e_sized_file_without_in_memory_ciphertext_transport --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise media_transfer_streams --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise media_transfer_streams_e2e_sized_file_without_in_memory_ciphertext_transport --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The SQLite stored-media-retry retention slice added after large-media profiling
hardens replay behavior for transient refreshed-media download failures. A new
`wa-client` regression persists a pending media retry and a successful retry
response, replays it once without the refreshed media available, and verifies
that both the pending descriptor and retry-event row stay in SQLite while the
coordinator retains the pending entry. A second reopen then provides the
refreshed encrypted media, verifies the plaintext download, and proves both
SQLite rows and the coordinator entry are cleaned only after the successful
replay. The same pass also tightened an existing retry-resend test so its
mock-encryptor mutex guard is dropped before awaiting connection shutdown,
allowing no-default test clippy to cover this area. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise,sqlite-store sqlite_stored_media_retry_events_retain_success_response_after_download_failure --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise,sqlite-store stored_media_retry_events --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise,sqlite-store sqlite_stored_media_retry_events_retain_success_response_after_download_failure --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise,sqlite-store --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise,sqlite-store --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise plan_retry_resend_normalizes_legacy_c_us_requester_and_chat_before_replay --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The Signal replacement pre-key failure slice added after SQLite media-retry
retention hardens the store-backed one-to-one provider session boundary. A new
`wa-core` regression establishes an inbound one-time-pre-key session, captures
the persisted provider session and identity, feeds a tampered signed-pre-key
replacement message with the same remote identity but a new base ratchet key,
and verifies the decrypt failure leaves the existing session and identity
unchanged. The test then proves the old session still decrypts a normal message
before accepting the valid replacement pre-key message and confirming the stored
remote ratchet moves to the replacement base key. This narrows the remaining
Signal ratchet edge-case gap around failed replacement pre-key messages not
poisoning an existing session. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_preserves_existing_session_after_failed_replacement_pre_key --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_preserves_existing_session_after_failed_replacement_pre_key --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_preserves_session_after_failed --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_inbound_pre_key_without_one_time_pre_key --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The follow-up Signal one-time replacement pre-key failure slice broadens the
same store-backed provider boundary. A new `wa-core` regression establishes an
inbound one-time-pre-key session, keeps a second local one-time pre-key for a
replacement message from the same remote identity, tampers that replacement
message before authentication, and verifies the failed decrypt leaves the
existing provider session, identity, and replacement one-time pre-key unchanged.
It then proves the old session can still decrypt a normal message before
accepting the valid replacement message, consuming the second pre-key, and
moving the stored remote ratchet to the replacement base key. This further
narrows the remaining one-to-one ratchet edge-case gap around failed
replacement pre-key messages not poisoning existing store-backed state. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_preserves_state_after_failed_one_time_replacement --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_preserves_state_after_failed_one_time_replacement --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_preserves_existing_session_after_failed_replacement_pre_key --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The next Signal identity-change replacement slice adds the decodable
provider-session sibling to the earlier opaque-session guard. A new `wa-core`
regression establishes an inbound one-time-pre-key session, keeps a second
one-time pre-key for replacement, then submits a replacement pre-key message
for the same remote JID but signed by a different remote identity. The decrypt
is rejected as an identity change, while the established provider session,
stored identity, and replacement pre-key remain unchanged. The test then proves
the old session still decrypts and that a valid same-identity replacement can
consume the held pre-key and advance the stored remote ratchet. This narrows
the remaining Signal provider parity gap around one-to-one identity-change
handling during replacement pre-key sessions. It was verified in the current VM
with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_preserves_state_after_one_time_replacement_identity_change --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_preserves_state_after_one_time_replacement_identity_change --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_preserves_state_after_failed_one_time_replacement --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The signed-pre-key replacement identity-change slice completes the immediate
store-backed sibling of the one-time replacement guard. A new `wa-core`
regression establishes an inbound one-time-pre-key session, then submits a
fresh-base signed-pre-key-only replacement message for the same remote JID but
with a different remote identity. The decrypt is rejected before the established
provider session or identity is changed, the already-consumed one-time pre-key
stays consumed, and the old session still decrypts. The same test then accepts
a valid same-identity signed-pre-key-only replacement and confirms the stored
remote ratchet advances to the replacement base key while the identity remains
unchanged. This narrows the remaining one-to-one Signal provider parity gap
around replacement pre-key identity-change handling across both one-time and
signed-pre-key-only replacement paths. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_preserves_state_after_signed_replacement_identity_change --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_preserves_state_after_signed_replacement_identity_change --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_preserves_state_after_one_time_replacement_identity_change --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The missing-one-time replacement base-key guard tightens another store-backed
provider fallback edge. The existing fallback for a consumed one-time pre-key
now only treats a pre-key wrapper as an existing-session replay when the
wrapper base key matches the stored remote ratchet key; otherwise the path
returns to the normal missing-pre-key rejection instead of attempting to
decrypt and advance the existing provider session. A new `wa-core` regression
establishes a one-time-pre-key session, crafts a valid new-ratchet inner
message wrapped as a pre-key message with the already-consumed pre-key id and a
fresh base key, and verifies it is rejected as a missing local pre-key while the
stored session and identity remain unchanged. The test then proves the original
session still decrypts a normal message. This narrows the one-to-one Signal
provider parity gap around replay/replacement discrimination when local
one-time pre-key material has already been consumed. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_rejects_missing_one_time_replacement_with_new_base_key --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_rejects_missing_one_time_replacement_with_new_base_key --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_rejects_inbound_pre_key_when_one_time_pre_key_missing --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_preserves_state_after_failed_one_time_replacement --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The same-base missing-one-time fallback slice preserves the compatible side of
the tightened guard. A new `wa-core` regression establishes a one-time-pre-key
session, consumes that local pre-key, then wraps the sender's next
provider-session message in a pre-key container using the already-consumed
pre-key id and the current stored remote ratchet/base key. The provider accepts
that same-base wrapper through the existing-session fallback, advances the
stored receiving chain exactly once, keeps the local pre-key consumed, preserves
the stored identity, and rejects a replay of the same wrapped message without
mutating state. This narrows the one-to-one Signal provider parity gap around
consumed one-time pre-key replay handling by proving both sides of the
base-key discriminator. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_missing_one_time_existing_session_same_base --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_missing_one_time_existing_session_same_base --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_rejects_missing_one_time_replacement_with_new_base_key --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The receive-facade same-base missing-one-time fallback slice lifts the core
provider proof into the client inbound path. A new `wa-client` regression
processes an initial store-backed `pkmsg`, consumes the local one-time pre-key,
then processes the sender's next provider-session ciphertext wrapped in another
`pkmsg` that reuses the consumed pre-key id and the current stored
remote-ratchet/base key. The receive facade emits the decrypted message event,
acks it as a normal message, advances the stored receiving chain exactly once,
keeps the one-time pre-key consumed, preserves the stored identity, and rejects
a replay of the wrapped message with a parsing-error ACK while leaving the
session unchanged. This narrows the Phase 11 receive/decrypt integration gap
for consumed one-time pre-key replay handling through public client processing
APIs. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_missing_one_time_existing_session_same_base --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The offline receive same-base missing-one-time fallback slice mirrors that
client receive proof for offline batches. A new `wa-client` regression processes
an offline `pkmsg`, consumes the one-time pre-key, then processes the next
provider-session ciphertext inside an offline `pkmsg` that reuses the consumed
pre-key id and the current remote-ratchet/base key. Offline processing emits
the decrypted event, sends a normal ACK, advances the stored receiving chain
once, keeps the pre-key consumed, preserves identity, and rejects a replay with
a parsing-error ACK while preserving the session. This narrows the Phase 11
offline-node behavior gap for the same consumed one-time pre-key compatibility
case covered by the direct receive facade. It was verified in the current VM
with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_offline_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_offline_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The spawned incoming processor same-base missing-one-time fallback slice lifts
the direct receive proof into the background raw-node pump. The existing
spawned-processor raw pre-key regression now sends the sender's next
provider-session ciphertext as a same-base `pkmsg` using the consumed pre-key
id, verifies the ACK, decrypted event, persistence, local pre-key consumption,
identity preservation, and receiving-chain advancement, rejects a replay of the
same wrapped ciphertext without a batch or session mutation, then continues
with a fresh normal provider-session `msg` to prove the spawned processor
remains in sync. This narrows the Phase 11 spawned receive/decrypt integration
gap for Baileys-compatible consumed one-time pre-key wrappers beyond the direct
and offline receive facades. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_decrypts_raw_pre_key_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_decrypts_raw_pre_key_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The spawned offline raw-node same-base missing-one-time fallback slice completes
the raw-node pump sibling of the direct, explicit-offline, and live-spawned
receive proofs. The existing spawned offline raw pre-key regression now wraps
the sender's next provider-session ciphertext in an offline `pkmsg` that reuses
the consumed one-time pre-key id and stored base key, verifies ACK, decrypted
event, persistence, pre-key consumption, identity preservation, and
receiving-chain advancement, rejects a replay of that wrapped offline child
without emitting a batch or mutating the session, then continues with a fresh
offline normal provider-session `msg` to prove the spawned processor remains in
sync across offline batches. This narrows the Phase 11 spawned offline
receive/decrypt integration gap for Baileys-compatible consumed one-time
pre-key wrappers. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_decrypts_offline_raw_pre_key_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_decrypts_offline_raw_pre_key_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_decrypts_raw_pre_key_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_offline_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The media-capable spawned Signal-provider pump same-base fallback slice carries
the consumed one-time-pre-key wrapper proof into the combined placeholder,
retry-resend, and media-retry processor. A new
`wa-client` regression starts
`spawn_incoming_processor_with_placeholder_retry_and_media_retry_with_signal_provider`,
decrypts an initial store-backed `pkmsg`, then sends the sender's next
provider-session ciphertext as another same-base `pkmsg` through that combined
raw-node pump. The test verifies ACKs, decrypted message events, persistence,
local pre-key consumption, identity preservation, receiving-chain advancement,
replay rejection without a batch or session mutation, and a subsequent normal
provider-session `msg` to prove the side-effect-capable pump remains in sync.
This narrows the Phase 11 automatic raw-node pump integration gap beyond the
basic live/offline Signal-provider processors by covering the media-capable
combined processor used for placeholder, retry, and media-retry workflows. It
was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_placeholder_retry_media_and_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_placeholder_retry_media_and_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_placeholder_retry_media_and_signal_provider_preserves_legacy_missing_key --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_decrypts_raw_pre_key_message --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The retry-resend spawned Signal-provider pump same-base fallback slice carries
the consumed one-time-pre-key wrapper proof into the automatic retry-resend
processor. A new `wa-client` regression starts
`spawn_incoming_processor_with_retry_resend_with_signal_provider`, decrypts an
initial store-backed `pkmsg`, then sends the sender's next provider-session
ciphertext as a same-base `pkmsg` through the retry-capable raw-node pump. The
test verifies ACKs, decrypted events, persistence, local pre-key consumption,
identity preservation, receiving-chain advancement, replay rejection without a
batch or session mutation, no retry statistics side effect, and a subsequent
normal provider-session `msg` to prove the retry-resend pump remains in sync
for ordinary message nodes. This narrows the Phase 11 automatic raw-node pump
integration gap separately from the basic Signal-provider processor and the
media-capable combined processor. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The placeholder-plus-retry spawned Signal-provider pump same-base fallback

The placeholder-only spawned Signal-provider pump same-base fallback slice

The media-retry-only spawned Signal-provider pump same-base fallback slice
carries the consumed one-time-pre-key wrapper proof into the automatic
media-retry processor without the placeholder/retry-resend branches. A new
`wa-client` regression starts
`spawn_incoming_processor_with_media_retry_with_signal_provider`, decrypts an
initial store-backed `pkmsg`, then sends the sender's next provider-session
ciphertext as a same-base `pkmsg` through the media-retry-capable raw-node
pump. The test verifies ACKs, decrypted events, persistence, local pre-key
consumption, identity preservation, receiving-chain advancement, replay
rejection without a batch or session mutation, and a subsequent normal
provider-session `msg` to prove the media-retry-only pump remains in sync for
ordinary message nodes. This narrows the Phase 11 automatic raw-node pump
integration gap separately from the media-capable placeholder/retry/media
processor and the placeholder/retry-only processors. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_media_retry_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_media_retry_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_media_retry_signal_provider_preserves_legacy_retry_key --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_media_retry_signal_provider_replays_legacy_stored_events_on_start --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_placeholder_retry_media_and_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The direct placeholder-resend Signal-provider facade same-base fallback slice
carries the consumed one-time-pre-key wrapper proof into the explicit
placeholder-resend receive wrapper. A new `wa-client` regression processes an
initial store-backed `pkmsg` through
`process_incoming_node_with_placeholder_resend_with_signal_provider`, then
sends the sender's next provider-session ciphertext as a same-base `pkmsg`
through that facade. The test verifies ACKs, decrypted events, persistence,
local pre-key consumption, identity preservation, receiving-chain advancement,
replay rejection with a parsing-error ACK without placeholder resend, batch
emission, or session mutation, and a subsequent normal provider-session `msg`
to prove the facade remains in sync while ordinary messages do not trigger
placeholder resend side effects. This narrows the Phase 11 explicit
convenience-facade integration gap separately from the spawned
placeholder-resend pump. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_placeholder_resend_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

then sends the sender's next provider-session ciphertext as a same-base

persistence, local pre-key consumption, identity preservation,
receiving-chain advancement, replay rejection with a parsing-error ACK without
placeholder resend, retry-resend, media-retry, batch emission, retry statistic
movement, or session mutation, and a subsequent normal provider-session `msg`
to prove the facade remains in sync while ordinary messages do not trigger

The offline placeholder/retry/media Signal-provider facade same-base fallback
slice carries the consumed one-time-pre-key wrapper proof into the explicit
combined offline-node receive wrapper. A new `wa-client` regression processes
an initial store-backed offline `pkmsg` through
`process_offline_node_with_placeholder_retry_and_media_retry_with_signal_provider`,
then sends the sender's next provider-session ciphertext as a same-base
offline `pkmsg` through that facade. The test verifies ACKs, decrypted events,
persistence, local pre-key consumption, identity preservation,
receiving-chain advancement, replay rejection with a parsing-error ACK without
placeholder resend, retry-resend, media-retry, batch emission, retry statistic
movement, or session mutation, and a subsequent normal provider-session
offline `msg` to prove the facade remains in sync while ordinary offline
messages do not trigger combined side effects. This narrows the Phase 11
offline explicit convenience-facade integration gap separately from the basic
offline Signal-provider wrapper and the spawned/direct combined pumps. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_offline_node_with_placeholder_retry_media_and_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_offline_node_with_placeholder_retry_media_and_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`

The basic direct/offline Signal-provider same-base recovery slice closes the
post-replay gap in the base receive wrappers. The existing `wa-client`
regressions for
`process_incoming_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper` and
`process_offline_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper` now send a
normal provider-session `msg` after the replayed same-base `pkmsg` is rejected.
They verify the normal ACK/event path still works, the decrypted payload is
emitted, the receiving chain advances to counter 3, the stored remote ratchet
key is preserved, and no skipped message keys remain. This keeps the basic
direct and offline facades aligned with the side-effect-capable wrappers that
already prove recovery after replay rejection. It was verified in the current
VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_offline_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_offline_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The inbound call timeout receive-path slice closes a Phase 11 coverage gap for
live call stanza processing through the public client wrapper. The new
`wa-client` regression
`process_incoming_node_enriches_call_timeout_from_persisted_offer` sends a
one-to-one video call `offer` through `Client::process_incoming_node`, verifies
the call ACK shape, emitted `calls_update`, and persisted offer cache, then
sends a later `timeout` stanza for the same call. It verifies the terminal call
event is enriched from the persisted offer metadata, the derived missed-video
call message is emitted and stored with the expected stub fields, and the
offer cache is removed after the terminal event. This covers the actual inbound
client path rather than only the lower-level call persistence helpers. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_enriches_call_timeout_from_persisted_offer --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_enriches_call_timeout_from_persisted_offer --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The inbound group-call offer receive-path slice extends the live call stanza
coverage beyond missed-call timeouts. The new `wa-client` regression
`process_incoming_node_emits_group_call_offer_message` sends a group video call
`offer` through `Client::process_incoming_node`, verifies the call ACK shape,
the emitted and persisted `calls_update`, persisted offer-cache entry, and the
separate derived `Message.call` upsert for the group call offer. The test
decodes the generated protobuf payload and checks that the call key, group
metadata, caller PN, video/group flags, and stored message event all match the
incoming call stanza. This covers Baileys-style group-call offer message
generation through the public inbound client wrapper rather than only through
the lower-level call-event mapper. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_emits_group_call_offer_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_emits_group_call_offer_message --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The offline group-call timeout receive-path slice closes the same-call
offer/timeout collision that can happen when derived call messages share the
call id as their message key. The client-side derived-message appender now
keeps real message upserts authoritative but lets a generated call `timeout`
stub replace an earlier generated group-call `offer` upsert for the same key.
The new `wa-client` regression
`process_offline_node_prefers_group_call_timeout_stub_over_offer_message`
sends an offline batch containing a group video call `offer` followed by its
`timeout`, verifies both call ACKs and enriched `calls_update` records, then
asserts that the emitted and persisted derived message is the offline
missed-group-video stub with inherited offer metadata rather than the earlier
`Message.call` offer payload. It also verifies terminal cache cleanup. This
narrows the broader call edge-case gap for offline batches where Baileys-style
call-derived messages update the same conversation/message key. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_offline_node_prefers_group_call_timeout_stub_over_offer_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_offline_node_prefers_group_call_timeout_stub_over_offer_message --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The spawned raw-node call timeout slice promotes the offline group-call
offer/timeout behavior through the automatic processor path. The new
`wa-client` regression
`incoming_processor_prefers_group_call_timeout_stub_over_offer_message` starts
`spawn_incoming_processor`, injects an encoded offline raw node containing a
group video call `offer` followed by its `timeout`, verifies the processor
sends both call ACKs, emits enriched `calls_update` records, and emits/stores
only the missed-group-video stub as the final derived message for the shared
call id. It also verifies terminal offer-cache cleanup through the spawned
path. This narrows the Phase 11 automatic raw-node pump integration gap for
call-derived message updates. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_prefers_group_call_timeout_stub_over_offer_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_prefers_group_call_timeout_stub_over_offer_message --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The incoming-node group notification derived-stub slice promotes the
payload-bearing group notification message-stub path through the public client
wrapper. The new `wa-client` regression
`process_incoming_node_emits_group_notification_message_stub` sends a live
`w:gp2` group notification that changes the disappearing-message duration
through `Client::process_incoming_node`, verifies the notification ACK,
raw-node emission, parsed `groups_update`, and the separate derived
`messages_upsert` stub. The test decodes the generated
`ProtocolMessage::EphemeralSetting` payload, verifies the stored group update
and message event, and keeps the public receive path aligned with the lower
level group-notification mapper and persistence tests. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_emits_group_notification_message_stub --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_emits_group_notification_message_stub --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The spawned raw-node group notification derived-stub slice promotes the
payload-bearing group notification message-stub path through the automatic
processor. The new `wa-client` regression
`incoming_processor_emits_group_notification_message_stub` starts
`spawn_incoming_processor`, injects an encoded live `w:gp2` group notification
that changes the disappearing-message duration, verifies the notification ACK,
raw-node emission, parsed `groups_update`, and separate derived
`messages_upsert` stub. The test decodes the generated

update and message event. This narrows the Phase 11 automatic raw-node pump
integration gap for group-derived message updates. It was verified in the
current VM with:

The offline-node group notification derived-stub slice promotes the
payload-bearing group notification message-stub path through the explicit
offline receive wrapper. The new `wa-client` regression
`process_offline_node_emits_group_notification_message_stub` sends an
`offline` node containing a live `w:gp2` group notification that changes the
disappearing-message duration through `Client::process_offline_node`, verifies
the child notification ACK, raw child-node emission, parsed `groups_update`,
and the separate derived `messages_upsert` stub. The test decodes the
generated `ProtocolMessage::EphemeralSetting` payload, verifies the stored
group update and message event, and checks the offline child-processing
counters. This narrows the Phase 11 explicit offline-node notification edge
gap for group-derived message updates. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_offline_node_emits_group_notification_message_stub --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_offline_node_emits_group_notification_message_stub --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`

The offline group-notification append-kind slice tightens the Baileys-style
offline semantics for derived group message stubs. Group notification parsing
now preserves a non-empty raw `offline` marker on the `GroupUpdateEvent`, and
`group_message_events_from_group_update_events` emits offline-derived
group-notification message stubs with `kind=append` plus `offline=true` instead
of treating them as live `notify` inserts. The core regressions
`maps_group_notification_to_group_update_event` and
`maps_group_updates_to_group_notification_message_stubs` pin marker
preservation and derived append-kind behavior, while the existing
`wa-client` regression `process_offline_node_emits_group_notification_message_stub`
now verifies the public offline wrapper persists the same marker on both the
group update and generated `ProtocolMessage::EphemeralSetting` message stub.
This narrows the Phase 11 offline-node notification edge gap for
group-derived message updates. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise maps_group_notification_to_group_update_event --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise maps_group_updates_to_group_notification_message_stubs --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise maps_group_notification_to_group_update_event --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise maps_group_updates_to_group_notification_message_stubs --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_offline_node_emits_group_notification_message_stub --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_offline_node_emits_group_notification_message_stub --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The spawned offline group-notification append-stub slice promotes the offline
group notification append semantics through the automatic raw-node processor.
The new `wa-client` regression
`incoming_processor_emits_offline_group_notification_append_stub` starts
`spawn_incoming_processor`, injects an encoded `offline` node containing a
`w:gp2` disappearing-message notification, verifies the child notification ACK,
raw child-node emission, parsed `groups_update`, and derived
`messages_upsert` stub, then decodes the generated
`ProtocolMessage::EphemeralSetting` payload. It also verifies both persisted
records carry the offline marker and that the generated message uses
`kind=append`. This narrows the Phase 11 automatic raw-node pump gap for
offline group-derived message updates. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_emits_offline_group_notification_append_stub --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_emits_offline_group_notification_append_stub --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The combined offline placeholder/retry/media group-notification append-stub
slice promotes the same offline group notification append semantics through
the side-effect-capable offline convenience facade. The new `wa-client`
regression
`process_offline_node_with_placeholder_retry_media_and_signal_provider_emits_group_notification_append_stub`
drives
`process_offline_node_with_placeholder_retry_and_media_retry_with_signal_provider`
with an `offline` node containing a `w:gp2` disappearing-message notification,
verifies the child notification ACK, raw child-node emission, parsed
`groups_update`, and derived `messages_upsert` stub, decodes the generated
`ProtocolMessage::EphemeralSetting` payload, and verifies both persisted
records carry `offline=true` with the generated message using `kind=append`.
It also asserts that placeholder resend, retry resend, and media retry result
buckets remain empty for this notification-only path. This narrows the Phase 11
explicit side-effect facade gap for offline group-derived message updates. It
was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_offline_node_with_placeholder_retry_media_and_signal_provider_emits_group_notification_append_stub --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_offline_node_with_placeholder_retry_media_and_signal_provider_emits_group_notification_append_stub --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The spawned combined placeholder/retry/media group-notification append-stub
slice promotes the offline group notification append semantics through the
automatic side-effect-capable raw-node processor. The new `wa-client`
regression
`incoming_processor_with_placeholder_retry_media_and_signal_provider_emits_offline_group_notification_append_stub`
starts
`spawn_incoming_processor_with_placeholder_retry_and_media_retry_with_signal_provider`,
injects an encoded `offline` node containing a `w:gp2`
disappearing-message notification, verifies the child notification ACK, raw
child-node emission, parsed `groups_update`, and derived `messages_upsert`
stub, decodes the generated `ProtocolMessage::EphemeralSetting` payload, and
verifies both persisted records carry `offline=true` with the generated message
using `kind=append`. It also checks the notification-only path does not emit
extra media retry side-effect events or outbound side-effect frames. This
narrows the Phase 11 automatic raw-node pump gap for side-effect-capable
offline group-derived message updates. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_placeholder_retry_media_and_signal_provider_emits_offline_group_notification_append_stub --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_placeholder_retry_media_and_signal_provider_emits_offline_group_notification_append_stub --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The spawned media-retry-only group-notification append-stub slice promotes the
offline group notification append semantics through the automatic media-retry
raw-node processor without the placeholder/retry-resend branches. The new
`wa-client` regression
`incoming_processor_with_media_retry_signal_provider_emits_offline_group_notification_append_stub`
starts `spawn_incoming_processor_with_media_retry_with_signal_provider`,
injects an encoded `offline` node containing a `w:gp2`
disappearing-message notification, verifies the child notification ACK, raw
child-node emission, parsed `groups_update`, and derived `messages_upsert`
stub, decodes the generated `ProtocolMessage::EphemeralSetting` payload, and
verifies both persisted records carry `offline=true` with the generated message
using `kind=append`. It also checks the notification-only path does not emit
extra media retry side-effect events or outbound side-effect frames. This
narrows the Phase 11 automatic raw-node pump gap for media-retry-capable
offline group-derived message updates separately from the combined
placeholder/retry/media processor. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_media_retry_signal_provider_emits_offline_group_notification_append_stub --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_media_retry_signal_provider_emits_offline_group_notification_append_stub --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The spawned placeholder-resend-only group-notification append-stub slice
promotes the offline group notification append semantics through the automatic
placeholder-capable raw-node processor without retry/media branches. The new
`wa-client` regression
`incoming_processor_with_placeholder_resend_signal_provider_emits_offline_group_notification_append_stub`
starts `spawn_incoming_processor_with_placeholder_resend_with_signal_provider`,
injects an encoded `offline` node containing a `w:gp2`
disappearing-message notification, verifies the child notification ACK, raw
child-node emission, parsed `groups_update`, and derived `messages_upsert`
stub, decodes the generated `ProtocolMessage::EphemeralSetting` payload, and
verifies both persisted records carry `offline=true` with the generated message
using `kind=append`. It also checks the notification-only path does not emit
an outbound placeholder-resend side-effect frame. This narrows the Phase 11
automatic raw-node pump gap for placeholder-capable offline group-derived
message updates separately from the media-retry-capable processors. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_placeholder_resend_signal_provider_emits_offline_group_notification_append_stub --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_placeholder_resend_signal_provider_emits_offline_group_notification_append_stub --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The public incoming-node group participant-add stub slice broadens group
notification receive coverage beyond the disappearing-message payload stub.
The new `wa-client` regression
`process_incoming_node_emits_group_participant_add_message_stub` sends a live
`w:gp2` participant-add notification through `Client::process_incoming_node`,
verifies the notification ACK, raw-node emission, parsed `groups_update`
participant metadata, and derived non-payload `messages_upsert` stub, decodes
the generated JSON stub parameters for LID/PN/username/admin role metadata, and
verifies both persisted records match the emitted events. This narrows the
Phase 11/14 group-notification edge-case gap for public receive wrappers
separately from the existing ephemeral-setting payload coverage. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_emits_group_participant_add_message_stub --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_emits_group_participant_add_message_stub --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The public offline-node group participant-add append-stub slice carries the
non-payload participant metadata coverage into explicit offline processing.
The new `wa-client` regression
`process_offline_node_emits_group_participant_add_append_stub` sends an
`offline` node containing a `w:gp2` participant-add notification through
`Client::process_offline_node`, verifies child ACK/counter behavior, raw child
emission, parsed `groups_update` participant metadata with `offline=true`, and
the derived non-payload `messages_upsert` stub using `kind=append`. The test
decodes the generated JSON stub parameters for LID/PN/username/admin role
metadata and verifies both persisted records match the emitted events. This
narrows the Phase 11/14 offline group-notification edge-case gap separately
from the disappearing-message payload append coverage. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_offline_node_emits_group_participant_add_append_stub --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_offline_node_emits_group_participant_add_append_stub --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

checks no extra outbound frame is emitted for this notification-only path. This

- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The spawned placeholder+retry offline group participant-add append-stub slice
carries the non-payload participant metadata coverage through the automatic
placeholder-and-retry raw-node processor without the media branch. The new
`wa-client` regression
`incoming_processor_with_placeholder_retry_signal_provider_emits_offline_group_participant_add_append_stub`
starts
`spawn_incoming_processor_with_placeholder_and_retry_resend_with_signal_provider`,
injects an encoded `offline` node containing a `w:gp2` participant-add
notification, verifies the child notification ACK, raw child-node emission,
parsed `groups_update` participant metadata with `offline=true`, and derived
non-payload `messages_upsert` stub using `kind=append`, decodes the generated
JSON stub parameters for LID/PN/username/admin role metadata, verifies both
persisted records, and checks no outbound placeholder-resend or retry-resend
side-effect frame is emitted. This narrows the Phase 11/14 automatic
placeholder+retry raw-node pump gap for offline group-notification edge cases
beyond disappearing-message payload stubs. It was verified in the current VM
with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_placeholder_retry_signal_provider_emits_offline_group_participant_add_append_stub --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_placeholder_retry_signal_provider_emits_offline_group_participant_add_append_stub --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The store-backed sender-key same-signer stale-chain retry slice narrows the
group Signal provider cryptographic recovery gap called out in Section 13.
`sender_key_decrypt_error_can_retry_distribution` now treats authenticated
sender-key body decrypt failures as eligible for the existing
distribution-candidate retry path, while preserving the commit-after-success
transaction rule: a fresher distribution is only written after it decrypts the
group ciphertext. The new `wa-core` regression
`store_sender_key_provider_recovers_same_signer_stale_chain_from_distribution_after_decrypt_failure`
stores a valid but stale same-key/same-signer sender-key record, persists a
newer distribution for the same signer, proves a re-signed truncated ciphertext
fails without mutating the stale record, then decrypts a valid fresh group
payload and verifies the recovered sender-key state advances from the fresh
distribution. This covers the stale-chain class that authenticates the sender
signature but fails body decrypt before distribution retry. It was verified in
the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_sender_key_provider_recovers_same_signer_stale_chain_from_distribution_after_decrypt_failure --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_sender_key_provider_recovers_same_signer_stale_chain_from_distribution_after_decrypt_failure --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_sender_key_provider --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_sender_key_provider --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The Signal stateful sender-key same-signer stale-chain retry fuzz slice extends
the store-backed sender-key recovery work into the existing
`signal_stateful_records` harness. The fuzz target now drives a deterministic
same-key/same-signer stale receiver record, proves the fresh group ciphertext
authenticates but fails body decrypt against the stale chain, applies the newer
same-signer distribution as a candidate, checks a re-signed truncated candidate
ciphertext is rejected without changing the original stale record, and then
verifies the valid fresh payload decrypts with the recovered record advanced
from the distribution. This gives the Section 13 Signal-provider proof surface
stateful coverage for the same stale-chain distribution retry class covered by
the focused store-backed regression. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin signal_stateful_records --quiet`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target cargo clippy --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin signal_stateful_records -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_sender_key_provider_recovers_same_signer_stale_chain_from_distribution_after_decrypt_failure --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_sender_key_provider_recovers_same_signer_stale_chain_from_distribution_after_decrypt_failure --quiet`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && rm -rf /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target && test ! -e fuzz/target`

The Signal sender-key same-signer stale-chain retry fixture slice extends the
shared deterministic Signal manifest for the recovery path covered by the
store-backed regression and stateful fuzz harness. `wa-testkit` now has a typed
`sender_key_distribution_stale_chain_retry` vector that builds a stale
same-key/same-signer receiver record, applies a newer same-signer distribution
candidate, verifies the original stale record fails body decrypt with a validly
signed fresh ciphertext, verifies a re-signed truncated body is rejected by the
candidate, and verifies the valid fresh body decrypts with the recovered record
advanced from the distribution. The fixture pins deterministic record,
distribution, ciphertext, and recovered-record bytes while deliberately avoiding
full signed message bytes because sender-key signatures are nondeterministic.
This gives future Signal provider replacement work a stable compatibility vector
for the same stale-chain retry class. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-testkit signal_fixtures_match_public_signal_primitives --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-testkit --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `test ! -e fuzz/target && test ! -e /tmp/whatsapp-agent-fuzz-target`

The consumed one-time same-base identity-change guard slice tightens the
store-backed one-to-one Signal fallback that handles pre-key wrappers after the
local one-time pre-key has already been consumed. The existing
`store_signal_provider_decrypts_missing_one_time_existing_session_same_base`
regression now first wraps the next valid provider-session message in a
same-base `pkmsg` whose identity key belongs to a different sender identity,
verifies the decrypt is rejected as `Signal provider identity changed for
123.7`, and proves the persisted provider session, identity row, and consumed
pre-key state remain unchanged. The same test then decrypts the valid
same-identity wrapper and keeps the replay/no-rollback assertion for the
advanced state. This gives the consumed one-time fallback the same
identity-change preservation proof already present for signed-pre-key-only
same-base replays. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_missing_one_time_existing_session_same_base --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_missing_one_time_existing_session_same_base --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`

The client receive-facade consumed one-time same-base identity-change slice
promotes the new core Signal fallback guard into public inbound processing. The
`process_incoming_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper` regression
now injects a same-base `pkmsg` wrapper for the next provider-session message
with a different sender identity before the valid wrapper, verifies the receive
facade reports the identity-change error, emits a parsing-error ACK, emits no
message event, and preserves the provider session, identity row, and consumed
pre-key state. The same test then decrypts the valid same-identity wrapper,
rejects its replay without mutation, and continues with the next normal
`msg` ciphertext. This narrows the Phase 11 receive/decrypt integration gap for
consumed one-time pre-key fallback identity-change handling through the
client-facing raw-node path. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`

The offline receive consumed one-time same-base identity-change slice mirrors
the client-facing raw-node guard for batched offline nodes. The
`process_offline_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper` regression
now injects a same-base `pkmsg` wrapper with a changed sender identity before
the valid wrapper, verifies the offline processor records the identity-change
error, emits a parsing-error ACK, emits no typed message event, and preserves
the provider session, identity row, and consumed pre-key state. It then
continues to decrypt the valid same-identity wrapper, reject its replay without
mutation, and process the following normal `msg` ciphertext. This carries the
consumed one-time fallback identity-change invariant through the explicit
offline batch receive path. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_offline_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_offline_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`

The spawned live raw-node consumed one-time same-base identity-change slice
carries the same invariant into the automatic incoming processor. The
`incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_decrypts_raw_pre_key_message`
regression now sends a same-base `pkmsg` wrapper for the next provider-session
message with a changed sender identity before the valid wrapper, verifies the
spawned processor emits a parsing-error ACK and no typed batch, and proves the
provider session, identity row, and consumed pre-key state remain unchanged. It
then decrypts the valid wrapper, rejects its replay without mutation, and
continues with the next normal `msg` ciphertext. This narrows the Phase 11
automatic raw-node pump gap for consumed one-time pre-key fallback
identity-change handling. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_decrypts_raw_pre_key_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_decrypts_raw_pre_key_message --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`

The spawned offline raw-node consumed one-time same-base identity-change slice
mirrors the spawned live guard for offline wrapper batches. The
`incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_decrypts_offline_raw_pre_key_message`
regression now sends a same-base `pkmsg` wrapper with a changed sender identity
inside an `offline` node before the valid wrapper, verifies the spawned
processor emits a parsing-error ACK and no typed batch, and proves the provider
session, identity row, and consumed pre-key state remain unchanged. It then
decrypts the valid same-identity wrapper, rejects its replay without mutation,
and continues with the next normal offline `msg` ciphertext. This carries the
consumed one-time fallback identity-change invariant through the spawned
offline raw-node pump. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_decrypts_offline_raw_pre_key_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_decrypts_offline_raw_pre_key_message --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`

The direct retry-resend Signal-provider facade consumed one-time same-base
identity-change slice carries the same fallback guard through the retry-aware
receive facade. The
`process_incoming_node_with_retry_resend_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper`
regression now injects a same-base `pkmsg` wrapper with a changed sender
identity before the valid wrapper, verifies the inbound result reports the
identity-change error, emits a parsing-error ACK, emits no typed message event,
does not schedule retry resend work, and preserves the provider session,
identity row, and consumed pre-key state. It then decrypts the valid
same-identity wrapper, rejects its replay without mutation, and processes the
following normal `msg` ciphertext. This extends the consumed one-time fallback
identity-change invariant into the retry-resend side-effect facade. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_retry_resend_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_retry_resend_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`

The spawned combined placeholder/retry/media Signal-provider pump consumed
