- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_placeholder_resend_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_placeholder_resend_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`

The status/broadcast store-backed Signal-provider invalid-target slice pins the
provider facade to the same preflight validation contract as the generic
status-send path. The
`send_status_text_with_signal_provider_rejects_non_user_targets_before_device_lookup`
regression now rejects group, broadcast, device-scoped, malformed device, and
empty status targets before any mock socket query or relay stanza is emitted,
while still constructing the loaded provider-backed facade from authenticated
credentials. This closes a status/broadcast edge case around store-backed
Signal-provider sends and prevents invalid audience inputs from reaching device
discovery or provider encryption. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_status_text_with_signal_provider_rejects_non_user_targets_before_device_lookup --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_status_text_with_signal_provider_rejects_non_user_targets_before_device_lookup --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`

The status/broadcast store-backed Signal-provider unusable-recipient slice
extends the provider facade failure-boundary coverage beyond invalid audience
syntax. The
`send_status_text_with_signal_provider_stops_before_provider_mutation_when_recipients_unusable`
regression now drives a status send where USync returns no usable target or own
devices, then a second send where the E2E session assertion query fails. Both
paths reject before any relay stanza is emitted and leave the provider session
and identity records for the audience JID absent, proving the loaded
store-backed provider is not mutated before recipient discovery/session
preconditions pass. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_status_text_with_signal_provider_stops_before_provider_mutation_when_recipients_unusable --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_status_text_with_signal_provider_stops_before_provider_mutation_when_recipients_unusable --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --lib -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`

The live media-retry e2e harness cfg-cleanup slice removes a recurring warning
from default-feature `wa-client` test builds while keeping the ignored
`http-media` response-roundtrip smoke available. The
`media_retry_kind_env` and `live_media_retry_uploaded_media` helpers in
`crates/wa-client/tests/live_e2e.rs` are now compiled only when the
`http-media` gated live media-retry response test is compiled, so focused
default `wa-client` test runs no longer emit the known dead-code warnings for
those helpers. This keeps the live media-retry validation surface clean without
changing runtime media retry behavior. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_status_text_with_signal_provider_stops_before_provider_mutation_when_recipients_unusable --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_status_text_with_signal_provider_stops_before_provider_mutation_when_recipients_unusable --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`

The group-notification participant-remove/leave receive slice promotes another
typed mapper edge through the client facade and persisted message-stub side
effects. The
`process_incoming_node_emits_group_participant_remove_message_stub` regression
now drives a live `w:gp2` remove notification where the actor removes a
different participant, verifying notification ACK shape, raw-node delivery,
typed `GroupsUpdate` participant metadata, a `GroupParticipantRemove` message
stub with Baileys-style participant JSON parameters, and stored group/message
events. The `process_offline_node_emits_group_participant_leave_append_stub`
regression then drives the offline self-remove form and verifies the
`participants_remove_is_leave`/`participants_leave` projection plus append-kind
`GroupParticipantLeave` stub persistence. This narrows the remaining group
notification edge-case gap beyond the previously landed participant-add stub
coverage. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_emits_group_participant_remove_message_stub --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_offline_node_emits_group_participant_leave_append_stub --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_emits_group_participant_remove_message_stub --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_offline_node_emits_group_participant_leave_append_stub --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`

The group-notification participant-promote/change-number receive slice extends
the client facade proof beyond add/remove/leave participant stubs. The
`process_incoming_node_emits_group_participant_promote_message_stub` regression
now drives a live `w:gp2` promote notification through `process_incoming_node`,
verifying ACK shape, raw-node delivery, typed `GroupsUpdate` participant
metadata including Baileys-style LID/PN/username aliases, a
`GroupParticipantPromote` message stub with participant JSON parameters, and
stored group/message events. The
`process_offline_node_emits_group_participant_change_number_append_stub`
regression drives an offline `modify` notification through `process_offline_node`
and verifies the append-kind `GroupParticipantChangeNumber` stub plus persisted
typed event fields. This further narrows the remaining group-notification
edge-case gap around participant role/change events. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_emits_group_participant_promote_message_stub --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_offline_node_emits_group_participant_change_number_append_stub --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_emits_group_participant_promote_message_stub --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_offline_node_emits_group_participant_change_number_append_stub --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`

The spawned raw-node group-notification participant-demote slice closes the
remaining participant role-change stub type in the automatic receive pump. The
`incoming_processor_emits_group_participant_demote_message_stub` regression now
starts `spawn_incoming_processor`, injects a live `w:gp2` demote notification,
and verifies notification ACK shape, raw-node delivery, typed `GroupsUpdate`
participant alias metadata, `GroupParticipantDemote` message-stub generation
with Baileys-style participant JSON parameters, store persistence for both group
and message events, and absence of extra outbound frames after the ACK. This
extends the explicit promote/change-number facade coverage into the spawned
raw-node receive path. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`

The direct group-notification participant-demote facade slice closes the
remaining explicit client receive-path asymmetry in the participant role-change
family. The `process_incoming_node_emits_group_participant_demote_message_stub`
regression now drives a live `w:gp2` demote notification through

`participants_demote_*` alias fields without role metadata,

The group picture-notification client receive slice promotes the top-level
`picture` notification mapper through the high-level facade. The
`process_incoming_node_emits_group_picture_icon_stub_and_contact_update`
regression now drives a live group picture `set` notification through
`process_incoming_node`, verifies notification ACK shape, raw-node delivery,
the combined typed batch containing both `GroupsUpdate` picture fields and
`ContactsUpdate` image-state fields, the derived `GroupChangeIcon` message stub
with Baileys-style picture id parameters, and persistence of the group, contact,
and message events. This narrows the group-notification edge-case gap for
picture updates beyond the core mapper-only coverage. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`

The offline group picture-remove receive slice covers the complementary icon
delete path through the client facade. The
`process_offline_node_emits_group_picture_remove_icon_append_stub_and_contact_update`
regression now drives an offline top-level group `picture` delete notification
through `process_offline_node`, verifies notification ACK shape, raw-node
delivery, a combined typed batch with `picture_removed=true` group fields plus
the matching `ContactsUpdate` image-state removal, and an append-kind
`GroupChangeIcon` message stub without picture-id parameters. It also verifies
persistence of the group, contact, and message events, extending the prior live
picture-set proof to offline delete semantics. It was verified in the current
VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise group_participant_invite_message_stub --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise group_participant_accept_fallback_message_stub --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise group_invite_revoke_append_stub --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise group_participant_invite_message_stub --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise group_participant_accept_fallback_message_stub --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise group_invite_revoke_append_stub --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`

The business notification alias receive slice expands the generic business
notification mapper beyond raw nested-field retention. Recognized business
notification children now preserve Baileys-style parent actor PN and username
aliases as `actor_pn`/`actor_username`, while invalid actor PN aliases fail
recognized business updates instead of being silently stored. The
`process_incoming_node_emits_business_notification_alias_metadata` regression
drives a live `business` notification through `process_incoming_node`, verifying
ACK shape, raw-node delivery, typed `BusinessNotificationEvent` alias metadata,
hyphenated attribute normalization, nested bytes-to-hex retention, and native
store persistence. The
`incoming_processor_emits_business_notification_alias_metadata` regression
mirrors the same alias and nested-byte coverage through `spawn_incoming_processor`,
proving the automatic raw-node pump emits and persists the business update
without extra outbound frames after the ACK. This narrows the extended-surface
business notification and automatic receive-pump gaps called out in Section 13.
It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core maps_business_notifications_to_typed_events --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features maps_business_notifications_to_typed_events --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_emits_business_notification_alias_metadata --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_emits_business_notification_alias_metadata --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_emits_business_notification_alias_metadata --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_emits_business_notification_alias_metadata --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`

The newsletter receive-facade slice promotes the existing newsletter
notification mappers through the public client path. The
`process_incoming_node_emits_newsletter_notification_events` regression now
drives a live newsletter notification through `process_incoming_node`, verifying
ACK shape, raw-node delivery, reaction/view/participant/settings typed events,
plaintext newsletter message batch coalescing, decoded protobuf payload, and
native persistence for every emitted newsletter/message record. The
`process_incoming_node_emits_newsletter_mex_admin_promotion_update` regression
drives a nested `NotificationNewsletterAdminPromote` WMex notification through
the same facade, verifying typed participant promotion metadata and persisted
newsletter participant state. This narrows the extended-surface newsletter
send/receive and MEX-admin-promotion receive gap called out in Section 13. It
was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise newsletter_notification_events --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise newsletter_mex_admin_promotion --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise newsletter_notification_events --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise newsletter_mex_admin_promotion --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`

The newsletter automatic receive-pump slice mirrors the public facade coverage
through `spawn_incoming_processor`. The
`incoming_processor_emits_newsletter_notification_events` regression now sends a
wire-encoded newsletter notification through the mock connection, verifying ACK
shape, decoded raw-node delivery, reaction/view/participant/settings typed
events, plaintext newsletter message batch coalescing, decoded protobuf payload,
native persistence for every emitted newsletter/message record, and no extra
outbound frames. The
`incoming_processor_emits_newsletter_mex_settings_update` regression drives a
nested `NotificationNewsletterUpdate` WMex payload with `newsletter_id` aliases
through the automatic pump, and the
`incoming_processor_emits_newsletter_mex_admin_promotion_update` regression
drives a nested `NotificationNewsletterAdminPromote` payload with
`participantJid` aliases through the same path. Together they close the
spawned receive proof for newsletter notification and MEX settings/admin
promotion updates that was still missing after the direct
`process_incoming_node` coverage. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_emits_newsletter --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_emits_newsletter --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`

The business notification alias-child receive slice broadens the generic
business notification mapper beyond the original profile/catalog/product/
collection/order/cover-photo child names. Core now recognizes
`business_profile_update`, `profile_update`, `product_catalog_update`,
`catalog_update`, collection add/edit/delete/update aliases, product
add/edit/delete aliases, `order_status_update`, and cart/cart-update
notification children, preserving their normalized attributes, text, and nested
fields in durable `BusinessNotificationEvent`s. The
`maps_business_notifications_to_typed_events` regression now covers the alias
set plus parent `phoneNumber`/`senderUsername` actor metadata, and the
`processes_business_notification_alias_children_to_typed_batch_and_ack`
regression proves multi-alias business updates coalesce into a typed batch with
the expected ACK. The fuzz structured generator now emits
`product_catalog_update`, `collection_update`, and `cart_update` forms. The
`incoming_processor_emits_business_notification_alias_children` regression drives
the alias set through `spawn_incoming_processor`, verifying ACK shape, decoded
raw-node delivery, typed business events, native persistence for each record, and
no extra outbound frames. This narrows the missing business-notification and
automatic receive-pump gaps called out in Section 13. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core maps_business_notifications_to_typed_events --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core processes_business_notification_alias_children_to_typed_batch_and_ack --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features maps_business_notifications_to_typed_events --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features processes_business_notification_alias_children_to_typed_batch_and_ack --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_emits_business_notification_alias_children --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_emits_business_notification_alias_children --quiet`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/fuzz-check cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin business_notification_parse --quiet`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/fuzz-check cargo clippy --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin business_notification_parse -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`

The community create-result alias slice broadens the community create facade
without losing the direct metadata fallback. Core
`parse_community_create_result_jid` now accepts create result stubs carried in
the result stanza `from`/`jid` attributes or an empty `<community id|jid="...">`
child, while still treating full `<community subject=...>` metadata as a direct
metadata response and preserving missing-id/invalid-JID errors for stub forms.
The community fuzz target now drives `parse_community_create_result_jid` and
generates group, empty-community, root-`from`, root-`jid`, and direct metadata
create-result shapes. The
`community_create_methods_accept_result_jid_aliases` regression verifies the
client follows root and empty-community stubs with a metadata fetch and still
returns full direct `<community>` metadata without sending an extra follow-up
query. This narrows the broader community edge-case gap called out in Section
13. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise builds_community_links_membership_invites_and_settings --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise builds_community_links_membership_invites_and_settings --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise community_create_methods_accept_result_jid_aliases --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise community_create_methods_accept_result_jid_aliases --quiet`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/fuzz-check cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin community_surface_parse --quiet`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/fuzz-check cargo clippy --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin community_surface_parse -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`

The community result-wrapper alias slice broadens read-side community parsing
for participating and linked-subgroup responses. Core
`parse_community_participating_result` now accepts either `<communities>` with
`community` children or group-style `<groups>` wrappers with `group`/`community`
children, reusing the existing metadata parser and validation. Core
`parse_community_linked_groups` now accepts `<sub_groups>`, `<linked_groups>`,
and `<groups>` wrappers with `group`, `linked_group`, or `community` children
while preserving linked-group JID/owner/creation/size validation. The community
fuzz target now generates the wrapper aliases. The
`community_read_methods_accept_group_style_result_wrappers` regression verifies
the public client facade parses group-wrapper participating communities and
`linked_groups`/`groups` linked-subgroup aliases through the mock connection,
including owner normalization and no extra outbound frames. This narrows the
broader community read edge-case gap called out in Section 13. It was verified
in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise parses_community_metadata_participating_and_linked_groups --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise parses_community_metadata_participating_and_linked_groups --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise community_read_methods_accept_group_style_result_wrappers --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise community_read_methods_accept_group_style_result_wrappers --quiet`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/fuzz-check cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin community_surface_parse --quiet`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/fuzz-check cargo clippy --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin community_surface_parse -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`

The same-base pre-key wrapper signed-pre-key validation slice tightens the
project-owned Signal provider fallback for consumed one-time and
signed-pre-key-only pre-key wrappers. Store-backed same-base fallback now
validates the wrapper signed-pre-key id against local credential material when
that material is available before decrypting the embedded session message, while
preserving the existing replay rejection path that can run before credential
material is loaded. The core regressions
`store_signal_provider_decrypts_missing_one_time_existing_session_same_base`
and `store_signal_provider_decrypts_inbound_pre_key_without_one_time_pre_key`
now inject same-base wrappers with the correct base key and identity but a
mismatched signed-pre-key id, verify the mismatch is rejected before session
advancement, and prove the existing provider session, identity, and consumed or
unrelated one-time pre-key state remain unchanged. This narrows the remaining
Signal provider parity gap around pre-key wrapper fallback validation. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_missing_one_time_existing_session_same_base --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_inbound_pre_key_without_one_time_pre_key --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_missing_one_time_existing_session_same_base --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_decrypts_inbound_pre_key_without_one_time_pre_key --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-core/src/signal.rs`

The public same-base pre-key wrapper signed-pre-key validation slice promotes
the core fallback guard through the direct client receive facade.
`process_incoming_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper` now sends a
same-base `pkmsg` wrapper with the correct consumed one-time pre-key id, base
key, and sender identity but a mismatched signed-pre-key id through
`process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider`, verifies the public path emits a
parsing-error ACK with no typed message batch, and proves the client-owned
provider session, identity, and consumed one-time pre-key state remain unchanged
before the later valid same-base wrapper advances the session. This narrows the
Phase 11 receive/decrypt integration gap for consumed one-time fallback
validation at the public facade boundary. It was verified in the current VM
with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs`

The automatic same-base pre-key wrapper signed-pre-key validation slice mirrors
the direct public-facade proof through `spawn_incoming_processor`. The
`incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_decrypts_raw_pre_key_message`
regression now injects a wire-encoded same-base `pkmsg` wrapper with the
correct consumed one-time pre-key id, base key, and sender identity but a
mismatched signed-pre-key id, verifies the spawned raw-node receive pump emits a
parsing-error ACK with no typed message batch, and proves the client-owned
provider session, identity, and consumed one-time pre-key state remain unchanged
before the later valid same-base wrapper advances the session. This narrows the
Phase 11 automatic receive-pump gap for consumed one-time fallback validation.
It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_decrypts_raw_pre_key_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_decrypts_raw_pre_key_message --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The offline automatic same-base pre-key wrapper signed-pre-key validation slice
extends the same fallback proof through offline stanza pumping.
`incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_decrypts_offline_raw_pre_key_message`
now injects an `<offline>`-wrapped same-base `pkmsg` with the correct consumed
one-time pre-key id, base key, and sender identity but a mismatched
signed-pre-key id, verifies the spawned receive pump emits a parsing-error ACK
with no typed batch, and proves the client-owned provider session, identity,
and consumed one-time pre-key state remain unchanged before the later valid
offline same-base wrapper advances the session. This narrows the Phase 11
offline receive-pump gap for consumed one-time fallback validation. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_decrypts_offline_raw_pre_key_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_decrypts_offline_raw_pre_key_message --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The public and retry-resend same-base pre-key wrapper signed-pre-key
validation correction tightens the facade coverage placement. The plain
`process_incoming_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper` regression
now owns the direct `process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider` proof for a
same-base wrapper with a mismatched signed-pre-key id, including parsing-error
ACK, no typed batch, and unchanged session, identity, and consumed pre-key
state before the later valid wrapper advances. The retry-resend direct
same-base regression now routes its own signed-pre-key mismatch frame through
`process_incoming_node_with_retry_resend_with_signal_provider`, proving the
retry-resend facade also returns no retry resend and preserves the same state on
that rejection. This narrows the Phase 11 direct receive and retry-resend
facade validation gap for consumed one-time fallback wrappers. It was verified
in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_retry_resend_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_retry_resend_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The spawned retry-resend same-base pre-key wrapper signed-pre-key validation
slice extends the mismatch proof through the automatic retry-resend receive
pump. `incoming_processor_with_retry_resend_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper`
now injects a same-base `pkmsg` wrapper with the correct consumed one-time
pre-key id, base key, and sender identity but a mismatched signed-pre-key id
after the identity-change rejection, verifies the spawned retry-resend pump
emits only a parsing-error ACK with no typed batch or retry-resend side effect,
and proves the provider session, identity, consumed one-time pre-key state, and
retry statistics remain unchanged before the later valid wrapper advances the
session. This narrows the Phase 11 automatic retry-resend receive-pump gap for
consumed one-time fallback validation. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_retry_resend_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_retry_resend_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The spawned placeholder-retry same-base pre-key wrapper signed-pre-key
validation slice carries the same mismatch proof through the combined
placeholder and retry-resend receive pump.
`incoming_processor_with_placeholder_retry_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper`
now injects a same-base `pkmsg` wrapper with the correct consumed one-time
pre-key id, base key, and sender identity but a mismatched signed-pre-key id
after the identity-change rejection, verifies the spawned combined pump emits

The direct media-retry same-base pre-key wrapper signed-pre-key validation
slice extends the mismatch proof through
`process_incoming_node_with_media_retry_with_signal_provider`.
`process_incoming_node_with_media_retry_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper`
now injects a same-base `pkmsg` wrapper with the correct consumed one-time
pre-key id, base key, and sender identity but a mismatched signed-pre-key id
after the identity-change rejection, verifies the facade returns a
parsing-error ACK with no typed batch or media-retry side effect, and proves the
provider session, identity, and consumed one-time pre-key state remain unchanged
before the later valid wrapper advances the session. This narrows the Phase 11
direct media-retry receive facade gap for consumed one-time fallback
validation. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_media_retry_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_media_retry_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The direct combined placeholder/retry/media same-base pre-key wrapper
signed-pre-key validation slice extends the mismatch proof through
`process_incoming_node_with_placeholder_retry_and_media_retry_with_signal_provider`.

pre-key id, base key, and sender identity but a mismatched signed-pre-key id
after the identity-change rejection, verifies the facade returns a
parsing-error ACK with no typed batch, placeholder resend, retry resend, or
media-retry side effect, and proves the provider session, identity, and
consumed one-time pre-key state remain unchanged before the later valid wrapper
advances the session. This narrows the Phase 11 direct combined receive facade
gap for consumed one-time fallback validation. It was verified in the current
VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_placeholder_retry_media_and_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper --quiet`

The offline combined placeholder/retry/media same-base pre-key wrapper
signed-pre-key validation slice extends the mismatch proof through
`process_offline_node_with_placeholder_retry_and_media_retry_with_signal_provider`.
`process_offline_node_with_placeholder_retry_media_and_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper`
now injects an `<offline>`-wrapped same-base `pkmsg` with the correct consumed
one-time pre-key id, base key, and sender identity but a mismatched
signed-pre-key id after the identity-change rejection, verifies the combined
offline facade emits a parsing-error ACK with no typed batch, placeholder
resend, retry resend, or media-retry side effect, and proves the provider
session, identity, consumed one-time pre-key state, and retry statistics remain
unchanged before the later valid wrapper advances the session. This narrows the
Phase 11 offline combined receive facade gap for consumed one-time fallback
validation. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The spawned media-retry same-base pre-key wrapper signed-pre-key validation
slice extends the mismatch proof through the automatic media-retry receive
pump. `incoming_processor_with_media_retry_signal_provider_accepts_same_base_pre_key_wrapper`
now injects a same-base `pkmsg` wrapper with the correct consumed one-time
pre-key id, base key, and sender identity but a mismatched signed-pre-key id
after the identity-change rejection, verifies the spawned media-retry pump emits
only a parsing-error ACK with no typed batch or media-retry side effect, and
proves the provider session, identity, and consumed one-time pre-key state remain
unchanged before the later valid wrapper advances the session. This narrows the

validation. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

pre-key id, base key, and sender identity but a mismatched signed-pre-key id
after the identity-change rejection, verifies the spawned combined pump emits

after the identity-change rejection, verifies the spawned placeholder-resend
pump emits only a parsing-error ACK with no typed batch and no extra outbound

The store-backed Signal provider far-future counter preservation slice promotes
two fixture-backed one-to-one ratchet bounds into the native session store path.
`store_signal_provider_rejects_far_future_counters_without_mutation` now stores
provider session and identity records, rejects both an active-chain message whose
counter jumps beyond the configured Signal forward limit and a new-ratchet
message whose previous-chain counter jumps beyond that limit, proves the stored
session bytes are unchanged after each rejection, and then decrypts the valid
current-chain message for each JID. This narrows the remaining Signal provider
parity gap around store-backed one-to-one ratchet edge cases and failed-decrypt
state preservation beyond the direct primitive and deterministic fixture
coverage. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core store_signal_provider_rejects_far_future_counters_without_mutation --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_rejects_far_future_counters_without_mutation --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-core/src/signal.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The store-backed Signal provider previous-chain consumed-replay preservation
slice tightens the multi-skipped-key ratchet path after an inbound new-ratchet
step. `store_signal_provider_consumes_multiple_previous_chain_skipped_keys_after_ratchet`
now replays an old-chain message after that skipped key has already been
consumed, verifies the replay is rejected as a backwards previous-chain counter,
proves the persisted session bytes remain unchanged, and then continues with the
remaining old-chain skipped key plus the next valid new-chain message. This
narrows the remaining store-backed one-to-one ratchet edge-case gap around
previous-chain skipped-key replay handling after consumption. It was verified in
the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core store_signal_provider_consumes_multiple_previous_chain_skipped_keys_after_ratchet --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_consumes_multiple_previous_chain_skipped_keys_after_ratchet --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-core/src/signal.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The client receive-path previous-chain consumed-replay preservation slice lifts
the store-backed skipped-key replay guard through the direct and automatic
receive facades. `process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider_accepts_new_remote_ratchet`,
`incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_accepts_new_remote_ratchet`, and
`incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_accepts_offline_new_remote_ratchet` now
replay a late old-chain message after its skipped key has already been consumed,
verify a parsing-error ACK with no typed batch, prove the provider session bytes
remain unchanged, and then continue with the remaining old-chain/new-chain
messages. This narrows the Phase 11 receive/decrypt integration gap around
store-backed one-to-one ratchet replay handling after previous-chain skipped-key
consumption. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider_accepts_new_remote_ratchet --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider_accepts_new_remote_ratchet --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_accepts_new_remote_ratchet --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_accepts_new_remote_ratchet --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_accepts_offline_new_remote_ratchet --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_accepts_offline_new_remote_ratchet --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The explicit offline Signal-provider new-ratchet slice carries the previous-chain
consumed-replay guard through `process_offline_node_with_signal_provider`.
`process_offline_signal_provider_accepts_new_remote_ratchet` now decrypts an
offline first pre-key message, sends a local reply to force a new remote ratchet,
accepts the first new-ratchet message, consumes a late previous-chain skipped
key, rejects a replay of that consumed previous-chain message with a parsing
ACK and unchanged provider session bytes, then continues with the remaining
old-chain and next new-chain messages. This narrows the Phase 11 explicit
offline receive facade gap around store-backed one-to-one ratchet replay
handling after previous-chain skipped-key consumption. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_offline_signal_provider_accepts_new_remote_ratchet --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_offline_signal_provider_accepts_new_remote_ratchet --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The explicit offline placeholder+retry+media Signal-provider new-ratchet slice
carries the consumed previous-chain replay guard through
`process_offline_node_with_placeholder_retry_and_media_retry_with_signal_provider`.
`process_offline_node_with_placeholder_retry_media_and_signal_provider_accepts_new_remote_ratchet`
now drives the combined offline facade through the first pre-key message, local
reply, first new-ratchet message, late previous-chain skipped-key consumption,
consumed previous-chain replay rejection, and valid old/new-chain continuation.
Each step verifies the wrapper returns no placeholder resend, retry resend, or
media retry side-effect work for the pure message path. The replay path also
verifies a parsing-error ACK, no typed event, no outbound side-effect frame,
unchanged provider session bytes, and unchanged retry success statistics. This
narrows the Phase 11 explicit combined offline receive-facade gap around
store-backed one-to-one ratchet replay handling after previous-chain skipped-key
consumption. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_offline_node_with_placeholder_retry_media_and_signal_provider_accepts_new_remote_ratchet --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_offline_node_with_placeholder_retry_media_and_signal_provider_accepts_new_remote_ratchet --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The direct placeholder-resend Signal-provider new-ratchet slice carries the
same consumed previous-chain replay guard through
`process_incoming_node_with_placeholder_resend_with_signal_provider`.
`process_incoming_node_with_placeholder_resend_signal_provider_accepts_new_remote_ratchet`
now decrypts the first pre-key message, drives a local reply and first
new-ratchet message through the placeholder-capable facade, consumes a late
previous-chain skipped key, rejects a replay of that consumed key with a
parsing-error ACK while proving no placeholder resend side effect fires and the
provider session bytes remain unchanged, then continues with the remaining
old-chain and next new-chain messages. This narrows the Phase 11 direct
placeholder-resend receive facade gap around store-backed one-to-one ratchet

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

message, local reply, first new-ratchet message, late previous-chain
skipped-key consumption, consumed previous-chain replay rejection, and valid
old/new-chain continuation. The replay path verifies a parsing-error ACK, no
typed batch, no media-retry event or processed side-effect, no extra outbound
frame, and unchanged provider session bytes. This narrows the Phase 11
automatic media-retry receive-pump gap around store-backed one-to-one ratchet
replay handling after previous-chain skipped-key consumption. It was verified
in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_media_retry_signal_provider_accepts_new_remote_ratchet --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_media_retry_signal_provider_accepts_new_remote_ratchet --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The spawned placeholder+retry Signal-provider new-ratchet slice carries the
consumed previous-chain replay guard through
`spawn_incoming_processor_with_placeholder_and_retry_resend_with_signal_provider`.
`incoming_processor_with_placeholder_retry_signal_provider_accepts_new_remote_ratchet`
now drives the combined automatic placeholder/retry receive pump through the
first pre-key message, local reply, first new-ratchet message, late
previous-chain skipped-key consumption, consumed previous-chain replay
rejection, and valid old/new-chain continuation. The replay path verifies a
parsing-error ACK, no typed batch, no extra placeholder or retry side-effect
frame, unchanged provider session bytes, and unchanged retry success
statistics. This narrows the Phase 11 combined automatic receive-pump gap
around store-backed one-to-one ratchet replay handling after previous-chain
skipped-key consumption. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`

The spawned placeholder+retry+media Signal-provider new-ratchet slice carries
the consumed previous-chain replay guard through
`spawn_incoming_processor_with_placeholder_retry_and_media_retry_with_signal_provider`.
`incoming_processor_with_placeholder_retry_media_and_signal_provider_accepts_new_remote_ratchet`
now drives the fully combined automatic receive pump through the first pre-key
message, local reply, first new-ratchet message, late previous-chain
skipped-key consumption, consumed previous-chain replay rejection, and valid
old/new-chain continuation. The replay path verifies a parsing-error ACK, no
typed batch, no media-retry event or processed side-effect, no extra
placeholder/retry/media outbound frame, unchanged provider session bytes, and
unchanged retry success statistics. This narrows the Phase 11 combined
media-capable automatic receive-pump gap around store-backed one-to-one
ratchet replay handling after previous-chain skipped-key consumption. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`

The deterministic Signal previous-chain consumed-replay fixture slice hardens
the project-owned provider against an old-ratchet replay after one skipped key
has been consumed while later skipped keys from the same previous chain remain.
`decrypt_signal_provider_session_record_message` now rejects that case before
attempting to ratchet back to the old key, returning the deterministic
previous-chain backwards-counter error class instead of surfacing a generic body
decrypt failure. The `provider_session_previous_chain_replay` fixture schema now
accepts `expected_old_replay_error`, and both previous-chain replay vectors opt
into proving the consumed replay rejection while the canonical provider session
record remains unchanged. This narrows the remaining Signal provider parity and
golden-vector gap around previous-chain skipped-key replay handling after
consumption. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures signal_fixtures_match_public_signal_primitives --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core signal_provider_session_record_keeps_previous_chain_skipped_keys_after_ratchet --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core store_signal_provider_consumes_multiple_previous_chain_skipped_keys_after_ratchet --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise signal_provider_session_record_keeps_previous_chain_skipped_keys_after_ratchet --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_signal_provider_consumes_multiple_previous_chain_skipped_keys_after_ratchet --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-core/src/signal.rs crates/wa-testkit/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`
- `sh -c 'git diff --no-index --check -- /dev/null crates/wa-testkit/tests/signal_fixtures.rs > /tmp/signal_fixtures.check; status=$?; test "$status" -eq 1; test ! -s /tmp/signal_fixtures.check'`
- `sh -c 'git diff --no-index --check -- /dev/null tests/fixtures/signal/manifest.json > /tmp/signal_manifest.check; status=$?; test "$status" -eq 1; test ! -s /tmp/signal_manifest.check'`

The deterministic Signal sender-key consumed skipped-key replay fixture slice
makes the group sender-key replay contract explicit in the shared fixture
manifest. `SignalSenderKeyRecordOutOfOrderFixture` now accepts
`expected_replay_error`, and `sender_key_record_out_of_order_skipped_key` opts
into checking the duplicate sender-key iteration rejection after its skipped key
has already been consumed. The verifier now also proves the canonical
sender-key record bytes remain unchanged after that replay attempt. This narrows
the remaining Signal sender-key golden-vector parity gap around skipped group
message-key replay rejection and rollback behavior. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`

- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-testkit/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`
- `sh -c 'git diff --no-index --check -- /dev/null crates/wa-testkit/tests/signal_fixtures.rs > /tmp/signal_fixtures.check; status=$?; test "$status" -eq 1; test ! -s /tmp/signal_fixtures.check'`
- `sh -c 'git diff --no-index --check -- /dev/null tests/fixtures/signal/manifest.json > /tmp/signal_manifest.check; status=$?; test "$status" -eq 1; test ! -s /tmp/signal_manifest.check'`

The deterministic Signal sender-key multi-state consumed replay fixture slice
now proves the direct fixture verifier keeps the canonical sender-key record
bytes unchanged after rejecting a duplicate iteration on the already-consumed
old-state message. This extends the existing multi-state replay error check
with rollback coverage at the shared golden-vector boundary, matching the
store-backed replay mutation regression already covered in `wa-core`. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`

- `git diff --check -- docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The store-backed Signal sender-key bounded-pruning replay slice now proves that
a retained skipped group message key becomes unreplayable after consumption
inside the bounded skipped-key window. After the pruning regression rejects the
already-pruned first message without mutating the stored sender-key record, it
decrypts the retained second message, verifies the skipped-key window advances,
replays that consumed message, checks the duplicate-iteration rejection, and
proves the persisted sender-key record remains byte-for-byte unchanged. This
narrows the remaining group sender-key replay/rollback parity gap around
bounded skipped-key pruning. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core store_sender_key_provider_prunes_oldest_skipped_group_message_keys_from_store --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_sender_key_provider_prunes_oldest_skipped_group_message_keys_from_store --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-core/src/signal.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The store-backed Signal sender-key out-of-order consumed replay slice now
extends the persisted group receive regression to the final retained skipped
message. After decrypting the third message first, consuming the second skipped
message, rejecting the second-message replay, and accepting the fourth current
message, the regression now consumes the remaining first skipped message,
replays it, verifies the duplicate-iteration rejection, and proves the persisted
sender-key record remains byte-for-byte unchanged. This narrows the remaining
group sender-key replay/rollback parity gap for out-of-order receive paths after
all skipped keys have been consumed. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core store_sender_key_provider_decrypts_out_of_order_group_payloads_from_store --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_sender_key_provider_decrypts_out_of_order_group_payloads_from_store --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-core/src/signal.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The store-backed Signal sender-key out-of-order failed-decrypt slice now
extends the same persisted group receive regression before the consumed replay
path. After decrypting the third message first and then message 2, the
regression now rejects a corrupt-signature message 1 and a valid-signature
truncated-body message 1 while proving the persisted sender-key record remains
byte-for-byte unchanged, then continues through the existing message-2 replay,
message-4 current decrypt, remaining message-1 decrypt, and message-1 consumed
replay assertions. This narrows the remaining group sender-key
failed-decrypt/rollback parity gap for out-of-order receive paths before a
skipped sender-key is consumed. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-core store_sender_key_provider_decrypts_out_of_order_group_payloads_from_store --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise store_sender_key_provider_decrypts_out_of_order_group_payloads_from_store --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-core/src/signal.rs crates/wa-testkit/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`
- `sh -c 'git diff --no-index --check -- /dev/null crates/wa-testkit/tests/signal_fixtures.rs > /tmp/signal_fixtures.check; status=$?; test "$status" -eq 1; test ! -s /tmp/signal_fixtures.check'`
- `sh -c 'git diff --no-index --check -- /dev/null tests/fixtures/signal/manifest.json > /tmp/signal_manifest.check; status=$?; test "$status" -eq 1; test ! -s /tmp/signal_manifest.check'`

The direct client Signal sender-key out-of-order consumed replay slice lifts the
same group replay/rollback contract through
`process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider`. After the direct receive facade
decrypts message 2 first and persists message 1 as a skipped sender-key, it now
replays message 2, verifies the parsing-error ACK and duplicate-iteration
provider error, proves no typed event is emitted, and checks the persisted
sender-key record remains unchanged before the later failed-decrypt and valid
message-1 paths consume the remaining skipped key. This narrows the Phase 11

handling. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The spawned Signal sender-key out-of-order consumed replay slice lifts the same

After the automatic receive pump decrypts message 2 first and persists message 1

After the `<offline>`-wrapped pump decrypts message 2 first and persists message

The client Signal sender-key out-of-order invalid-signature rollback slice now
lifts the store-backed corrupt-signature skipped-message invariant through the
direct receive facade, the spawned receive pump, and the spawned offline receive
pump. After each path decrypts message 2 first and persists message 1 as a
skipped sender-key, it now corrupts the encoded message-1 signature, verifies
the parsing-error ACK/NACK behavior, proves no typed message batch is emitted,
and checks the persisted sender-key record remains unchanged before the existing
valid-signature failed-decrypt and valid message-1 paths consume the skipped
key. This narrows the Phase 11 group sender-key receive and receive-pump gap
around invalid-signature rollback before skipped group messages are consumed. It
was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`

- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The direct client Signal sender-key distribution recovery replay slice now
proves recovered group sender-key records reject immediate replays without
rollback. After `process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider` repairs missing,
deleted, and corrupt receiver records from the cached sender-key distribution,
each recovered message is replayed, verifies the duplicate-iteration
parsing-error ACK, emits no typed event, and leaves the repaired sender-key
record byte-for-byte unchanged. This narrows the Phase 11 direct group
sender-key distribution recovery gap around replay handling after recovery. It
was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The spawned Signal sender-key distribution recovery replay slice carries the
same recovered-record replay guard through
`spawn_incoming_processor_with_signal_provider`. After the automatic live pump
repairs missing, deleted, and corrupt receiver records from the cached

The client Signal sender-key distribution-recovery invalid-signature rollback

the cached fresh sender-key distribution, the same fresh group message is
replayed through the offline wrapper, verifies the parsing-error ACK, emits no
typed batch, leaves the repaired two-state sender-key record unchanged, and
preserves the cached fresh distribution bytes. This narrows the Phase 11
automatic offline group sender-key stale-distribution recovery gap around replay
handling after recovery. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_recovers_offline_stale_sender_key_record_from_distribution --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_signal_provider_recovers_offline_stale_sender_key_record_from_distribution --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The client Signal sender-key stale-distribution recovery invalid-signature
rollback slice now extends the repaired stale-record path across the direct
receive facade, spawned receive pump, and spawned offline receive pump. After
each path recovers the fresh two-state sender-key record from the cached
distribution and decrypts the group message, it now corrupts that recovered
message's encoded signature, verifies parsing-error ACK/NACK behavior, proves
no typed event or batch is emitted, and checks both the repaired sender-key
record and cached fresh distribution bytes remain unchanged before the existing
immediate replay assertions. This narrows the Phase 11 stale-distribution group
sender-key recovery gap around invalid-signature rollback after stale-record
repair. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise recovers_stale_sender_key_record_from_distribution --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise recovers_stale_sender_key_record_from_distribution --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise recovers_offline_stale_sender_key_record_from_distribution --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise recovers_offline_stale_sender_key_record_from_distribution --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The direct client Signal sender-key distribution-message stale-cache slice now
keeps the cached recovery distribution monotonic for same key/signing-key
updates. `StoreSignalRepository::store_sender_key_distribution` no longer
overwrites a cached sender-key distribution with a lower-iteration same-signer
distribution. The direct inbound distribution-message regression now processes
the initial regular plus fast-ratchet distributions, advances the fast state
with a higher-iteration distribution, then processes the original stale fast
distribution and proves both the provider sender-key record and cached
distribution bytes remain at the advanced state. This narrows the Phase 11
direct group sender-key distribution-message gap around stale inbound
distribution rollback. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider_canonicalizes_legacy_sender_key_distribution_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_signal_provider_canonicalizes_legacy_sender_key_distribution_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core provider_state_store_preserves_same_key_sender_key_distribution_after_stale --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise provider_state_store_preserves_same_key_sender_key_distribution_after_stale --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-core/src/signal.rs crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The spawned Signal sender-key distribution-message stale-cache slice carries
the same monotonic cached-distribution guard through
`spawn_incoming_processor_with_signal_provider`. After the live spawned pump
processes the initial regular plus fast-ratchet sender-key distributions, it
advances the fast sender-key state with a higher-iteration distribution, then
processes the original stale fast distribution and proves both the provider
sender-key record and cached distribution bytes remain at the advanced state.
This narrows the Phase 11 spawned live group sender-key distribution-message
gap around stale inbound distribution rollback. It was verified in the current
VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The spawned offline Signal sender-key distribution-message stale-cache slice
now carries the same monotonic cached-distribution guard through offline
`spawn_incoming_processor_with_signal_provider` frames. After the spawned pump
processes the initial offline regular plus fast-ratchet sender-key
distributions, it advances the fast sender-key state with a higher-iteration
offline distribution, then processes the original stale offline fast
distribution and proves both the provider sender-key record and cached
distribution bytes remain at the advanced state. This narrows the Phase 11
spawned offline group sender-key distribution-message gap around stale inbound
distribution rollback. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`

The client Signal sender-key distribution-message malformed-distribution
rollback slice now extends the direct, spawned live, and spawned offline
canonicalization regressions after the higher-iteration fast-ratchet
distribution has advanced the provider state. Each path now processes a
plaintext message carrying malformed fast-ratchet sender-key distribution bytes,
verifies parsing-error ACK/NACK behavior, proves no typed event or batch is
emitted, and checks both PN/LID cached distribution aliases plus the advanced
provider sender-key record remain unchanged before the existing stale
same-signer distribution is accepted as a message without rolling back state.
This narrows the Phase 11 group sender-key distribution-message gap around
malformed attached distribution rollback after monotonic cache advancement. It
was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise canonicalizes_legacy_sender_key_distribution_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise canonicalizes_legacy_sender_key_distribution_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise canonicalizes_offline_legacy_sender_key_distribution_message --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise canonicalizes_offline_legacy_sender_key_distribution_message --quiet`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The Signal sender-key cached-distribution compatibility slice now factors the
same-signer stale-cache replacement rule into
`should_replace_cached_signal_sender_key_distribution` and routes
`StoreSignalRepository::store_sender_key_distribution` through that shared
helper. The signal fixture manifest adds
`sender_key_distribution_cache_preserves_newer_same_signer_bytes`, and the
`wa-testkit` verifier now pins that a lower-iteration same-key/same-signer
incoming distribution does not replace the newer cached distribution bytes
while an empty cache still stores the incoming bytes. This narrows the remaining
Signal provider integration and fixture gap around cache-level sender-key
distribution rollback semantics that external provider adapters must preserve.
It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures signal_fixtures_match_public_signal_primitives --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core provider_state_store_preserves_same_key_sender_key_distribution_after_stale --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise provider_state_store_preserves_same_key_sender_key_distribution_after_stale --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-testkit --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-core/src/signal.rs crates/wa-core/src/lib.rs crates/wa-testkit/src/lib.rs crates/wa-testkit/tests/signal_fixtures.rs tests/fixtures/signal/manifest.json docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The Signal sender-key cached-distribution different-signer replacement fixture
slice now extends the same deterministic cache vector beyond stale same-signer
preservation. The manifest pins a lower-iteration replacement distribution with
the same key id but a different signing key, and the `wa-testkit` verifier now
proves the retained newer same-signer cache is replaced by that different-signer
candidate while still preserving the no-existing-cache storage branch. This
narrows the remaining Signal provider integration and fixture gap around
cache-level sender-key distribution replacement semantics that external
provider adapters must preserve. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures signal_fixtures_match_public_signal_primitives --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-testkit --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-testkit/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`
- `sh -c 'git diff --no-index --check -- /dev/null crates/wa-testkit/tests/signal_fixtures.rs > /tmp/signal_fixtures.check; status=$?; test "$status" -eq 1; test ! -s /tmp/signal_fixtures.check'`
- `sh -c 'git diff --no-index --check -- /dev/null tests/fixtures/signal/manifest.json > /tmp/signal_manifest.check; status=$?; test "$status" -eq 1; test ! -s /tmp/signal_manifest.check'`

The Signal sender-key cached-distribution malformed-incoming preservation slice
now pins the cache helper's error branch after a valid cached distribution
already exists. `StoreSignalRepository` now rejects malformed incoming
sender-key distribution bytes after a successful store and proves the existing
cached bytes remain unchanged. The deterministic cache fixture also carries
malformed incoming bytes plus the expected error substring and verifies the
different-signer replacement cache remains intact after that rejection. This
narrows the remaining Signal provider integration and fixture gap around
cache-level sender-key distribution error preservation. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-core/src/signal.rs crates/wa-testkit/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

- `sh -c 'git diff --no-index --check -- /dev/null tests/fixtures/signal/manifest.json > /tmp/signal_manifest.check; status=$?; test "$status" -eq 1; test ! -s /tmp/signal_manifest.check'`

The Signal sender-key cached-distribution malformed-existing replacement slice
now pins the cache helper's recovery branch when stored cache bytes are already
corrupt but the incoming distribution is valid. `StoreSignalRepository` now
seeds a corrupt sender-key distribution cache row and proves a valid write
replaces it. The deterministic cache fixture also carries malformed existing
bytes and verifies the helper chooses the valid replacement distribution as the
post-cache bytes. This narrows the remaining Signal provider integration and
fixture gap around cache-level sender-key distribution recovery from corrupt
persisted state. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`

- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-testkit --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures -- -D warnings`

- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-core/src/signal.rs crates/wa-testkit/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

- `sh -c 'git diff --no-index --check -- /dev/null tests/fixtures/signal/manifest.json > /tmp/signal_manifest.check; status=$?; test "$status" -eq 1; test ! -s /tmp/signal_manifest.check'`

The Signal sender-key cached-distribution equal-iteration same-signer
replacement slice now pins the cache helper's non-stale branch when key id,
iteration, and signer are unchanged but the incoming chain key bytes differ.
`StoreSignalRepository` stores an equal-iteration same-signer replacement and
proves the cached distribution bytes advance to the replacement. The
deterministic cache fixture now carries equal-iteration incoming bytes and
verifies that replacement before the existing different-signer branch runs.
This narrows the remaining Signal provider integration and fixture gap around
cache-level sender-key distribution replacement semantics that are not covered
by the newer-same-signer preservation path. It was verified in the current VM
with:

- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`

- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-core/src/signal.rs crates/wa-testkit/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`
- `sh -c 'git diff --no-index --check -- /dev/null crates/wa-testkit/tests/signal_fixtures.rs > /tmp/signal_fixtures.check; status=$?; test "$status" -eq 1; test ! -s /tmp/signal_fixtures.check'`
- `sh -c 'git diff --no-index --check -- /dev/null tests/fixtures/signal/manifest.json > /tmp/signal_manifest.check; status=$?; test "$status" -eq 1; test ! -s /tmp/signal_manifest.check'`

The deterministic Signal provider-session current-chain tamper fixture now also
pins the canonical receiver session bytes before the rejection path runs.
`SignalProviderSessionTamperRejectFixture` accepts
`receiver_record_before_reject_hex`, and the `wa-testkit` verifier checks the
generated pre-rejection record before corrupting the current-chain ciphertext,
then still proves the tampered message rejects and the untouched valid message
advances to the existing canonical post-decrypt bytes. This narrows the
remaining Signal provider compatibility-fixture gap around current-chain
rollback semantics that external provider adapters must preserve. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures signal_fixtures_match_public_signal_primitives --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures --quiet`
