- `sh -c 'git diff --no-index --check -- /dev/null crates/wa-testkit/tests/signal_fixtures.rs > /tmp/signal_fixtures.check; status=$?; test "$status" -eq 1; test ! -s /tmp/signal_fixtures.check'`
- `sh -c 'git diff --no-index --check -- /dev/null tests/fixtures/signal/manifest.json > /tmp/signal_manifest.check; status=$?; test "$status" -eq 1; test ! -s /tmp/signal_manifest.check'`

The deterministic Signal provider-session new-ratchet and future active-chain
tamper fixtures now also pin the canonical receiver session bytes before their
rejection paths run. `SignalProviderSessionNewRatchetTamperRejectFixture` and
`SignalProviderSessionFutureTamperRejectFixture` accept
`receiver_record_before_reject_hex`, and the `wa-testkit` verifier checks the
generated pre-rejection records before corrupting the new-ratchet ciphertext or
future-counter ciphertext. The existing assertions still prove the tampered
messages reject and the untouched valid messages advance through the canonical
post-decrypt record bytes. This narrows the remaining Signal provider
compatibility-fixture gap around rollback semantics that external provider
adapters must preserve before and after a ratchet step. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `sh -c 'git diff --no-index --check -- /dev/null tests/fixtures/signal/manifest.json > /tmp/signal_manifest.check; status=$?; test "$status" -eq 1; test ! -s /tmp/signal_manifest.check'`

The deterministic Signal provider-session far-future counter fixtures now also
pin the canonical receiver session bytes before their rejection paths run.
`SignalProviderSessionFarFutureCounterFixture` and
`SignalProviderSessionFarFuturePreviousCounterFixture` accept
`receiver_record_before_reject_hex`, and the `wa-testkit` verifier checks the
generated pre-rejection records before rejecting an active-chain far-future
counter or a new-ratchet far-future previous-chain counter. The existing
assertions still prove the valid message decrypts from the same original
receiver bytes and advances to the canonical post-decrypt records. This narrows
the remaining Signal provider compatibility-fixture gap around counter-bound
rollback semantics that external provider adapters must preserve. It was

The deterministic Signal provider-session stale previous-counter fixture now
also pins the canonical receiver session bytes before the rejection path runs.
`SignalProviderSessionStalePreviousCounterFixture` accepts
`receiver_record_before_reject_hex`, and the `wa-testkit` verifier checks the
generated pre-rejection record before rejecting a new-ratchet message whose
previous-chain counter moved backwards. The existing assertions still prove the

- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-testkit/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`
- `sh -c 'git diff --no-index --check -- /dev/null crates/wa-testkit/tests/signal_fixtures.rs > /tmp/signal_fixtures.check; status=$?; test "$status" -eq 1; test ! -s /tmp/signal_fixtures.check'`
- `sh -c 'git diff --no-index --check -- /dev/null tests/fixtures/signal/manifest.json > /tmp/signal_manifest.check; status=$?; test "$status" -eq 1; test ! -s /tmp/signal_manifest.check'`

The deterministic Signal sender-key multi-state decrypt fixture now also pins
the canonical receiver sender-key record bytes before its rejection paths run.
`SignalSenderKeyRecordMultiStateDecryptFixture` accepts
`receiver_record_before_reject_hex`, and the `wa-testkit` verifier checks the
generated two-state pre-rejection record before rejecting an invalid-signature
old-state group message, a valid-signature failed-decrypt old-state group
message, and a far-future old-state group-message iteration. The existing
assertions still prove the untouched old-state message decrypts from the same
original receiver bytes, rejects its replay without mutation, and then decrypts
the preserved replacement state. This narrows the remaining group sender-key
compatibility-fixture gap around multi-state rollback semantics that external
provider adapters must preserve. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures signal_fixtures_match_public_signal_primitives --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-testkit/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`
- `sh -c 'git diff --no-index --check -- /dev/null crates/wa-testkit/tests/signal_fixtures.rs > /tmp/signal_fixtures.check; status=$?; test "$status" -eq 1; test ! -s /tmp/signal_fixtures.check'`
- `sh -c 'git diff --no-index --check -- /dev/null tests/fixtures/signal/manifest.json > /tmp/signal_manifest.check; status=$?; test "$status" -eq 1; test ! -s /tmp/signal_manifest.check'`

The direct Signal-provider own PN/LID linked-device dedupe slice now covers
`send_text_with_signal_provider` when the local account has both a PN JID and
LID JID. The new regression drives a direct send whose USync device lookup
returns the remote audience plus duplicate local linked devices through both
the own PN and own LID rows. It proves the session query and final participant
list keep only the canonical linked PN device, decrypts the remote payload as a
plain direct text message, decrypts the local linked-device payload as a
`device_sent` wrapper whose destination stays on the remote chat JID, and
leaves no duplicate own-LID provider session record. This narrows the remaining
direct Signal-provider send edge-case gap around own PN/LID linked-device
aliasing. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise send_text_with_signal_provider_deduplicates_own_pn_lid_linked_device_aliases --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise send_text_with_signal_provider_deduplicates_own_pn_lid_linked_device_aliases --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The direct retry-resend Signal-provider own PN/LID linked-device dedupe slice
now covers all-device retry replay through
`execute_retry_resends_with_signal_provider`. The new regression prepares a
cached direct retry job from an account that has both `999:7@s.whatsapp.net`
and `ownlid@lid`, receives USync device rows that advertise the same own linked
device through both identities, asserts the retry session query and final
participant list collapse to `123@s.whatsapp.net` plus the canonical
`999:8@s.whatsapp.net` own linked device, decrypts the remote `pkmsg` as the
cached direct text, decrypts the own-linked `pkmsg` as a `device_sent` wrapper
whose destination remains the remote chat JID, proves no duplicate own-LID
provider session record is stored, and verifies the retry cache is finalized
after success. This narrows the remaining direct Signal-provider retry resend
edge-case gap around own PN/LID linked-device aliasing. It was verified in the
current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise execute_retry_resends_with_signal_provider_deduplicates_own_pn_lid_all_devices --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise execute_retry_resends_with_signal_provider_deduplicates_own_pn_lid_all_devices --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The direct incoming retry-resend Signal-provider own PN/LID linked-device
dedupe slice now carries the same all-device retry replay guard through
`process_incoming_node_with_retry_resend_with_signal_provider`. The new
regression processes an incoming retry receipt from the remote primary JID,
verifies the ACK, forces the requester identity-refresh session query, then
receives USync rows that advertise the same own linked device through both
`999:7@s.whatsapp.net` and `ownlid@lid`. It proves the replay only fetches the
missing canonical own linked-device session, collapses final participants to
`123@s.whatsapp.net` and `999:8@s.whatsapp.net`, decrypts the remote and
own-linked `pkmsg` payloads, rejects a duplicate own-LID provider session
record, and finalizes retry-cache accounting after success. This narrows the
remaining direct Signal-provider incoming retry resend edge-case gap around own
PN/LID linked-device aliasing. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_retry_resend_signal_provider_deduplicates_own_pn_lid_all_devices --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_retry_resend_signal_provider_deduplicates_own_pn_lid_all_devices --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The spawned retry-resend Signal-provider own PN/LID linked-device dedupe slice
now carries the same all-device retry replay guard through
`spawn_incoming_processor_with_retry_resend_with_signal_provider`. The new
automatic-pump regression sends a raw retry receipt frame from the remote
primary JID, verifies the emitted ACK, answers the forced requester
identity-refresh query, answers a USync query that advertises the same own
linked device under both the local PN and LID identities, and proves the
replay only refreshes the missing canonical linked-device session. It then
verifies the final relay participants collapse to `123@s.whatsapp.net` and
`999:8@s.whatsapp.net`, decrypts the remote and own-linked `pkmsg` payloads,
rejects a duplicate own-LID provider session record, and confirms retry-cache
finalization after the spawned pump succeeds. This narrows the remaining
automatic direct Signal-provider retry resend edge-case gap around own PN/LID
linked-device aliasing. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_retry_resend_signal_provider_deduplicates_own_pn_lid_all_devices --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise incoming_processor_with_retry_resend_signal_provider_deduplicates_own_pn_lid_all_devices --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The direct combined placeholder/retry/media Signal-provider own PN/LID
linked-device dedupe slice now carries the all-device retry replay guard
through
`process_incoming_node_with_placeholder_retry_and_media_retry_with_signal_provider`.
The new regression processes a retry receipt through the combined facade,
verifies the ACK, proves no placeholder resend or media-retry side effect is
emitted, answers the forced requester identity-refresh query, and then answers
USync rows that expose the same own linked device through both the local PN and
LID identities. It verifies the retry replay refreshes only the missing
canonical own linked-device session, collapses final relay participants to
`123@s.whatsapp.net` and `999:8@s.whatsapp.net`, decrypts the remote and
own-linked `pkmsg` payloads, leaves no duplicate own-LID provider session
record, and finalizes retry-cache accounting after success. This narrows the
remaining combined direct Signal-provider retry resend edge-case gap around own
PN/LID linked-device aliasing. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_placeholder_retry_media_and_signal_provider_deduplicates_own_pn_lid_all_devices --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_incoming_node_with_placeholder_retry_media_and_signal_provider_deduplicates_own_pn_lid_all_devices --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The offline combined placeholder/retry/media Signal-provider own PN/LID
linked-device dedupe slice now carries the all-device retry replay guard
through
`process_offline_node_with_placeholder_retry_and_media_retry_with_signal_provider`.
The new regression processes an `<offline>`-wrapped retry receipt through the
combined facade, verifies the child ACK and offline event accounting, proves no
placeholder resend or media-retry side effect is emitted, answers the forced
requester identity-refresh query, and then answers USync rows that expose the
same own linked device through both the local PN and LID identities. It verifies
the offline retry replay refreshes only the missing canonical own linked-device
session, collapses final relay participants to `123@s.whatsapp.net` and
`999:8@s.whatsapp.net`, decrypts the remote and own-linked `pkmsg` payloads,
leaves no duplicate own-LID provider session record, and finalizes retry-cache
accounting after success. This narrows the remaining combined offline
Signal-provider retry resend edge-case gap around own PN/LID linked-device
aliasing. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --features memory-store,noise process_offline_node_with_placeholder_retry_media_and_signal_provider_deduplicates_own_pn_lid_all_devices --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-client --no-default-features --features memory-store,noise process_offline_node_with_placeholder_retry_media_and_signal_provider_deduplicates_own_pn_lid_all_devices --quiet`

- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-client/src/lib.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

refreshes the missing canonical linked-device session. It verifies final relay

decrypts the remote and own-linked `pkmsg` payloads, leaves no duplicate
own-LID provider session record, and finalizes retry-cache accounting after the
placeholder-capable pump succeeds. This narrows the remaining non-media

The deterministic Signal sender-key out-of-order skipped-key fixture now also
pins the canonical receiver sender-key record bytes before its skipped-message
rejection paths run. `SignalSenderKeyRecordOutOfOrderFixture` accepts
`receiver_record_before_reject_hex`, and the `wa-testkit` verifier checks the
generated post-second-decrypt record before rejecting the tampered skipped
message, the invalid-signature skipped message, and the consumed skipped-key
replay. The existing assertions still prove the untouched first skipped message
decrypts from the same pre-rejection record and advances to the canonical
post-consumption bytes. This narrows the remaining group sender-key
compatibility-fixture gap around out-of-order skipped-message rollback
semantics that external provider adapters must preserve. It was verified in
the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures signal_fixtures_match_public_signal_primitives --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-testkit --tests -- -D warnings`

- `cargo fmt --all --check`

- `sh -c 'git diff --no-index --check -- /dev/null crates/wa-testkit/tests/signal_fixtures.rs > /tmp/signal_fixtures.check; status=$?; test "$status" -eq 1; test ! -s /tmp/signal_fixtures.check'`
- `sh -c 'git diff --no-index --check -- /dev/null tests/fixtures/signal/manifest.json > /tmp/signal_manifest.check; status=$?; test "$status" -eq 1; test ! -s /tmp/signal_manifest.check'`

The deterministic Signal provider-session out-of-order skipped-key fixture now
also pins the canonical receiver session bytes before its skipped-message
rejection paths run. `SignalProviderSessionOutOfOrderFixture` accepts
`receiver_record_before_reject_hex`, and the `wa-testkit` verifier checks the
generated post-second-decrypt record before rejecting the tampered skipped
message and the consumed skipped-key replay. The existing assertions still
prove the untouched first skipped message decrypts from the same pre-rejection
record and that the following current-chain message advances to the canonical
post-third bytes. This narrows the remaining Signal provider
compatibility-fixture gap around out-of-order skipped-message rollback
semantics that external provider adapters must preserve. It was verified in
the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures signal_fixtures_match_public_signal_primitives --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures --quiet`

The deterministic Signal provider-session replay fixtures now pin the receiver
session bytes before their replay rejection paths run. The current-chain
`SignalProviderSessionReplayRejectFixture` now carries the exact replay error
string plus `receiver_record_before_reject_hex`, and
`SignalProviderSessionNewRatchetReplayFixture` also carries
`receiver_record_before_reject_hex`. The `wa-testkit` verifier checks those
generated post-first-decrypt records before rejecting duplicate current-chain
and new-ratchet messages, then still proves the following valid messages
advance through the canonical post-decrypt records. This narrows the remaining
Signal provider compatibility-fixture gap around replay rollback semantics
that external provider adapters must preserve. It was verified in the current
VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures signal_fixtures_match_public_signal_primitives --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures --quiet`

The deterministic Signal provider-session previous-chain replay fixtures now
pin the receiver session bytes before both old-chain rejection boundaries.
`SignalProviderSessionPreviousChainReplayFixture` accepts
`receiver_record_before_old_reject_hex` for the post-new-ratchet record used to
reject tampered previous-chain messages, and
`receiver_record_before_old_replay_hex` for the post-old-chain record used to
reject consumed previous-chain replays. The `wa-testkit` verifier checks those
generated records before the rejection branches and still proves the valid
old-chain and continuation messages advance through their canonical records.
This narrows the remaining Signal provider compatibility-fixture gap around
previous-chain skipped-key rollback semantics that external provider adapters
must preserve. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-testkit --test signal_fixtures signal_fixtures_match_public_signal_primitives --quiet`

The deterministic Signal pre-key invalid-session fixture slice now treats the
manifest error strings as exact surfaced errors instead of substrings for the
invalid signed-pre-key, invalid one-time pre-key, and pre-key id/state mismatch
vectors. The `wa-testkit` verifier now uses exact equality for those branches,
and the manifest carries the full `protocol error: ...` strings where the
public error display includes that prefix. This narrows the remaining Signal
compatibility-fixture gap around pre-key session rejection diagnostics that
external provider adapters must preserve. It was verified in the current VM
with:

The deterministic Signal pre-key whisper invalid-message fixture slice now
treats malformed pre-key whisper diagnostics as exact surfaced errors instead
of substrings. The `wa-testkit` verifier now uses exact equality for invalid
base-key, invalid identity-key, and invalid pre-key whisper wire vectors, and
the manifest carries the full `protocol error: ...` strings for those cases.
This narrows the remaining Signal compatibility-fixture gap around malformed

treats provider-session tamper, active-chain far-future, previous-chain

sender-key far-future rejection checks as exact surfaced errors instead of
substrings. The only remaining `contains` use in the Signal fixture verifier is

The stateful Signal fuzz target structured diagnostic slice now treats
provider-session stale previous-counter, far-future, replay, sender-key
invalid-signature, failed-decrypt, far-future, replay, and record-invariant
diagnostics as exact surfaced errors instead of broad substrings. The fuzz
oracle now computes the expected dynamic counter values and compares the full
public `CoreError::to_string()` output, so fuzz regressions catch public error
surface drift in the same areas pinned by deterministic fixtures. It was
verified in the current VM with:

The `wa-core` sender-key diagnostic unit-test slice now treats sender-key
far-future iteration, malformed sender-key distribution, and invalid
sender-key record diagnostics as exact surfaced errors instead of broad

`CoreError` messages as the deterministic fixture and fuzz oracles for these
sender-key branches, including the concrete `25001`, `29`, and `35` counter and
length values. It was verified in the current VM with:

The `wa-core` provider-session and E2E diagnostic unit-test slice now treats
E2E session-query errors, provider-session validation malformed-record and
malformed-identity reasons, provider identity-change rejection, runtime
malformed provider-session decrypt rejection, and provider-session far-future
counter diagnostics as exact surfaced errors instead of broad substrings. The
regular Signal unit-test surface now leaves only membership checks using
`contains`, so deterministic public diagnostic assertions align with the Signal
fixture verifier and fuzz targets. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core signal --quiet`

The `wa-core` generated media-thumbnail extractor slice now bounds ffmpeg video
frame extraction by both configured thumbnail width and height before the
second-stage JPEG pass, reducing oversized temporary frames for e2e-sized video
inputs while preserving the final aspect-fit encoder validation. The local
thumbnail tests now assert the exact ffmpeg and pdftoppm argument contracts,
including temporary output naming, and pin thumbnail input validation errors as
exact public `CoreError` strings instead of broad substrings. The generated
remote-thumbnail upload path was rechecked in both default and no-default
feature builds so the extractor output still uploads with the parent media key
and attaches returned dimensions. It was verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core --features image thumbnail --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features noise,image uploads_generated_remote_media_thumbnails_from_extractors --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise,image uploads_generated_remote_media_thumbnails_from_extractors --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features image --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise,image --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-core/src/thumbnail.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`
- `sh -c 'git diff --no-index --check -- /dev/null crates/wa-core/src/thumbnail.rs > /tmp/thumbnail.check; status=$?; test "$status" -eq 1; test ! -s /tmp/thumbnail.check'`

The `wa-core` fetched/generated link-preview thumbnail slice now covers the
cached runtime path that fetches link metadata, fetches the preview image,
generates inline plus high-quality thumbnails, uploads the high-quality
thumbnail as `ThumbnailLink`, and attaches both thumbnail forms to the returned
message content. The new cache regression fetches the same preview and image
twice while asserting only one high-quality thumbnail upload reaches the media
transport, and the oversized image rejection now pins the exact public
`CoreError` string while proving no thumbnail upload was attempted. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core --features link-preview,image fetch_link_preview_with_thumbnail_cached_reuses_high_quality_upload --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features link-preview,image fetch_link_preview_with_thumbnail_rejects_oversized_image --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --features link-preview,image uploads_generated_link_preview_thumbnail_images --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features link-preview,image fetch_link_preview_with_thumbnail_cached_reuses_high_quality_upload --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features link-preview,image fetch_link_preview_with_thumbnail_rejects_oversized_image --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --features link-preview,image --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features link-preview,image --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-core/src/media.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The `wa-core` media retry error-handling slice now pins retry application
failure diagnostics as exact public `CoreError` strings, including explicit
retry error status and encrypted-notification stanza-id mismatch cases. The
download-after-retry guard now has a regression test proving non-success retry
results return the exact `NotFound` diagnostic before any media download is
attempted, and batch retry processing now asserts the exact propagated error
reason while retaining the pending descriptor for later retries. It was
verified in the current VM with:

- `cargo test -p wa-core rejects_invalid_media_retry_applications --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core download_after_retry_rejects_non_success_without_download --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core media_retry_coordinator_collects_batch_errors --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core media_retry --quiet`
- `cargo test -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise media_retry --quiet`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo clippy -p wa-core --no-default-features --features noise --tests -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --all --manifest-path Cargo.toml --check`
- `git diff --check -- crates/wa-core/src/media.rs docs/baileys_rust_rewrite_plan.md`

The current VM cannot run `node --check
tools/compat/generate-binary-node-fixtures.mjs` because Node.js is not
installed.

The current VM cannot run `wa-core --all-features` without installing
`pkg-config`/OpenSSL development discovery because that feature set enables the
optional `native-tls` backend.

On the VM, run the same gates first after copying the workspace. If a command
fails because dependencies are not yet downloaded, install the Rust toolchain
from `rust-toolchain.toml` and allow Cargo to fetch dependencies.

### Recommended Next Implementation Order

1. Resume from the remaining-gap list in Section 13, not from the earliest phase
   header.
2. PROVE the project-owned Signal provider behind the existing
   `SignalCryptoProvider`/`SignalRepository` boundary. This is the highest-risk
   and highest-value remaining task. Adopt-a-crate was evaluated and REJECTED
   (2026-06-22, see Settled Decisions): no permissive maintained crate covers
   sender-key groups plus durable serialization; `signalapp/libsignal` is the
   only complete option but is AGPL-3.0-only and would still need wire-framing
   patches. Concretely: (a) keep green coverage of all three `SignalCryptoProvider`
   methods and round-trip coverage of the three custom record encodings;
   (b) add cross-implementation conformance vectors under `tools/compat`
   (web-derived from the public Baileys/libsignal-node encoder) that lock the
   non-standard `msg`/`pkmsg`/`skmsg` wire framing; (c) harden out-of-order
   skipped-key handling, X3DH bootstrap, and sender-key signature verification
   via the existing `fuzz` targets.
3. Broaden the newly wired store-backed Signal direct send/receive paths with
   one-to-one ratchet edge cases and compatibility fixtures, then harden group
   sender-key and status/broadcast send edge cases.
4. Continue media parity with live/e2e media retry validation,
   live/e2e validation for fetched and generated link-preview thumbnails,
   broader video/document thumbnail extraction/upload coverage, and
   performance/memory profiling over e2e-sized fixtures.
5. Add compatibility fixture generation from the local reference, then broaden
   golden vectors, property tests, and fuzz targets.
6. Finish receive/sync-state integration around the real decryptor, app-state
   retry/resync edge cases, live history event pumping, and broader notification
   edge cases.
7. Broaden group/newsletter/business/community edge-case coverage and ignored
   live e2e validation.
8. Broaden public examples, API docs, transition guide, support matrix, and
   ignored live e2e coverage as APIs stabilize, then complete final
   hardening/performance review.

### Progress And Time Estimate

Risk-adjusted overall rewrite progress is approximately 50 percent complete.
The foundation surface is broader than that number suggests, but the unfinished
parts include the highest-risk work: real Signal cryptographic compatibility,
full live send/receive behavior, live media retry/thumbnail parity,
compatibility fixture coverage, and live e2e validation.

Estimated remaining effort for one senior Rust engineer already familiar with
this codebase:

- Serious mock-tested beta: 8 to 12 full-time engineering weeks.
- Production-quality parity target with live e2e harnesses, broader fixtures,
  fuzzing, docs, and release hardening: 12 to 20 full-time engineering weeks.
- Add 3 to 6 weeks if the preferred Signal provider cannot be adapted cleanly
  and a project-owned compatibility adapter must be completed instead.

These estimates assume continued single-go implementation with focused
test-fix-retest loops and no major upstream protocol breakage during the move.

## 14. Settled Decisions And Open-Decision Status

Decided:

- Use an immediate multi-crate Cargo workspace. Do not start as a single crate.
- Use neutral crate, module, file, package, and public API names. Do not inherit
  upstream package naming.
- Keep the project-owned `SignalRepository`/`SignalCryptoProvider` boundary and
  use the project-owned crypto/session adapter as the durable provider. Decided
  2026-06-22 after an adversarially-verified crate spike: no maintained
  permissive Rust Signal crate covers sender-key groups plus durable record
  serialization (the only complete, maintained, pure-Rust stack is
  `signalapp/libsignal`, which is AGPL-3.0-only and therefore rejected by the
  permissive-license requirement), and even adopting it would require patching
  its wire framing to this project's non-standard `msg`/`pkmsg` format
  (no leading version byte; 8-byte truncated HMAC embedded inside the protobuf
  ciphertext field). The remaining Phase 9 work is to PROVE the project-owned
  provider with cross-implementation conformance vectors and fuzz hardening, not
  to adopt an external crate. Revisit only if the user later accepts AGPL-3.0
  network copyleft for the whole product.
- CONFIRMED 2026-06-22 via an authoritative oracle (the `libsignal` npm package
  Baileys/WhatsApp use; tooling under `tools/compat/signal_*` + vectors under
  `tests/fixtures/signal_conformance.json`): the project-owned 1:1 WhisperMessage
  wire format is currently INCOMPATIBLE with WhatsApp. libsignal emits
  `versionByte(0x33) || protobuf{ratchetKey,counter,prevCounter,ciphertext=pureAES}
  || MAC8` with MAC = HMAC-SHA256(macKey, senderIdPub(33) ‖ receiverIdPub(33) ‖
  versionByte ‖ protobuf)[..8]; the repo emits pure protobuf with no version byte
  and the 8-byte MAC embedded inside the ciphertext field, computed over the AES
  ciphertext only. The KDF/ratchet labels (WhisperRatchet/WhisperMessageKeys/
  WhisperText/WhisperGroup, HMAC 0x01/0x02 stepping) are already correct, so the
  fix is concentrated in 1:1 message (de)serialization + MAC: add the version
  byte, move the MAC outside the protobuf, make the ciphertext field pure AES, and
  compute the MAC over identities‖version‖protobuf (threading sender/receiver
  identity public keys through encrypt/decrypt). Every Signal primitive must be
  re-validated against the oracle since none was checked before. This is the core
  of proving the provider and the project's dominant remaining risk.
- Use SQLite as the default native, versioned, transaction-safe runtime store.
- Check in generated Rust protobuf code and provide an explicit regeneration
  script plus drift-check tooling.
- Do not implement WAM telemetry in the main rewrite.
- Use Rust 1.96 as the project toolchain/MSRV.
- Keep live e2e tests in this repository.
- Treat current feature flags as manifest-defined. Planned flags such as
  `media`, `serde`, and `mock` remain planned until crate manifests add them.

Storage format decision:

- The primary store is what the Rust client reads and writes during normal use.
  It should be SQLite by default and designed for Rust safety and reliability:
  explicit schema versions, migrations, transactions, redacted debug output, and
  zeroized secret fields.
- The rewrite will not implement import/export as part of the main `/goal`.
- Users should pair/login fresh into the native Rust store.
- Do not make legacy or foreign session formats part of the runtime path.
- Import/export may be considered later as a separate optional tool if a real
  migration or interoperability requirement appears.
- If import/export is added later, it must be explicit conversion tooling with
  careful validation and secret-handling warnings, not something the client
  depends on during normal operation.

Open decisions:

- None at the top-level as of this revision. New implementation findings should
  be added here only when they require a real project-level decision.

## 15. Single-Go Goal Operating Mode

Use exactly one `/goal` for the complete rewrite. If none exists when
implementation resumes, create it. If that goal has already started, continue it.
Section 13 is the source of truth for landed foundations and known remaining
gaps. Do not recreate completed crates or capabilities; verify them when touched
and continue from the next incomplete capability named in the remaining-gap list,
parity checklist, or phase acceptance criteria.

Operating checklist:

1. Keep continuing the existing single-go implementation objective instead of
   opening separate phase goals.
2. Treat completed foundations as reusable implementation substrate, not as
   whole-phase completion unless all phase deliverables and validation are done.
3. Keep focused test-fix-retest loops active for every touched capability.
4. Continue adding compatibility fixtures, property tests, and fuzz targets; the
   focused unit tests already present do not satisfy those validation
   requirements by themselves.
5. Prefer mock protocol coverage until live e2e tests are explicitly configured;
   live e2e tests still belong in this repository and remain ignored by default.
6. Continue directly into the next incomplete capability rather than stopping at
   any foundation checkpoint.

## 16. Definition Of Done For The Rewrite

The rewrite is complete when:

- The public Rust client can perform QR login, pairing code login, session
  restore, Signal-backed send/receive messaging, media transfer, USync, group
  operations, app-state sync, core privacy/profile operations, newsletters,
  business helpers, and communities.
- The API parity checklist in Section 9 is complete, or each unmet item has a
  documented exclusion or version-specific limitation.
- Key reference tests have Rust equivalents or documented exclusions.
- Binary/protobuf/crypto behavior is covered by golden compatibility fixtures.
- Secrets are redacted and zeroized where practical.
- Network parsers are fuzzed and bounded.
- The workspace passes fmt, clippy, tests, docs, and release checks.
- Examples compile and demonstrate the supported public workflows.
- Any explicitly excluded behavior or version-specific limitation is documented
  in a parity matrix.
